      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1: (torch.compiler_dynamo_deepdive)=\n2: \n3: # Dynamo Deep-Dive\n4: \n5: TorchDynamo (or simply Dynamo) is the tracer within `torch.compile`,\n6: and it is, more often than not, the one to blame for those insane\n7: backtraces. However, we cannot blindly blame Dynamo for these errors. In\n8: order to provide the user with the flexibility it does, Dynamo is given\n9: the arduous task of understanding any Python program. In particular,\n10: Dynamo has to implement a good part of the Python programming language\n11: internally!\n12: ",
            "endLine": 12,
            "startLine": 1
          },
          {
            "content": "409: \n410: we can see the guard that failed by running `TORCH_LOGS=recompiles`\n411: \n412: ",
            "endLine": 412,
            "startLine": 409
          },
          {
            "content": "176: \n177: To summarize: 1. Dynamo is a Python tracer 2. Given some inputs, it\n178: returns an FX graph with the PyTorch functions that were executed 3. It\n179: ",
            "endLine": 179,
            "startLine": 176
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/source/torch.compiler_dynamo_deepdive.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/docs/source/torch.compiler_dynamo_deepdive.md"
      },
      {

{

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\\n4: \\n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\\n9: \\n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\\n13: \\n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\\n19: - Guard insertion and management\\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: import collections\\n23: import contextlib\\n24: import copy\\n25: import functools\\n26: import inspect\\n27: import itertools\\n28: import logging\\n29: import operator\\n30: import re\\n31: import sys\\n32: import traceback\\n33: import warnings\\n34: import weakref\\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\\n37: from types import CodeType\\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\\n40: \\n41: import sympy\\n42: \\n43: import torch._guards\\n44: import torch._logging\\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\\n46: import torch.nn\\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\\n51: from torch._guards import (\\n52:     CompileContext,\\n53:     CompileId,\\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\\n55:     Source,\\n56:     tracing,\\n57:     TracingContext,\\n58: )\\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\\n65:     free_symbols,\\n66:     guard_scalar,\\n67:     is_symbolic,\\n68:     ShapeEnv,\\n69:     Specialization,\\n70: )\\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\\n76: \\n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\\n79: from .bytecode_transformation import (\\n80:     create_call_function,\\n81:     create_instruction,\\n82:     create_load_const,\\n83:     Instruction,\\n84:     unique_id,\\n85: )\\n86: from .code_context import code_context\\n87: from .codegen import PyCodegen\\n88: from .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\\n89: from .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\\n90: from .exc import (\\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\\n92:     exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\\n93:     SkipFrame,\\n94:     unimplemented_v2,\\n95:     unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\\n96: )\\n97: from import is_dynamic_nn_module\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n4: \n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\n9: \n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n13: \n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\n19: - Guard insertion and management\n20: \"\"\"\n21: \n22: import collections\n23: import contextlib\n24: import copy\n25: import functools\n26: import inspect\n27: import itertools\n28: import logging\n29: import operator\n30: import re\n31: import sys\n32: import traceback\n33: import warnings\n34: import weakref\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\n37: from types import CodeType\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n40: \n41: import sympy\n42: \n43: import torch._guards\n44: import torch._logging\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\n46: import torch.nn\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\n51: from torch._guards import (\n52:     CompileContext,\n53:     CompileId,\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n55:     Source,\n56:     tracing,\n57:     TracingContext,\n58: )\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n65:     free_symbols,\n66:     guard_scalar,\n67:     is_symbolic,\n68:     ShapeEnv,\n69:     Specialization,\n70: )\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n76: \n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\n79: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n80:     create_call_function,\n81:     create_instruction,\n82:     create_load_const,\n83:     Instruction,\n84:     unique_id,\n85: )\n86: from .code_context import code_context\n87: from .codegen import PyCodegen\n88: from .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\n89: from .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\n90: from .exc import (\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\n92:     exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\n93:     SkipFrame,\n94:     unimplemented_v2,\n95:     unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\n96: )\n97: from .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\n98: from .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\n99: from .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\n100: from .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\n101: ",

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1550:         cg.restore_stack(stack_values, value_from_source=not tx.export)\\n1551:         self.side_effects.codegen_update_mutated(cg)\\n1552: \\n1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\\n1554:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1555:         Remove \\\"creation_timestamp\\\" from node meta\\n1556: \\n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\\n1560:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\\n1563:         for node in nodes:\\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\\\"creation_timestamp\\\", None)\\n1565: \\n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\\n1568:             if (\\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1571:                 and not node1._erased\\n1572:             ):\\n1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]\\n1574:                 if (\\n1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1577:                     and not node2._erased\\n1578:                 ):\\n1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]\\n1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)\\n1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)\\n1582: \\n1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:\\n1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}\\n1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n1586:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\", None)\\n1587:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\\n1588:                 size = example_value.size()\\n1589:                 ret[node.name] = [s if isinstance(s, int) else repr(s) for s in size]\\n1590:         return ret\\n1591: \\n1592:     def get_graph_sizes(self, name: str) -> str:\\n1593:         graph_sizes_str = \\\"TRACED GRAPH TENSOR SIZES\\\\n\\\"\\n1594:         graph_sizes_str += f\\\"===== {name} =====\\\\n\\\"\\n1595:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n1596:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\", None)\\n1597:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\\n1598:                 size = example_value.size()\\n1599:                 graph_sizes_str += f\\\"{node.name}: {tuple(size)}\\\\n\\\"\\n1600:                 concrete_size = []\\n1601:                 has_symint = False\\n1602:                 for sz in size:\\n1603:                     if isinstance(sz, int):\\n1604:                         concrete_size.append(sz)\\n1605:                     elif isinstance(sz, torch.SymInt):\\n1606:                         has_symint = True\\n1607:                         concrete_size.append(sz.node.hint)\\n1608:                     else:\\n1609:                         break\\n1610:                 else:\\n1611:                     if has_symint:\\n1612:                         graph_sizes_str += (\\n1613:                             f\\\"{node.name} (concrete): {tuple(concrete_size)}\\\\n\\\"\\n1614:                         )\\n1615:         return graph_sizes_str\\n1616: \\n1617:     @contextlib.contextmanager\\n1618:     def restore_global_state(self) -> Any:\\n1619:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1620:         Momentarily restores the global state to what it was prior to tracing the current output\\n1621: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1550-1620\"}",

    "content": "1550:         cg.restore_stack(stack_values, value_from_source=not tx.export)\n1551:         self.side_effects.codegen_update_mutated(cg)\n1552: \n1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\n1554:         \"\"\"\n1555:         Remove \"creation_timestamp\" from node meta\n1556: \n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\n1560:         \"\"\"\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n1563:         for node in nodes:\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\"creation_timestamp\", None)\n1565: \n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\n1568:             if (\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\n1571:                 and not node1._erased\n1572:             ):\n1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]\n1574:                 if (\n1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\n1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\n1577:                     and not node2._erased\n1578:                 ):\n1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]\n1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)\n1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)\n1582: \n1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:\n1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}\n1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n1586:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\", None)\n1587:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\n1588:                 size = example_value.size()\n1589:                 ret[node.name] = [s if isinstance(s, int) else repr(s) for s in size]\n1590:         return ret\n1591: \n1592:     def get_graph_sizes(self, name: str) -> str:\n1593:         graph_sizes_str = \"TRACED GRAPH TENSOR SIZES\\n\"\n1594:         graph_sizes_str += f\"===== {name} =====\\n\"\n1595:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n1596:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\", None)\n1597:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\n1598:                 size = example_value.size()\n1599:                 graph_sizes_str += f\"{node.name}: {tuple(size)}\\n\"\n1600:                 concrete_size = []\n1601:                 has_symint = False\n1602:                 for sz in size:\n1603:                     if isinstance(sz, int):\n1604:                         concrete_size.append(sz)\n1605:                     elif isinstance(sz, torch.SymInt):\n1606:                         has_symint = True\n1607:                         concrete_size.append(sz.node.hint)\n1608:                     else:\n1609:                         break\n1610:                 else:\n1611:                     if has_symint:\n1612:                         graph_sizes_str += (\n1613:                             f\"{node.name} (concrete): {tuple(concrete_size)}\\n\"\n1614:                         )\n1615:         return graph_sizes_str\n1616: \n1617:     @contextlib.contextmanager\n1618:     def restore_global_state(self) -> Any:\n1619:         \"\"\"\n1620:         Momentarily restores the global state to what it was prior to tracing the current output\n1621: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1550-1620"
  }
}

\",\"endLine\":1560,\"startLine\":1557},{\"content\":\"1584: \\n1585:     set_names(list(graph.inputs()), input_names, \\\"input\\\")\\n1586:     set_names(list(graph.outputs()), output_names, \",\"endLine\":1097,\"startLine\":1093}],\"file\":\"torch/onnx/utils.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/onnx/utils.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"570: \\n571: # TODO: The parameter use_output_and_grad_bw is required because some operations\\n572: # that utilize this function, such as the while_loop, may require (grad, fwd_outputs)\\n573: def create_fw_bw_graph(fn, use_output_and_grad_bw, fw_inputs, fw_outputs):\\n574:     from torch._functorch.aot_autograd import AOTConfig, create_joint\\n575: \",\"endLine\":575,\"startLine\":570},{\"content\":\"125:         )\\n126:         gm = _CURRENT_MAKE_FX_TRACER.trace_subgraph(\\n127:             _maybe_run_with_interpreter(fn), *args\\n128: \",\"endLine\":128,\"startLine\":125},{\"content\":\"579:     #\\n580:     # 1. make_fx fails to capture operations on input: the inputs are wrapped as _to_functional_tensor_wrapper,\\n581:     # but they will be unwrapped before entering ProxyTorchDispatchMode as part of the dispatching.\\n582:     # However, it's the outside wrapper that tracer creates proxies for. This casuses tracer fail to\\n583:     # fetch the proxy for the inputs and fail to capture any operations on them.\\n584:     #\\n585:     # 2. make_fx fails to capture output: the outputs after ProxyTorchDispatchMode are further\\n586:     # wrapped as FunctionalTensorWrapper in Functionalize key after return. However, the tracer\\n587:     # only associates the inner tensor with proxy in ProxyTorchDispatchMode. Therefore,\\n588:     # when creating the output node, it fails to associate the wrapped tensor with its proxy.\\n589:     # Instead, it will create _tensor_constant as output.\\n590: \\n591: \",\"endLine\":591,\"startLine\":579}],\"file\":\"torch/_higher_order_ops/utils.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_higher_order_ops/utils.py\"}]}",

            "content": "3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\n3262: ",
            "endLine": 3262,
            "startLine": 3255
          },
          {
            "content": "2368: \n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\n2370:     \"\"\"\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\n2375:     \"\"\"\n2376: ",
            "endLine": 2376,
            "startLine": 2368
          },

            "content": "570: \n571: # TODO: The parameter use_output_and_grad_bw is required because some operations\n572: # that utilize this function, such as the while_loop, may require (grad, fwd_outputs)\n573: def create_fw_bw_graph(fn, use_output_and_grad_bw, fw_inputs, fw_outputs):\n574:     from torch._functorch.aot_autograd import AOTConfig, create_joint\n575: ",

          {
            "content": "125:         )\n126:         gm = _CURRENT_MAKE_FX_TRACER.trace_subgraph(\n127:             _maybe_run_with_interpreter(fn), *args\n128: ",
            "endLine": 128,
            "startLine": 125
          },
          {
            "content": "579:     #\n580:     # 1. make_fx fails to capture operations on input: the inputs are wrapped as _to_functional_tensor_wrapper,\n581:     # but they will be unwrapped before entering ProxyTorchDispatchMode as part of the dispatching.\n582:     # However, it's the outside wrapper that tracer creates proxies for. This casuses tracer fail to\n583:     # fetch the proxy for the inputs and fail to capture any operations on them.\n584:     #\n585:     # 2. make_fx fails to capture output: the outputs after ProxyTorchDispatchMode are further\n586:     # wrapped as FunctionalTensorWrapper in Functionalize key after return. However, the tracer\n587:     # only associates the inner tensor with proxy in ProxyTorchDispatchMode. Therefore,\n588:     # when creating the output node, it fails to associate the wrapped tensor with its proxy.\n589:     # Instead, it will create _tensor_constant as output.\n590: \n591: ",
            "endLine": 591,
            "startLine": 579
          }
        ],
        "file": "torch/_higher_order_ops/utils.py",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_higher_order_ops/utils.py"
      }
    ]
  }
}

type: ignore[has-type]\\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n817:             package=package,\\n818:         )\\n819: \\n820:         try:\\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n823:                 tracer.run()\\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851:         code: CodeType,\\n852:         one_graph: bool,\\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n857:             stack.enter_context(\\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n860:                 )\\n861:             )\\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\\n864: \\n865:         return (\\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\\n867:         )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\\n868: \\n869:     @maybe_cprofile\\n870:     def _compile_inner(\\n871:         code: CodeType,\\n872:         one_graph: bool,\\n873:         hooks: Hooks,\\n874:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n875:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n876:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\\n877:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\\n878: \\n879:         def log_bytecode(\\n880:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\\n881:         ) -> None:\\n882:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n883:                 bytecode_log.debug(\\n884:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\\n885:                 )\\n886: \\n887:         log_bytecode(\\n888:             \\\"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\\\",\\n889:             code.co_name,\\n890:             code.co_filename,\\n891:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n892:             code,\\n893:         )\\n894: \\n895:         out_code = None\\n896:         for attempt in itertools.count():\\n897:             CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\\n898:             try:\\n899:                 with dynamo_timed(\\n900:                     f\\\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL800-900\"}",

then\\n1624:   test_linux_aarch64\\n1625: elif [[ \\\"${TEST_CONFIG}\\\" == *backward* ]]; then\\n1626:   test_forward_backward_compatibility\\n1627:   # Do NOT add tests after bc check tests, see its comment.\\n1628: elif [[ \\\"${TEST_CONFIG}\\\" == *xla* ]]; then\\n1629:   install_torchvision\\n1630: \",\"endLine\":1630,\"startLine\":1621},{\"content\":\"793: \\n794: test_dynamo_benchmark() {\\n795:   # Usage: test_dynamo_benchmark huggingface 0\\n796:   TEST_REPORTS_DIR=$(pwd)/test/test-reports\\n797: \\n798: \",\"endLine\":798,\"startLine\":793}],\"file\":\".ci/pytorch/test.sh\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/.ci/pytorch/test.sh\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3633: \\n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\\n3636:     #\\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\\n3638:     #\\n3639: \",\"endLine\":3639,\"startLine\":3633},{\"content\":\"3676:         allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts: bool = False,\\n3677:         # XXX Add any new settings that could affect FakeTensor evaluation\\n3678:         # to: torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._ShapeEnvSettings\\n3679:         trace_asserts: bool = False,\\n3680: \",\"endLine\":3680,\"startLine\":3676},{\"content\":\"3680:     ) -> None:\\n3681:         if duck_shape is None:\\n3682:             duck_shape = config.use_duck_shape\\n3683: \\n3684:         self.settings = ShapeEnvSettings(\\n3685:             # Not directly used by ShapeEnv; indirectly used by FakeTensor\\n3686:             allow_scalar_outputs=allow_scalar_outputs,\\n3687:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n3688:             # End\\n3689: \",\"endLine\":3689,\"startLine\":3680}],\"file\":\"torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1568: \\n1569: # Run PyTorch tests with TorchDynamo\\n1570: TEST_WITH_TORCHINDUCTOR: bool = TestEnvironment.def_flag(\\n1571:     \\\"TEST_WITH_TORCHINDUCTOR\\\",\\n1572:     env_var=\\\"PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_INDUCTOR\\\",\\n1573: )\\n1574: # AOT_EAGER not tested in ci, useful for debugging\\n1575: TEST_WITH_AOT_EAGER: bool = TestEnvironment.def_flag(\\n1576:     \\\"TEST_WITH_AOT_EAGER\\\",\\n1577:     env_var=\\\"PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_AOT_EAGER\\\",\\n1578: )\\n1579: TEST_WITH_TORCHDYNAMO: bool = TestEnvironment.def_flag(\\n1580:     \\\"TEST_WITH_TORCHDYNAMO\\\",\\n1581:     env_var=\\\"PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO\\\",\\n1582:     implied_by_fn=lambda: TEST_WITH_TORCHINDUCTOR or TEST_WITH_AOT_EAGER,\\n1583: )\\n1584: TEST_WITHOUT_COMPILED_AUTOGRAD: bool = TestEnvironment.def_flag(\\n1585:     \\\"TEST_WITHOUT_COMPILED_AUTOGRAD\\\",\\n1586:     env_var=\\\"PYTORCH_TEST_WITHOUT_COMPILED_AUTOGRAD\\\",\\n1587: )\\n1588: \",\"endLine\":1588,\"startLine\":1568},{\"content\":\"1775: \\n1776: def make_dynamo_test(\\n1777:     fn: Optional[Callable[..., Any]] = None\\n1778: \",\"endLine\":1778,\"startLine\":1775},{\"content\":\"115: # TODO: Expand this class to handle arbitrary settings in addition to boolean flags?\\n116: class TestEnvironment:\\n117:     # Set of env vars to set for the repro command that is output on test failure.\\n118:     # Specifically, this includes env vars that are set to non-default values and\\n119:     # are not implied. Maps from env var name -> value (int)\\n120:     repro_env_vars: dict = {}\\n121: \\n122:     # Defines a flag usable throughout the test suite, determining its value by querying\\n123:     # the specified environment variable.\\n124:     #\\n125: \",\"endLine\":125,\"startLine\":115}],\"file\":\"torch/testing/_internal/common_utils.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/testing/_internal/common_utils.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1054:             context is not None\\n1055:             and context.fake_mode is not None\\n1056:             and context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n1057:         ):\\n1058:             context.fake_mode.shape_env.cleanup()\\n1059:         _TLS.tracing_context = old_context\\n1060: \",\"endLine\":1060,\"startLine\":1054},{\"content\":\"146:     RANDOM_VALUE = 5\\n147:     SHAPE_ENV = 6\\n148:     LOCAL_FSDP_MODULE = 7\\n149: \",\"endLine\":149,\"startLine\":146},{\"content\":\"1123:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1124:     from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor, FakeTensorMode\\n1125: \\n1126:     fake_modes = []\\n1127: \\n1128: \",\"endLine\":1128,\"startLine\":1123}],\"file\":\"torch/_guards.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_guards.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"39: \\n40:     fake_mode: fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode\\n41:     \\\"\\\"\\\"The fake tensor mode used for tracing model using fake tensors and parameters.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n42: \\n43: \",\"endLine\":43,\"startLine\":39},{\"content\":\"96:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n97:     from torch._subclasses import fake_tensor\\n98:     from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import ShapeEnv\\n99: \\n100:     # This overrides the internal `FakeTensorMode` instance created by `torch._dynamo.export`[1].\\n101:     # It is a good idea to keep them in sync (constructor args) to maintain the same default behavior\\n102:     # [1] `torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py::InstructionTranslator::OutputGraph.__init__`\\n103:     # Mixed fake/real tensors are only allowed when `torch.onnx.dynamo_export` is not called within `FakeTensorMode`\\n104:     # This is needed because models can create new parameters during `forward(self, *args, **kwargs)` run\\n105:     fake_mode = fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode(\\n106:         allow_non_fake_inputs=not torch._guards.detect_fake_mode(),\\n107:         shape_env=ShapeEnv(\\n108:             allow_scalar_outputs=False, allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=False\\n109:         ),\\n110: \",\"endLine\":110,\"startLine\":96},{\"content\":\"47: @contextlib.contextmanager\\n48: def enable_fake_mode():\\n49:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Enable fake mode for the duration of the context.\\n50: \\n51:     Internally it instantiates a :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode` context manager\\n52:     that converts user input and model parameters into :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensor`.\\n53: \\n54:     A :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensor`\\n55:     is a :class:`torch.Tensor` with the ability to run PyTorch code without having to\\n56:     actually do computation through tensors allocated on a ``meta`` device. Because\\n57:     there is no actual data being allocated on the device, this API allows for\\n58: \",\"endLine\":58,\"startLine\":47}],\"file\":\"torch/onnx/_internal/_exporter_legacy.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/onnx/_internal/_exporter_legacy.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"604:     # case this is NOT serializable.  That only happens when you're\\n605:     # re-fakeifying a fake tensor with an existing ShapeEnv... maybe we\\n606:     # can get rid of this use case entirely.  Notably, even if we are\\n607:     # fakeifying a real tensor into a fake tensor with symbolic shapes, the\\n608:     # size here is NOT dynamic\\n609: \",\"endLine\":609,\"startLine\":604},{\"content\":\"932:             assert t.stride is not None\\n933:             if shape_env is not None:\\n934:                 fake_mode = t.fake_mode\\n935:                 if fake_mode is not None and fake_mode.shape_env is shape_env:\\n936:                     # Don't reallocate the sizes; the shape envs are the same,\\n937:                     # so reuse the old sizes/strides/etc\\n938: \",\"endLine\":938,\"startLine\":932},{\"content\":\"1770:                         # this is difficult to do because we don't have storage\\n1771:                         # subclasses.  Relevant test is\\n1772:                         # DynamicShapesFunctionTests::test_add_dynamic_shapes in\\n1773:                         # test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py\\n1774:                         maybe_fake_mgr: AbstractContextManager[None] = (\\n1775:                             contextlib.nullcontext()\\n1776: \",\"endLine\":1776,\"startLine\":1770}],\"file\":\"torch/_subclasses/meta_utils.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_subclasses/meta_utils.py\"}]}",

            "endLine": 43,
            "startLine": 39
          },
          {
            "content": "96:     \"\"\"\n97:     from torch._subclasses import fake_tensor\n98:     from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import ShapeEnv\n99: \n100:     # This overrides the internal `FakeTensorMode` instance created by `torch._dynamo.export`[1].\n101:     # It is a good idea to keep them in sync (constructor args) to maintain the same default behavior\n102:     # [1] `torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py::InstructionTranslator::OutputGraph.__init__`\n103:     # Mixed fake/real tensors are only allowed when `torch.onnx.dynamo_export` is not called within `FakeTensorMode`\n104:     # This is needed because models can create new parameters during `forward(self, *args, **kwargs)` run\n105:     fake_mode = fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode(\n106:         allow_non_fake_inputs=not torch._guards.detect_fake_mode(),\n107:         shape_env=ShapeEnv(\n108:             allow_scalar_outputs=False, allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=False\n109:         ),\n110: ",
            "endLine": 110,
            "startLine": 96
          },
          {
            "content": "47: @contextlib.contextmanager\n48: def enable_fake_mode():\n49:     \"\"\"Enable fake mode for the duration of the context.\n50: \n51:     Internally it instantiates a :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode` context manager\n52:     that converts user input and model parameters into :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensor`.\n53: \n54:     A :class:`torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensor`\n55:     is a :class:`torch.Tensor` with the ability to run PyTorch code without having to\n56:     actually do computation through tensors allocated on a ``meta`` device. Because\n57:     there is no actual data being allocated on the device, this API allows for\n58: ",
            "endLine": 58,
            "startLine": 47
          }
        ],

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1040: \\n1041:     Calls to TracingContext.get() while not under a `with tracing()` context\\n1042:     will return None.\\n1043:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1044:     old_context = getattr(_TLS, \\\"tracing_context\\\", None)\\n1045:     _TLS.tracing_context = context\\n1046:     try:\\n1047:         yield context\\n1048:     except Exception as e:\\n1049:         if not hasattr(e, \\\"real_stack\\\") and context is not None:\\n1050:             e.real_stack = context.extract_stack()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1051:         raise\\n1052:     finally:\\n1053:         if (\\n1054:             context is not None\\n1055:             and context.fake_mode is not None\\n1056:             and context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n1057:         ):\\n1058:             context.fake_mode.shape_env.cleanup()\\n1059:         _TLS.tracing_context = old_context\\n1060: \\n1061: \\n1062: # Subclasses can be found in torch/_dynamo/source.py\\n1063: # TODO(voz): Consider a toplevel torch/_source.py\\n1064: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\\n1065: class Source:\\n1066:     def is_dict_key(self) -> bool:\\n1067:         return False\\n1068: \\n1069:     def is_ephemeral(self) -> bool:\\n1070:         return False\\n1071: \\n1072:     def reconstruct(self, codegen: PyCodegen) -> None:\\n1073:         raise NotImplementedError\\n1074: \\n1075:     def guard_source(self) -> GuardSource:\\n1076:         raise NotImplementedError\\n1077: \\n1078:     def name(self) -> str:\\n1079:         raise NotImplementedError\\n1080: \\n1081: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_guards.py%3FL1040-1080\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1040: \n1041:     Calls to TracingContext.get() while not under a `with tracing()` context\n1042:     will return None.\n1043:     \"\"\"\n1044:     old_context = getattr(_TLS, \"tracing_context\", None)\n1045:     _TLS.tracing_context = context\n1046:     try:\n1047:         yield context\n1048:     except Exception as e:\n1049:         if not hasattr(e, \"real_stack\") and context is not None:\n1050:             e.real_stack = context.extract_stack()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1051:         raise\n1052:     finally:\n1053:         if (\n1054:             context is not None\n1055:             and context.fake_mode is not None\n1056:             and context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n1057:         ):\n1058:             context.fake_mode.shape_env.cleanup()\n1059:         _TLS.tracing_context = old_context\n1060: \n1061: \n1062: # Subclasses can be found in torch/_dynamo/source.py\n1063: # TODO(voz): Consider a toplevel torch/_source.py\n1064: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n1065: class Source:\n1066:     def is_dict_key(self) -> bool:\n1067:         return False\n1068: \n1069:     def is_ephemeral(self) -> bool:\n1070:         return False\n1071: \n1072:     def reconstruct(self, codegen: PyCodegen) -> None:\n1073:         raise NotImplementedError\n1074: \n1075:     def guard_source(self) -> GuardSource:\n1076:         raise NotImplementedError\n1077: \n1078:     def name(self) -> str:\n1079:         raise NotImplementedError\n1080: \n1081: ",

that affects the\\n79: # issued guards, when ShapeEnv.produce_guards is called.\\n80: @dataclass\\n81: class ShapeEnvEvent:\\n82:     # ShapeEnv method.\\n83:     f: Callable\\n84: \",\"endLine\":84,\"startLine\":75},{\"content\":\"30: #\\n31: # What is a ShapeEnv event?\\n32: # -------------------------\\n33: # We consider a ShapeEnv event every function call (ShapeEnv method or\\n34: # independent function) that modifies the state of the ShapeEnv instance.\\n35: # Such calls are recorded alongside their positional and keyword arguments,\\n36: # so that it may be replayed over a different ShapeEnv instance.\\n37: #\\n38: # See [Note: ShapeEnv State Equality] for what is considered the state\\n39: # of a ShapeEnv instance.\\n40: #\\n41: \",\"endLine\":41,\"startLine\":30},{\"content\":\"238:         assert args and args[0] == \\\"self\\\", (\\n239:             \\\"record_shapeenv_event should only wrap methods on ShapeEnv; refactor   "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "75: \n76: # Event class for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary time.\n77: #\n78: # Represents a method call that mutates ShapeEnv in a way that affects the\n79: # issued guards, when ShapeEnv.produce_guards is called.\n80: @dataclass\n81: class ShapeEnvEvent:\n82:     # ShapeEnv method.\n83:     f: Callable\n84: ",
            "endLine": 84,
            "startLine": 75
          },
          {
            "content": "30: #\n31: # What is a ShapeEnv event?\n32: # -------------------------\n33: # We consider a ShapeEnv event every function call (ShapeEnv method or\n34: # independent function) that modifies the state of the ShapeEnv instance.\n35: # Such calls are recorded alongside their positional and keyword arguments,\n36: # so that it may be replayed over a different ShapeEnv instance.\n37: #\n38: # See [Note: ShapeEnv State Equality] for what is considered the state\n39: # of a ShapeEnv instance.\n40: #\n41: ",
            "endLine": 41,
            "startLine": 30
          },
          {
            "content": "238:         assert args and args[0] == \"self\", (\n239:             \"record_shapeenv_event should only wrap methods on ShapeEnv; refactor your \"\n240:             \"code so that it calls into a method on ShapeEnv\"\n241:         )\n242: ",
            "endLine": 242,
            "startLine": 238
          }
        ],
        "file": "torch/fx/experimental/recording.py",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/recording.py"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "513: \n514: void runCleanupPasses(Module* m) {\n515:   auto methods = m->get_methods();\n516:   for (auto module : m->children()) {\n517:     runCleanupPasses(&module);\n518:   }\n519:   for (auto& method : methods) {\n520:     runCleanupPasses(method.graph());\n521:   }\n522: ",
            "endLine": 522,
            "startLine": 513
          },
          {
            "content": "381: \n382: // Find a unique name to add this method as\n383: // We try {method_name}, {method_name}1, {method_name}2, ...\n384: std::string mangleMethodName(\n385:     const std::string& method_name,\n386:     const ClassTypePtr& mod_type) {\n387:   for (size_t method_idx = 0;; method_idx++) {\n388:     auto mangled = method_name;\n389:     if (method_idx != 0) {\n390:       mangled += std::to_string(method_idx);\n391:     }\n392: ",
            "endLine": 392,
            "startLine": 381
          },
          {
            "content": "491: // Run a few clean-up passes to make the graph a bit cleaner.\n492: void runCleanupPasses(const std::shared_ptr<Graph>& g) {\n493:   for (Node* n : g->nodes()) {\n494: ",
            "endLine": 494,
            "startLine": 491
          }
        ],

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3550: def _suppress_guards(shape_env: ShapeEnv) -> Iterator[None]:\\n3551:     shape_env._suppress_guards_enter()\\n3552:     try:\\n3553:         yield\\n3554:     finally:\\n3555:         shape_env._suppress_guards_exit()\\n3556: \\n3557: \\n3558: @dataclass\\n3559: class _FrameLocalResult:\\n3560:     loc: Optional[str] = None\\n3561:     locals: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n3562:     symbols: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n3563: \\n3564: \\n3565: class ShapeEnv:\\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\\n3567:     #\\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\\n3569:     # =====================================================\\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\\n3573:     #\\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\\n3576:     def __init__(\\n3577:         self,\\n3578:         *,\\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\\n3582:     ) -> None:\\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\\n3584: \\n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\\n3586:         kwargs[\\\"should_record_events\\\"] = False\\n3587: \\n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\\n3589: \\n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\\n3591: \\n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\\n3596:             should_record_events\\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\\n3598:             else (\\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\\n3601:             )\\n3602:         )\\n3603: \\n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\\n3605:         #   - It should record events\\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\\n3609:         )\\n3610: \\n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\\n3619:             else []\\n3620:         )\\n3621: \\n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\\n3625:         #\\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\\n3628:         # with the GC.\\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\\n3632:         ] = {}\\n3633: \\n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\\n3636:     #\\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\\n3638:     #\\n3639:     def _init(\\n3640:         self,\\n3641:         *,\\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\\n3650:         #\\n3651: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL3550-3650\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3550: def _suppress_guards(shape_env: ShapeEnv) -> Iterator[None]:\n3551:     shape_env._suppress_guards_enter()\n3552:     try:\n3553:         yield\n3554:     finally:\n3555:         shape_env._suppress_guards_exit()\n3556: \n3557: \n3558: @dataclass\n3559: class _FrameLocalResult:\n3560:     loc: Optional[str] = None\n3561:     locals: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n3562:     symbols: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)\n3563: \n3564: \n3565: class ShapeEnv:\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\n3567:     #\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\n3569:     # =====================================================\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\n3573:     #\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\n3576:     def __init__(\n3577:         self,\n3578:         *,\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\n3582:     ) -> None:\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\n3584: \n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\n3586:         kwargs[\"should_record_events\"] = False\n3587: \n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\n3589: \n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\n3591: \n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\n3596:             should_record_events\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\n3598:             else (\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\n3601:             )\n3602:         )\n3603: \n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\n3605:         #   - It should record events\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\n3609:         )\n3610: \n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\n3619:             else []\n3620:         )\n3621: \n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\n3625:         #\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\n3628:         # with the GC.\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\n3632:         ] = {}\n3633: \n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\n3636:     #\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\n3638:     #\n3639:     def _init(\n3640:         self,\n3641:         *,\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\n3650:         #\n3651: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL3550-3650"
  }

torch._dynamo.testing import same\\n8: \\n9: \\n10: try:\\n11:     from diffusers.models import unet_2d\\n12: except ImportError:\\n13:     unet_2d = None\\n14: \\n15: \\n16: def maybe_skip(fn):\\n17:     if unet_2d is None:\\n18:         return unittest.skip(\\\"requires diffusers\\\")(fn)\\n19:     return fn\\n20: \\n21: \\n22: class TestBaseOutput(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\\n23: torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn, nargs=1, expected_ops=1)\\n30: \\n31:     @maybe_skip\\n32:     def test_assign(self):\\n33:         def fn(a):\\n34:             tmp = unet_2d.UNet2DOutput(a + 1)\\n35:             tmp.sample = a + 2\\n36:             return tmp\\n37: \\n38:         args = [torch.randn(10)]\\n39:         obj1 = fn(*args)\\n40: \\n41:         cnts = obj2.sample))\\n45:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\\n46:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\\n47: \\n48:     def _common(self, fn, op_count):\\n49:         args = [\\n50: unet_2d.UNet2DOutput):\\n65:             x = obj.sample * 10\\n66:             return x\\n67: \\n68:         self._common(fn, 1)\\n69: \\n70:     @maybe_skip\\n71:     def test_getitem(self):\\n72:     "content": "1: # Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n2: import unittest.mock\n3: \n4: import torch\n5: import torch._dynamo.test_case\n6: import torch._dynamo.testing\n7: from torch._dynamo.testing import same\n8: \n9: \n10: try:\n11:     from diffusers.models import unet_2d\n12: except ImportError:\n13:     unet_2d = None\n14: \n15: \n16: def maybe_skip(fn):\n17:     if unet_2d is None:\n18:         return unittest.skip(\"requires diffusers\")(fn)\n19:     return fn\n20: \n21: \n22: class TestBaseOutput(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n23:     @maybe_skip\n24:     def test_create(self):\n25:         def fn(a):\n26:             tmp = unet_2d.UNet2DOutput(a + 1)\n27:             return tmp\n28: \n29:         torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn, nargs=1, expected_ops=1)\n30: \n31:     @maybe_skip\n32:     def test_assign(self):\n33:         def fn(a):\n34:             tmp = unet_2d.UNet2DOutput(a + 1)\n35:             tmp.sample = a + 2\n36: torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(fn)\n43:         obj2 = opt_fn(*args)\n44:         self.assertTrue(same(obj1.sample, obj2.sample))\n45:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n46:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\n47: \n48:     def _common(self, fn, op_count):\n49:         args = [\n50:             unet_2d.UNet2DOutput(\n51:                 sample=torch.randn(10),\n52:             )\n53:         ]\n54:         obj1 = fn(*args)\n55:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n56:         opt_fn = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(fn)\n57:         obj2 = opt_fn(*args)\n58:         self.assertTrue(same(obj1, obj2))\n59:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n60:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, op_count)\n61: \n62:     @maybe_skip\n63: Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Sherlock Huang <bahuang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"99bc2f9\",\"date\":\"2025-08-11\",\"message\":\"Update export/schema.py (#160220)\\n\\nSummary:\\nModel could have multiple ExportedPrograms\\n- for different methods. They can have different weights.\\n- for different delegates. They can also have different weights.\\n\\nFor this reason, we make weight per ExportedProgram.\\n\\nAlso, we cleanup Model, and Program. IIUC, Model and Program are not used anywhere, so it's ok to make BC breaking change.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nCI\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D79917395\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160220\\nApproved by: https://github.com/angelayi, https://github.com/dolpm, https://... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Update export/schema.py (#160220)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/99bc2f94c1955657e950ebdad5f77e518785ccbd\"},{\"author\":\"Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>\",\"commit\":\"1128f4c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-08\",\"message\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA] cuDNN SDPA refactor/cleanup, nested tensor backward, test priority bump for `sm90`, `sm100` (#149282)\\n\\ncleanup tuple/tensor boilerplate in cuDNN SDPA, preparation for nested/ragged tensor backward\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149282\\nApproved by: https://github.com/drisspg\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA] cuDNN SDPA refactor/cleanup, nested tensor backward, test priority bump for `sm90`, `sm100` (#149282)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/1128f4c2a822cbe34a9d966306af15097179ffe1\"},{\"author\":\"Robert Hardwick <robert.hardwick@arm.com>\",\"commit\":\"334ecbd\",\"date\":\"2025-08-08\",\"message\":\"Add torchao to install_inductor_benchmark_deps cleanup stage (#160191)\\n\\nIt looks like `torcho` was missed from the cleanup during torchbench setup.\\n\\nFixes #160188\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160191\\nApproved by: https://github.com/huydhn\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Add torchao to install_inductor_benchmark_deps cleanup stage (#160191)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/334ecbd4ffe11858cae7d23d1190ddb4777c2513\"},{\"author\":\"codingwithsurya <suryafromcali@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"ea7fe0e\",\"date\":\"2025-08-06\",\"message\":\"[SymmMem]  Standardize NVSHMEM Triton wrappers on byte-based APIs + improve code clarity (#159136)\\n\\nQuick refactor for consistency and clarity.\\n\\n1. We now standardize all NVSHMEM data-moving collectives (put, get, alltoall, broadcast) to use their byte-based *_mem_block variants. This makes the API behavior more predictable and avoids mixing paradigms.\\n\\n2. Previously, some functions operated on element counts (nelems), while others expected byte sizes but still used `nelems` as the param name. That inconsistency was easy to miss and could lead to bugs, especially for devs not familiar with the... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[SymmMem]  Standardize NVSHMEM Triton wrappers on byte-based APIs + improve code clarity (#159136)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/ea7fe0ecf62b44185181fba8263cfb6cbf58fa09\"},{\"author\":\"Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"ba4ccf5\",\"date\":\"2025-08-08\",\"message\":\"turn on executon frame clenaup by default (#160110)\\n\\nSummary: Turning execution frame cleanup back on since D78621408 is done\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nSee D78621408\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D79730674\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160110\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jingsh\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"turn on executon frame clenaup by default (#160110)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/ba4ccf5d67e3d237f435eacc2bce3c6025f08491\"},{\"author\":\"Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"c669b0a\",\"date\":\"2025-08-06\",\"message\":\"Fix execution frame cleanup logic (#158717)\\n\\nSummary: This fixes a bug in the execution fram cleanup logic - previously, whenever we hit the time interval to clear out the frames, we were removing any cached execution frames beyond the configured minimum number (frameEntry.used was unused). Instead, we only want to clear frames that were NOT USED in during the last time interval. This diff refactors the executor to have the correct logic.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\n```\\nbuck2 test 'mode/dev-nosan' fbcode//sigmoid/inference/test_gpu:model_runner_test -- ModelRunnerTest.Basic_InterpreterCuda_Multithread_Cleanup... We do need to keep some old global book keeping for bc. The rest of logic is now all in cpp. Regarding the change brought in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152175, we did clean up in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158790 (including internal changes) so that we can safely remove it.\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D78777152](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D7... `test/mobile/model_test/gen_test_model.py`.\\n\\n### Changes:\\n- Corrected \\\"occurances\\\" to the correct spelling \\\"occurrences\\\"\\n- Renamed associated variables to reflect this change for consistency and clarity\\n\\nThis is a non-functional, cleanup-only PR to improve code readability.\\n\\nThanks to the PyTorch team for maintaining such a high-quality codebase\\n\\nPull Request (#157629)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/e0fd48be7d526625cacfd093f3db13506ca9cc17\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"7b7eafe\",\"date\":\"2025-06-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager https://github.com/wconstab, https://github.com/kwen2501, https://github.com/Skylion007\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[PG/nccl] improvements to eager init <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"2c372a0\",\"date\":\"2025-06-19\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/2c372a0502578e0136a84423c3f49c19c26d6bb7\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"920f6e6\",\"date\":\"2025-06-17\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/920f6e681ec70b664ed952255b8c1f97962f5de0\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"672ac2e\",\"date\":\"2025-06-10\",\"message\":\"Reapply \\\"Cleanup VS 2019 refs in pytorch (#145863)\\\" (#152613) (#155478)\\n\\nThis reverts commit e4f2282.\\nI believe fix PR was landed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153480 that triggered the revert.\\nHence this is reland.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/155478\\nApproved by: https://github.com/malfet\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Reapply \\\"Cleanup VS 2019 refs in pytorch (#145863)\\\" (#152613) (#155478)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/672ac2ec86d8ffe080399363df96597437e51115\"},{\"author\":\"Brian Hirsh <hirsheybar@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"6c05f2f\",\"date\":\"2025-06-09\",\"message\":\"[test] use JK to force graph break on slow aliasing/mutation/dynamic_shape behavior (#155257)\\n\\nSummary: test to unblock shampoo, needs cleanup\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nCI\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\nsteps:\\n  - jk.update:\\n      jk: pytorch/compiler:aliased_inputs_with_mutation_and_dyn_shapes_killswitch\\n      constant_bool: null\\n      consistent_pass_rate: null\\n      fractional_host_rollout: null\\n      sampling_rate: null\\n  - manual.note:\\n      content: Set it to false.\\n\\nReviewed By: c00w\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D76051868\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/155257\\nApproved by: https://github.... <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"85fb13d\",\"date\":\"2025-06-03\",\"message\":\"[BE] Cleanup cuda 12.4 artifacts from scripts and workflows (#154893)\\n\\nRemove artifacts. CUDA 12.4 was deprecated. hence no need to keep this code around\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154893\\nApproved by: https://github.com/nWEIdia, https://github.com/malfet, https://github.com/tinglvv\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[BE] Cleanup cuda 12.4 artifacts from scripts and workflows (#154893)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/85fb13d0d1078717fb09ff0a51fabb4b7b1b7707\"},{\"author\":\"Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"af9f18e\",\"date\":\"2025-06-02\",\"message\":\"[nativert] Free stale execution frames (#154636)\\n\\nSummary:\\nThis was implemented in SR due to caching of runtime instances building up and causing some memory usage spikes after some large amount traffic went through the model, and then once traffic went down, SR was still caching all the previous usage.\\n\\nWe need something similar on the Sigmoid side to make sure the static dispatch modules aren't hogging memory. Currently, all ExecutionFrame objects are being cached, and never freed if stale.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nAdded extra execution frames in tmp commit D75257998 and ran local replayer test to confir... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[nativert] Free stale execution frames (#154636)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/af9f18e87ed8d60f0235560a4bb1395008263ef8\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"6706751\",\"date\":\"2025-06-02\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[BE] Cleanup old ExecuTorch codegen and runtime code (#154165)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 515c19a3856e953c0fe23a0ed4fa844f8eea34d8.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154165 on behalf of https://github.com/seemethere due to This is failing when attempting to test against executorch main internally, author has acknowledged that this should be reverted ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154165#issuecomment-2931489616))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[BE] Cleanup old ExecuTorch codegen and runtime code (#154165)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/67067512a19070b80b5d420088595bdf9b0542e6\"},{\"author\":\"Mengwei Liu <larryliu@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"515c19a\",\"date\":\"2025-05-28\",\"message\":\"[BE] Cleanup old ExecuTorch codegen and runtime code (#154165)\\n\\nSummary: These files are added to pytorch/pytorch before ExecuTorch is\\nopensourced. Now is a good time to remove it from pytorch/pytorch, since\\nthe code is moved to pytorch/executorch already.\\n\\nTest Plan: Rely on CI jobs.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154165\\nApproved by: https://github.com/kimishpatel, https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/cyyever\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[BE] Cleanup old ExecuTorch codegen and runtime code (#154165)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/515c19a3856e953c0fe23a0ed4fa844f8eea34d8\"},{\"author\":\"Joaquin <joaquinmartinezfranco@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"cb56df5\",\"date\":\"2025-05-29\",\"message\":\"[Inductor]Cleanup autotune_fallback_to_aten post-deprecation (#154331)\\n\\nFixes #153298\\n\\nThis PR is the 3rd and final step of #147479\\nAll references to autotune_fallback_to_aten have been removed, and the feature is now deprecated.\\nAll calls to should_fallback_to_aten() were also removed, as they were deemed unnecessary.\\n\\n[henrylhtsang](https://github.com/henrylhtsang)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154331\\nApproved by: https://github.com/henrylhtsang\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[Inductor]Cleanup autotune_fallback_to_aten post-deprecation (#154331)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/cb56df55dc4d4e8846ed9711fa51654b71743798\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"3afbab6\",\"date\":\"2025-05-29\",\"message\":\"[BE] Remove unused release scripts. Add clarifications for the branch cut process (#154649)\\n\\nScripts in ``scripts/release/promote/`` are not used for a while.\\nWe use the ones in test-infra [here](https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/blob/main/release/) .\\nHence this small cleanup.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154649\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/huydhn\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[BE] Remove unused release scripts. <henrylhtsang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"b910d37\",\"date\":\"2025-05-19\",\"message\":\"[cutlass backend] Reduce log level for cutlass runtime error (#153457)\\n\\nWant to make sure we always call self.cleanup_run_fn() even if we crash.\\n\\nI think this is the reason why sometimes we get\\n```\\nin _dlclose\\nTypeError: 'NoneType' object is not callable\\n```\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D74629230](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D74629230/)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153457\\nApproved by: refactoring\\n1) do not use propagate_real_tensors to resolve eval under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\\n2) do not guard for dimensions of type  DimDynamic.OBLIVIOUS_SIZE under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152657\\nApproved by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/123649\\nCleanup old Docker files and `manylinuxaarch64-builder:cpu-aarch64` image which has been replaced by `manylinux2_28_aarch64-builder:cpu-aarch64`\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152428\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, \\\"spike\\\" observed  Improves on #151474 by removing unnecessary stride calculations and unused arguments to the helper function\\n\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/151351\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/151351\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/151794\\nApproved by: https://github.com/albanD,
