<nikita.shulga@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"14293c2\",\"date\":\"2025-04-18\",\"message\":\"[MPS] Allow isin for mixed types (#151600)\\n\\nTo follow pattern set by CPU and CUDA impls: define common_dtype and optionally casts `elements` and `test_elements` to common dtype if needed\\n\\n- Add regression test, though skip it on MacOS-13, as `isin` seems to produce garbage there even for same dtypes\\n```\\n>>> import torch\\n>>> x=torch.arange(4.0, device='mps')\\n>>> y=torch.arange(1.0, 3.0, device='mps')\\n>>> x, y, torch.isin(x, y), torch.isin(y, x)\\n(tensor([0., 1., 2., 3.], device='mps:0'), tensor([1., 2.], device='mps:0'), tensor([False,  True, False, False], device='mps:0'), tensor([False, False]... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[MPS] Allow isin for mixed types (#151600)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/14293c237729a99440470206c0f791a8e76224ec\"},{\"author\":\"Blaine Burton Rister <145300525+blaine-rister@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"c0a0761\",\"date\":\"2025-04-15\",\"message\":\"[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\\n\\nPreparatory refactor for https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146942.\\n\\n# Feature\\n\\nThis PR refactors the existing wrapper codegen into `WrapperLine` subclasses, extending the existing Memory Planning IR into a fully-fledged Wrapper IR. See the diagram below.\\n\\n![wrapper_ir](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a61db21b-caf3-45d2-bfdb-91066ae4ba6b)\\n\\nThe IR currently supports the following ops:\\n- All existing memory planning IR ops (`AllocateLine`, `FreeIfNotReusedLine`, etc.)\\n- Reinterpret views (`ReinterpretLine`)\\n- Kerne... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/c0a07618712fe25f44e8f700a23c5c977fa70063\"},{\"author\":\"Blaine Burton Rister <145300525+blaine-rister@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"fe7f425\",\"date\":\"2025-04-12\",\"message\":\"[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\\n\\nPreparatory refactor for https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146942.\\n\\n# Feature\\n\\nThis PR refactors the existing wrapper codegen into `WrapperLine` subclasses, extending the existing Memory Planning IR into a fully-fledged Wrapper IR. See the diagram below.\\n\\n![wrapper_ir](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a61db21b-caf3-45d2-bfdb-91066ae4ba6b)\\n\\nThe IR currently supports the following ops:\\n- All existing memory planning IR ops (`AllocateLine`, `FreeIfNotReusedLine`, etc.)\\n- Reinterpret views (`ReinterpretLine`)\\n- Kerne... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/fe7f425de7b76ef33d308d0a03779b97a914d186\"},{\"author\":\"Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>\",\"commit\":\"bb60e82\",\"date\":\"2025-04-11\",\"message\":\"c10d/Store: add queues (#150969)\\n\\nThis adds queue operations as described in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/150943.\\n\\nThis works by adding two new operations `queue_push` and `queue_pop`. The semantics are designed to be blocking with a timeout. Pushing will always succeed as the queue is infinite size. Popping will first call `wait` until the key is ready and then pop the value from the queue.\\n\\nThis implements queues for only: HashStore, TCPStore w/ libuv. FileStore and the legacy backends are not supported.\\n\\n`wait` and `check` work for queue operations though queue_push will only w... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"c10d/Store: add queues (#150969)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/bb60e8267204b46f607f24291918639fa4fb5ab6\"},{\"author\":\"Burak Turk <burakturk@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"38bec78\",\"date\":\"2025-04-11\",\"message\":\"cleanup JK for duplicate pt2 compile callbacks prevention (#148704)\\n\\nSummary: This diff cleans up the JK we used for enabling `add pt2 callbacks for backward pass and prevent duplicate callbacks` feature.\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D70643543\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/148704\\nApproved by: https://github.com/mlazos\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"cleanup JK for duplicate pt2 compile callbacks prevention (#148704)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/38bec787fa663b3cda18c945b2873b9478215804\"},{\"author\":\"shubhambhokare1 <shubhambhokare@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"1a56609\",\"date\":\"2025-04-09\",\"message\":\"[ONNX] Supporting different opset versions for torchlib registry (#149901)\\n\\n- Allows opset_version to determine which onnx decomposition to choose\\n- Adds a cleanup function to modify the registry after it is built\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149901\\nApproved by: https://github.com/justinchuby, https://github.com/titaiwangms\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[ONNX] Supporting different opset versions for torchlib registry (#149901)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/1a56609e75a31f92c55f5f74c7af92b6f1531aa5\"},{\"author\":\"Arash Pakbin <arash.pakbin@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"e6e07ec\",\"date\":\"2025-04-03\",\"message\":\"[ROCm] code cleanup of architecture checks (#150473)\\n\\nThis PR replaces several calls to `at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties()->gcnArchName` and `at::cuda::getDeviceProperties(device_index)->gcnArchName` when checking to see if the GPU architecture is in a certain list.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/150473\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily, https://github.com/cyyever\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[ROCm] code cleanup of architecture checks (#150473)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/e6e07ec1cf0b2770c24a12f57b4654fd7686c541\"},{\"author\":\"Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"45b1173\",\"date\":\"2025-03-26\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][TunableOp] TunableOp Context Manager for unit tests (#149930)\\n\\nThis PR is cleanup only. There are no feature changes or bug fixes.\\n\\nWe create a TunableOp context manager for setting up and cleanup. We re-write TunableOp unit tests in terms of this context manager. Ultimately reduces the amount of copy-paste code.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149930\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][TunableOp] TunableOp Context Manager for unit tests (#149930)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/45b11730f10f64171a9861c98782e1875bad87c9\"},{\"author\":\"eellison <elias.ellison@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"5757aa8\",\"date\":\"2025-03-21\",\"message\":\"Cudagraph fix + comment cleanup (#149741)\\n\\nCudagraphs is careful to not allow any memory recorded to escape globally without having a reference to the tensor. This is because we may later reclaim that memory for a cudagraph recording and we need to mark the tensor as erroring on access. Very occasionally, a stray tensor will have been allocated locally but not yet cleaned up. In this case, we enter the slow path and try to gc.collect() to deallocate it. From a hard to repro internal use case, this was fixed by an additional `cuda.synchronize()`.\\n\\ni also snuck in an outdated comment and a dupli... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Cudagraph fix + comment cleanup (#149741)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/5757aa87736bbc4d95f6219508325029c07e9749\"},{\"author\":\"Michael Lazos <mlazos@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"3474367\",\"date\":\"2025-03-21\",\"message\":\"[Dynamo] Cleanup state management for ctx managers (#149689)\\n\\nRemoves state indirection for ctx managers. This isn't needed anymore since VTs are mutable.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149689\\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[Dynamo] Cleanup state management for ctx managers (#149689)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/34743678b93fd79f76c3bce66f69b5ba585388c7\"},{\"author\":\"Matthias Braun <matthiasb@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"66dd00f\",\"date\":\"2025-03-21\",\"message\":\"Fix clang-tidy errors (#149581)\\n\\nSummary: Cleanup clang-tidy complaints in `EmbeddingBag.cpp`: Avoid shadowed variables and unused parameters.\\n\\nTest Plan: sandcastle\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D71512594\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149581\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/malfet\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Fix clang-tidy errors (#149581)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/66dd00fca0206924ff79ffcb35f0467d6ed678c2\"},{\"author\":\"Andrey Talman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"c9de76a\",\"date\":\"2025-03-19\",\"message\":\"Modify cuda aarch64 install for cudnn and nccl. Cleanup aarch64 cuda 12.6 docker (#149540)\\n\\n1. Use NCCL_VERSION=v2.26.2-1 . Fixes nccl cuda aarch64 related failure we see here: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/13955856471/job/39066681549?pr=149443 . After landing: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149351\\nTODO: Followup required to unify NCCL definitions across the x86 and aarch64 builds\\n\\n3. Cleanup Remove older CUDA versions for aarch64 builds . CUDA 12.6 where removed by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/148895\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pyto... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Modify cuda aarch64 install for cudnn and nccl. Cleanup aarch64 cuda 12.6 docker (#149540)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/c9de76a1e45e24e45dc00285859457092b7b2a0c\"},{\"author\":\"Aleksei Nikiforov <aleksei.nikiforov@linux.ibm.com>\",\"commit\":\"cfbeaf7\",\"date\":\"2025-03-19\",\"message\":\"Improve docker build cleanup on s390x runners (#149316)\\n\\nCurrently it sometimes still leaves a couple of processess running.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149316\\nApproved by: https://github.com/seemethere\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Improve docker build cleanup on s390x runners (#149316)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/cfbeaf7b7ed69aa239ca62fd0bc8da50ca1bc24f\"},{\"author\":\"Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>\",\"commit\":\"7ffadff\",\"date\":\"2025-03-08\",\"message\":\"c10d/ProcessGroup: cleanup abort and shutdown (#148798)\\n\\nThis adds `abort` and `shutdown` to `Backend` and `ProcessGroup` objects. This simplifies the logic in `distributed_c10d.py` by having a default noop implementation for all PGs.\\n\\nThis will be useful for torchft and upcoming versions of NCCL which will handle abort correctly. Currently `torchft` would have to call internal methods `_abort` on the PGNCCL object directly but with this change we can now just call `.abort()` and have it work for any PG implementation.\\n\\nTest plan:\\n\\n```\\npytest distributed/test_backends.py distributed/test_c10d_... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"c10d/ProcessGroup: cleanup abort and shutdown (#148798)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/7ffadff286837168be12b9cd43dbf4968cd54e65\"},{\"author\":\"Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>\",\"commit\":\"93e9dae\",\"date\":\"2025-03-04\",\"message\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\\n\\nDisabled by default for now behind `TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED=1`\\n\\nJust wanted to get this out before starting a series of SDPA cleanup PRs---the biggest thing is we don't need the boilerplate around all of the `build_graph_and_tensors*` functions anymore as we can now use the `UID`-style referencing of tensor nodes as was done for the Conv-V8 API backend.\\n\\nCC @drisspg\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/141178\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jbschlosser\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/93e9daed54249e3645fb1b1fe2012630b154a947\"},{\"author\":\"Arthur Laureus Wigo <126365160+arthurlw@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"9b7130b\",\"date\":\"2025-02-28\",\"message\":\"Clean temporary directory at exit (#147813)\\n\\nIssue: A temporary directory is created in [pytorch/torch/distributed/nn/jit/instantiator.py](https://github.com/arthurlw/pytorch/blob/clean-temp-directory-at-exit/torch/distributed/nn/jit/instantiator.py) but is never cleaned up, leading to a ResourceWarning on program exit.\\n\\nSolution: Registered an `atexit` handler to properly clean up the temporary directory when the program exits.\\n\\nFixes #147744\\n\\n**Line 23 in [0a49f8f](https://github.com/arthurlw/pytorch/commit/0a49f8fd3d34ee31f39bf7029ebb0b564433ac48)**\\n```python\\n23  atexit.register(_TEMP_DIR.c... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"Clean temporary directory at exit (#147813)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/9b7130b8db62e9e550366419fa33c0f530d80beb\"},{\"author\":\"Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"b13ad1a\",\"date\":\"2025-02-26\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][TunableOp] Remove extra transpose characters in hipBLASLt signature. (#147900)\\n\\nCleanup the TunableOp hipBLASLt signature of extra transpose characters.\\n\\nTest manually and no new regressions found.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147900\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][TunableOp] Remove extra transpose characters in hipBLASLt signature. (#147900)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/b13ad1a193f35817ef43c74cc76ba06f7a9c353c\"},{\"author\":\"Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"fd8ae1a\",\"date\":\"2025-02-21\",\"message\":\"[ROCm] gfx940 and gfx941 cleanup (#147394)\\n\\nRemoving gfx architectures not supported by ROCm.\\n\\nNOTE: For users wanting to build PyTorch for gfx archs that are *not* supported by the official wheels on download.pytorch.org, you can build PyTorch from source for your desired gfx arch [using the PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH env var](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/README.md#amd-rocm-support).\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147394\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jithunnair-amd, https://github.com/jeffdaily\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[ROCm] gfx940 and gfx941 cleanup (#147394)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/fd8ae1aa047b38b83325cc7b8ed8774f9f11008f\"},{\"author\":\"Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>\",\"commit\":\"533b884\",\"date\":\"2025-02-21\",\"message\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\\n\\nDisabled by default for now behind `TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED=1`\\n\\nJust wanted to get this out before starting a series of SDPA cleanup PRs---the biggest thing is we don't need the boilerplate around all of the `build_graph_and_tensors*` functions anymore as we can now use the `UID`-style referencing of tensor nodes as was done for the Conv-V8 API backend.\\n\\nCC @drisspg\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/141178\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jbschlosser\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/533b884870acd951e684e0bf551eb76904dec047\"},{\"author\":\"vasiliy <vasiliy@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"382fbcc\",\"date\":\"2025-02-20\",\"message\":\"add the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch (#147466)\\n\\nSummary:\\n\\nContinuing the work from https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146427\\n\\nAdds the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch, as detailed in\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/146414 . Please see the issue for a detailed definition of the format.  Example of basic functionality:\\n\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\n# round trip\\nx0 = torch.randn(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32)\\nx1 = x0.to(torch.float8_e8m0fnu)  # RNE rounding\\nx2 = x1.to(torch.float32)  # 2 ** exponent\\n\\n# creation with empty\\nx0 = torch.empty(4, 4, dtype=torch.float8_e8m0fnu)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"add the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch (#147466)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/382fbcc1e43ae5d46ec148bdfd8dcfb73da81b77\"},{\"author\":\"Aleksei Nikiforov <aleksei.nikiforov@linux.ibm.com>\",\"commit\":\"a00d2b5\",\"date\":\"2025-02-18\",\"message\":\"s390x: add cleanup for cancelled docker image builds (#147110)\\n\\nWhen podman image build is cancelled,\\na couple of processes are left behind,\\nand their existence prevents\\nproper shutdown of runner container.\\n\\nAdd cleanup step at the end of workflow\\nusing new option recently introduced in podman:\\nhttps://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25102\\n\\nExample of job preventing s390x worker cleaning up and restarting properly:\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/13289159296/job/37105230728\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147110\\nApproved by: https://github.com/h... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"s390x: add cleanup for cancelled docker image builds (#147110)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/a00d2b514477b7dee15111810a4c43788de4a5e7\"},{\"author\":\"rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"5dab0ae\",\"date\":\"2025-02-12\",\"message\":\"[SkipFiles] Some more cleanup (#147013)\\n\\nThis isn't a no-op but I think it's fine. It changes the case where a\\nfunction f1 in a module in MOD_SKIPFILES calls a function f2 in one of\\nthe deleted modules. Previously f2 would have been skipped, now f2 gets\\ninlined.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147013\\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang\\nghstack dependencies: #147016, #147012\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"title\":\"[SkipFiles] Some more cleanup     "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Sherlock Huang <bahuang@meta.com>",
        "commit": "99bc2f9",
        "date": "2025-08-11",
        "message": "Update export/schema.py (#160220)\n\nSummary:\nModel could have multiple ExportedPrograms\n- for different methods. They can have different weights.\n- for different delegates. They can also have different weights.\n\nFor this reason, we make weight per ExportedProgram.\n\nAlso, we cleanup Model, and Program. IIUC, Model and Program are not used anywhere, so it's ok to make BC breaking change.\n\nTest Plan:\nCI\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D79917395\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160220\nApproved by: https://github.com/angelayi, https://github.com/dolpm, https://... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Update export/schema.py (#160220)",

      {
        "author": "Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>",
        "commit": "1128f4c",
        "date": "2025-08-08",
        "message": "[cuDNN][SDPA] cuDNN SDPA refactor/cleanup, nested tensor backward, test priority bump for `sm90`, `sm100` (#149282)\n\ncleanup tuple/tensor boilerplate in cuDNN SDPA, preparation for nested/ragged tensor backward\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149282\nApproved by: https://github.com/drisspg\n\nCo-authored-by: Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>",

      {
        "author": "Robert Hardwick <robert.hardwick@arm.com>",
        "commit": "334ecbd",
        "date": "2025-08-08",
        "message": "Add torchao to install_inductor_benchmark_deps cleanup stage (#160191)\n\nIt looks like `torcho` was missed from the cleanup during torchbench setup.\n\nFixes #160188\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160191\nApproved by: https://github.com/huydhn",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Add torchao to install_inductor_benchmark_deps cleanup stage (#160191)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/334ecbd4ffe11858cae7d23d1190ddb4777c2513"
      },
      {
        "author": "codingwithsurya <suryafromcali@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "ea7fe0e",
        "date": "2025-08-06",
        "message": "[SymmMem]  Standardize NVSHMEM Triton wrappers on byte-based APIs + improve code clarity (#159136)\n\nQuick refactor for consistency and clarity.\n\n1. We now standardize all NVSHMEM data-moving collectives (put, get, alltoall, broadcast) to use their byte-based *_mem_block variants. This makes the API behavior more predictable and avoids mixing paradigms.\n\n2. Previously, some functions operated on element counts (nelems), while others expected byte sizes but still used `nelems` as the param name. That inconsistency was easy to miss and could lead to bugs, especially for devs not familiar with the... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[SymmMem]  Standardize NVSHMEM Triton wrappers on byte-based APIs + improve code clarity (#159136)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/ea7fe0ecf62b44185181fba8263cfb6cbf58fa09"
      },
      {
        "author": "Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>",
        "commit": "ba4ccf5",
        "date": "2025-08-08",
        "message": "turn on executon frame clenaup by default (#160110)\n\nSummary: Turning execution frame cleanup back on since D78621408 is done\n\nTest Plan:\nSee D78621408\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D79730674\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160110\nApproved by: https://github.com/jingsh",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "turn on executon frame clenaup by default (#160110)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/ba4ccf5d67e3d237f435eacc2bce3c6025f08491"
      },
      {
        "author": "Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>",
        "commit": "c669b0a",
        "date": "2025-08-06",
        "message": "Fix execution frame cleanup logic (#158717)\n\nSummary: This fixes a bug in the execution fram cleanup logic - previously, whenever we hit the time interval to clear out the frames, we were removing any cached execution frames beyond the configured minimum number (frameEntry.used was unused). Instead, we only want to clear frames that were NOT USED in during the last time interval. This diff refactors the executor to have the correct logic.\n\nTest Plan:\n```\nbuck2 test 'mode/dev-nosan' fbcode//sigmoid/inference/test_gpu:model_runner_test -- ModelRunnerTest.Basic_InterpreterCuda_Multithread_Cleanup... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Fix execution frame cleanup logic (#158717)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/c669b0ab87d9d4950e8031afc038b22ddfce3d9b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>",
        "commit": "78d7f0c",
        "date": "2025-07-31",
        "message": "disable execution frame cleanup (#159531)\n\nSummary: Want to disable execution frame cleanup until fix in D78621408 is merged\n\nTest Plan:\nCI\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D79306602\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159531\nApproved by: https://github.com/SherlockNoMad",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "disable execution frame cleanup (#159531)",

      {
        "author": "fduwjj <fduwjj@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "67e68e0",
        "date": "2025-07-28",
        "message": "[c10d] Cleanup split_group logic using the newly built splitGroup (#158488)\n\nwith https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157716 merged we want to further clean up the code on the python side for `split_group` API. We do need to keep some old global book keeping for bc. The rest of logic is now all in cpp. Regarding the change brought in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152175, we did clean up in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158790 (including internal changes) so that we can safely remove it.\n\nDifferential Revision: [D78777152](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D7... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[c10d] Cleanup split_group logic using the newly built splitGroup (#158488)",

      },
      {
        "author": "Max Podkorytov <4273004+tenpercent@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "ee2edf3",
        "date": "2025-07-27",
        "message": "[ROCm][CK][Inductor] enable gfx950 for max autotune with CK (#159195)\n\n+ update inductor config for new gfx arch\n+ fixes in codegen for conv2d and ck-tile matmul\n+ use appropriate fp8 dtypes\n+ test cleanup\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159195\nApproved by: https://github.com/chenyang78",

        "title": "[ROCm][CK][Inductor] enable gfx950 for max autotune with CK (#159195)",

      },

        "message": "Cleanup old caffe2 scripts (#158475)\n\nTesting on this one is grep based: if there were no reference to that script I can find, I deleted.\nWe can easily add any of these back if needed!\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158475\nApproved by: https://github.com/seemethere, https://github.com/huydhn, https://github.com/cyyever",

        "commit": "0d77364",
        "date": "2025-07-12",
        "message": "dist2: cleanup non-option methods on PG (missing, timeouts) (#158123)\n\nThis updates the ProcessGroup.* API to include timeouts on all non-option based overloaded methods. This also adds 2 missing ones `alltoall_base` and `barrier`.\n\nFollowing design in: https://docs.google.com/document/d/13R-1t_yESTvmAjcCN-wQjQQadIEu0JNIdS65uZawZzY/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.3ctbqqopzc89\n\nTest plan:\n\n```\npytest test/distributed/test_dist2.py\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158123\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/fduwjj",

        "message": "xpu: support custom ops with torch.library on xpu backend (#152879)\n\nFixes: https://github.com/intel/torch-xpu-ops/issues/1626\n\nThis PR started enabling of tests for `torch.library`, but more work is needed. Tests are using `torch._custom_ops` deprecated API planned for removal at pytorch 2.6 (not done). I think cleanup of pytorch would be nice before enabling more tests for xpu.\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/a2ccda3c605bc37457a465711cee40775df54d2e/torch/_custom_op/impl.py#L47\n\nCC: @EikanWang\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152879\nApproved by: https://...       {
        "author": "Abhishek Nandy <anandy@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "e0fd48b",
        "date": "2025-07-05",
        "message": "Fix typo: 'occurances' \u2192 'occurrences' in mobile model test (#157629)\n\nThis PR addresses a typo in the file `test/mobile/model_test/gen_test_model.py`.\n\n### Changes:\n- Corrected \"occurances\" to the correct spelling \"occurrences\"\n- Renamed associated variables to reflect this change for consistency and clarity\n\nThis is a non-functional, cleanup-only PR to improve code readability.\n\nThanks to the PyTorch team for maintaining such a high-quality codebase\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157629\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Fix typo: 'occurances' \u2192 'occurrences' in mobile model test (#157629)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/e0fd48be7d526625cacfd093f3db13506ca9cc17"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "7b7eafe",
        "date": "2025-06-25",
        "message": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\n\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\n\nImplementation notes:\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/7b7eafe7ba159feed8324ba64e53c113fe7cb8b2"
      },
      {
        "author": "Nikita Shulga <2453524+malfet@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "214e295",
        "date": "2025-06-26",
        "message": "Cleanup leftover miniconda brew installation (#156898)\n\nThat results in torch.compile being unable to produce working artifacts\n\nShould fix https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156833\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156898\nApproved by: https://github.com/seemethere, https://github.com/atalman",

      {
        "author": "Pavan Balaji <pavanbalaji@meta.com>",
        "commit": "0d8e4e2",
        "date": "2025-06-25",
        "message": "[PG/nccl] improvements to eager init (#156748)\n\nSummary:\n\nCleanup eager init management, to detect and throw a warning when multiple p2p are issued on the same PG in eager init mode.\n\nTest Plan: CI\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156748\nApproved by: https://github.com/wconstab, https://github.com/kwen2501, https://github.com/Skylion007",

        "title": "[PG/nccl] improvements to eager init (#156748)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/0d8e4e2327137b370d91bd23931cd7691a95cc33"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "2c372a0",
        "date": "2025-06-19",
        "message": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\n\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\n\nImplementation notes:\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/2c372a0502578e0136a84423c3f49c19c26d6bb7"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "920f6e6",
        "date": "2025-06-17",
        "message": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\n\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\n\nImplementation notes:\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)",

      {
        "author": "Georgia Phillips <georgiaphillips@meta.com>",
        "commit": "af9f18e",
        "date": "2025-06-02",
        "message": "[nativert] Free stale execution frames (#154636)\n\nSummary:\nThis was implemented in SR due to caching of runtime instances building up and causing some memory usage spikes after some large amount traffic went through the model, and then once traffic went down, SR was still caching all the previous usage.\n\nWe need something similar on the Sigmoid side to make sure the static dispatch modules aren't hogging memory. Currently, all ExecutionFrame objects are being cached, and never freed if stale.\n\nTest Plan:\nAdded extra execution frames in tmp commit D75257998 and ran local replayer test to confir... [truncated]",

        "author": "Joaquin <joaquinmartinezfranco@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "cb56df5",
        "date": "2025-05-29",
        "message": "[Inductor]Cleanup autotune_fallback_to_aten post-deprecation (#154331)\n\nFixes #153298\n\nThis PR is the 3rd and final step of #147479\nAll references to autotune_fallback_to_aten have been removed, and the feature is now deprecated.\nAll calls to should_fallback_to_aten() were also removed, as they were deemed unnecessary.\n\n[henrylhtsang](https://github.com/henrylhtsang)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154331\nApproved by: https://github.com/henrylhtsang",

      {
        "author": "atalman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "3afbab6",
        "date": "2025-05-29",
        "message": "[BE] Remove unused release scripts. Add clarifications for the branch cut process (#154649)\n\nScripts in ``scripts/release/promote/`` are not used for a while.\nWe use the ones in test-infra [here](https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/blob/main/release/) .\nHence this small cleanup.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154649\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/huydhn",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",

        "commit": "ef1d45b",
        "date": "2025-05-28",
        "message": "Cleanup parent fallback logic (#154006)\n\nThe `parent` in fallback_node_due_to_unsupported_type is a duplication of `unsupported_output_tensor` logic. remove it. tested that the tests in test_add_complex give same codegen. this fixes an issue in mx that @drisspg was running into.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154006\nApproved by: https://github.com/drisspg",

        "title": "Cleanup parent fallback logic (#154006)",

      },
      {
        "author": "henrylhtsang <henrylhtsang@meta.com>",
        "commit": "b910d37",
        "date": "2025-05-19",
        "message": "[cutlass backend] Reduce log level for cutlass runtime error (#153457)\n\nWant to make sure we always call self.cleanup_run_fn() even if we crash.\n\nI think this is the reason why sometimes we get\n```\nin _dlclose\nTypeError: 'NoneType' object is not callable\n```\n\nDifferential Revision: [D74629230](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D74629230/)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153457\nApproved by: https://github.com/ColinPeppler",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[cutlass backend] Reduce log level for cutlass runtime error (#153457)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/b910d37ec6e6167a5d796824f17d4ea3ab3daedb"
      },
      {
        "author": "Laith Sakka <lsakka@meta.com>",
        "commit": "0ec8fe4",
        "date": "2025-05-19",
        "message": "cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)\n\nso two things other than cleanups and refactoring\n1) do not use propagate_real_tensors to resolve eval under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\n2) do not guard for dimensions of type  DimDynamic.OBLIVIOUS_SIZE under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152657\nApproved by: https://github.com/pianpwk",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/0ec8fe46d7c5df509c74a158fc05ff67295fa93e"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "1748fa5",
        "date": "2025-05-16",
        "message": "Revert \"cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)\"\n\nThis reverts commit f7fb2f66e3b60b6e3d8b3ac78aa435b76f49bc11.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152657 on behalf of https://github.com/malfet due to Broke lint ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152657#issuecomment-2887539146))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Revert \"cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/1748fa529a93f4aa7b69998ee5387bc91b653502"
      },
      {
        "author": "Nikita Shulga <nikita.shulga@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "62d8e3c",
        "date": "2025-05-16",
        "message": "[BE][MPS] Cleanup log ops migration (#153727)\n\nIntroduced by https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153398\n\nWorkaround internal compiler error on MacOS-13 by providing boolean specialization\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153727\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[BE][MPS] Cleanup log ops migration (#153727)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/62d8e3cb40d465585e81a7f7b3576471bdaca391"
      },
      {
        "author": "Laith Sakka <lsakka@meta.com>",
        "commit": "f7fb2f6",
        "date": "2025-05-13",
        "message": "cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)\n\nso two things other than cleanups and refactoring\n1) do not use propagate_real_tensors to resolve eval under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\n2) do not guard for dimensions of type  DimDynamic.OBLIVIOUS_SIZE under guard_or_true/guard_or_false .\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152657\nApproved by: https://github.com/pianpwk",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "cleanup, refactor and add missing  self._dde_suppressed checks (#152657)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/f7fb2f66e3b60b6e3d8b3ac78aa435b76f49bc11"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "f363a3f",

        "message": "[MPS] SDPA specialized kernels (#152781)\n\nParitally fixes #139668 and #152550\n\nStill work in progress.         "author": "Nikita Shulga <nikita.shulga@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "14293c2",
        "date": "2025-04-18",
        "message": "[MPS] Allow isin for mixed types (#151600)\n\nTo follow pattern set by CPU and CUDA impls: define common_dtype and optionally casts `elements` and `test_elements` to common dtype if needed\n\n- Add regression test, though skip it on MacOS-13, as `isin` seems to produce garbage there even for same dtypes\n```\n>>> import torch\n>>> x=torch.arange(4.0, device='mps')\n>>> y=torch.arange(1.0, 3.0, device='mps')\n>>> x, y, torch.isin(x, y), torch.isin(y, x)\n(tensor([0., 1., 2., 3.], device='mps:0'), tensor([1., 2.], device='mps:0'), tensor([False,  True, False, False], device='mps:0'), tensor([False, False]... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[MPS] Allow isin for mixed types (#151600)",

      {
        "author": "Blaine Burton Rister <145300525+blaine-rister@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "c0a0761",
        "date": "2025-04-15",
        "message": "[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\n\nPreparatory refactor for https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146942.\n\n# Feature\n\nThis PR refactors the existing wrapper codegen into `WrapperLine` subclasses, extending the existing Memory Planning IR into a fully-fledged Wrapper IR. See the diagram below.\n\n![wrapper_ir](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a61db21b-caf3-45d2-bfdb-91066ae4ba6b)\n\nThe IR currently supports the following ops:\n- All existing memory planning IR ops (`AllocateLine`, `FreeIfNotReusedLine`, etc.)\n- Reinterpret views (`ReinterpretLine`)\n- Kerne... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)",

        "message": "[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)\n\nPreparatory refactor for https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146942.\n\n# Feature\n\nThis PR refactors the existing wrapper codegen into `WrapperLine` subclasses, extending the existing Memory Planning IR into a fully-fledged Wrapper IR. See the diagram below.\n\n![wrapper_ir](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a61db21b-caf3-45d2-bfdb-91066ae4ba6b)\n\nThe IR currently supports the following ops:\n- All existing memory planning IR ops (`AllocateLine`, `FreeIfNotReusedLine`, etc.)\n- Reinterpret views (`ReinterpretLine`)\n- Kerne... [truncated]",

        "title": "[Inductor] Refactor wrapper codegen to use Wrapper IR. (#150458)",

      {
        "author": "Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>",
        "commit": "bb60e82",
        "date": "2025-04-11",
        "message": "c10d/Store: add queues (#150969)\n\nThis adds queue operations as described in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/150943.\n\nThis works by adding two new operations `queue_push` and `queue_pop`. The semantics are designed to be blocking with a timeout. Pushing will always succeed as the queue is infinite size. Popping will first call `wait` until the key is ready and then pop the value from the queue.\n\nThis implements queues for only: HashStore, TCPStore w/ libuv. FileStore and the legacy backends are not supported.\n\n`wait` and `check` work for queue operations though queue_push will only w... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "c10d/Store: add queues (#150969)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/bb60e8267204b46f607f24291918639fa4fb5ab6"
      },

        "message": "cleanup JK for duplicate pt2 compile callbacks prevention (#148704)\n\nSummary: This diff cleans up the JK we used for enabling `add pt2 callbacks for backward pass and prevent duplicate callbacks` feature.\n\nDifferential Revision: D70643543\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/148704\nApproved by: https://github.com/mlazos",

        "message": "[ONNX] Supporting different opset versions for torchlib registry (#149901)\n\n- Allows opset_version to determine which onnx decomposition to choose\n- Adds a cleanup function to modify the registry after it is built\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149901\nApproved by: https://github.com/justinchuby, https://github.com/titaiwangms",

        "date": "2025-04-03",
        "message": "[ROCm] code cleanup of architecture checks (#150473)\n\nThis PR replaces several calls to `at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties()->gcnArchName` and `at::cuda::getDeviceProperties(device_index)->gcnArchName` when checking to see if the GPU architecture is in a certain list.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/150473\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily, https://github.com/cyyever",

      {
        "author": "Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>",
        "commit": "45b1173",
        "date": "2025-03-26",
        "message": "[ROCm][TunableOp] TunableOp Context Manager for unit tests (#149930)\n\nThis PR is cleanup only. There are no feature changes or bug fixes.\n\nWe create a TunableOp context manager for setting up and cleanup. We re-write TunableOp unit tests in terms of this context manager. Ultimately reduces the amount of copy-paste code.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149930\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[ROCm][TunableOp] TunableOp Context Manager for unit tests (#149930)",

      },
      {
        "author": "eellison <elias.ellison@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "5757aa8",
        "date": "2025-03-21",
        "message": "Cudagraph fix + comment cleanup (#149741)\n\nCudagraphs is careful to not allow any memory recorded to escape globally without having a reference to the tensor. This is because we may later reclaim that memory for a cudagraph recording and we need to mark the tensor as erroring on access. Very occasionally, a stray tensor will have been allocated locally but not yet cleaned up. In this case, we enter the slow path and try to gc.collect() to deallocate it. From a hard to repro internal use case, this was fixed by an additional `cuda.synchronize()`.\n\ni also snuck in an outdated comment and a dupli... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Cudagraph fix + comment cleanup (#149741)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/5757aa87736bbc4d95f6219508325029c07e9749"
      },

        "author": "Michael Lazos <mlazos@meta.com>",
        "commit": "3474367",
        "date": "2025-03-21",
        "message": "[Dynamo] Cleanup state management for ctx managers (#149689)\n\nRemoves state indirection for ctx managers. This isn't needed anymore since VTs are mutable.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149689\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[Dynamo] Cleanup state management for ctx managers (#149689)",

      },

        "author": "Matthias Braun <matthiasb@meta.com>",
        "commit": "66dd00f",
        "date": "2025-03-21",
        "message": "Fix clang-tidy errors (#149581)\n\nSummary: Cleanup clang-tidy complaints in `EmbeddingBag.cpp`: Avoid shadowed variables and unused parameters.\n\nTest Plan: sandcastle\n\nDifferential Revision: D71512594\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149581\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/malfet",

      {
        "author": "Andrey Talman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "c9de76a",
        "date": "2025-03-19",
        "message": "Modify cuda aarch64 install for cudnn and nccl. Cleanup aarch64 cuda 12.6 docker (#149540)\n\n1. Use NCCL_VERSION=v2.26.2-1 . Fixes nccl cuda aarch64 related failure we see here: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/13955856471/job/39066681549?pr=149443 . After landing: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149351\nTODO: Followup required to unify NCCL definitions across the x86 and aarch64 builds\n\n3. Cleanup Remove older CUDA versions for aarch64 builds . CUDA 12.6 where removed by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/148895\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pyto... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Modify cuda aarch64 install for cudnn and nccl. Cleanup aarch64 cuda 12.6 docker (#149540)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/c9de76a1e45e24e45dc00285859457092b7b2a0c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Aleksei Nikiforov <aleksei.nikiforov@linux.ibm.com>",
        "commit": "cfbeaf7",
        "date": "2025-03-19",
        "message": "Improve docker build cleanup on s390x runners (#149316)\n\nCurrently it sometimes still leaves a couple of processess running.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149316\nApproved by: https://github.com/seemethere",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Improve docker build cleanup on s390x runners (#149316)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/cfbeaf7b7ed69aa239ca62fd0bc8da50ca1bc24f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>",
        "commit": "7ffadff",
        "date": "2025-03-08",
        "message": "c10d/ProcessGroup: cleanup abort and shutdown (#148798)\n\nThis adds `abort` and `shutdown` to `Backend` and `ProcessGroup` objects. This simplifies the logic in `distributed_c10d.py` by having a default noop implementation for all PGs.\n\nThis will be useful for torchft and upcoming versions of NCCL which will handle abort correctly. Currently `torchft` would have to call internal methods `_abort` on the PGNCCL object directly but with this change we can now just call `.abort()` and have it work for any PG implementation.\n\nTest plan:\n\n```\npytest distributed/test_backends.py distributed/test_c10d_... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "c10d/ProcessGroup: cleanup abort and shutdown (#148798)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/7ffadff286837168be12b9cd43dbf4968cd54e65"
      },
      {
        "author": "Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>",
        "commit": "93e9dae",
        "date": "2025-03-04",
        "message": "[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\n\nDisabled by default for now behind `TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED=1`\n\nJust wanted to get this out before starting a series of SDPA cleanup PRs---the biggest thing is we don't need the boilerplate around all of the `build_graph_and_tensors*` functions anymore as we can now use the `UID`-style referencing of tensor nodes as was done for the Conv-V8 API backend.\n\nCC @drisspg\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/141178\nApproved by: https://github.com/jbschlosser",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/93e9daed54249e3645fb1b1fe2012630b154a947"
      },
      {
        "author": "Arthur Laureus Wigo <126365160+arthurlw@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "9b7130b",
        "date": "2025-02-28",
        "message": "Clean temporary directory at exit (#147813)\n\nIssue: A temporary directory is created in [pytorch/torch/distributed/nn/jit/instantiator.py](https://github.com/arthurlw/pytorch/blob/clean-temp-directory-at-exit/torch/distributed/nn/jit/instantiator.py) but is never cleaned up, leading to a ResourceWarning on program exit.\n\nSolution: Registered an `atexit` handler to properly clean up the temporary directory when the program exits.\n\nFixes #147744\n\n**Line 23 in [0a49f8f](https://github.com/arthurlw/pytorch/commit/0a49f8fd3d34ee31f39bf7029ebb0b564433ac48)**\n```python\n23  atexit.register(_TEMP_DIR.c... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "Clean temporary directory at exit (#147813)",

      },
      {
        "author": "Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>",
        "commit": "b13ad1a",
        "date": "2025-02-26",
        "message": "[ROCm][TunableOp] Remove extra transpose characters in hipBLASLt signature. (#147900)\n\nCleanup the TunableOp hipBLASLt signature of extra transpose characters.\n\nTest manually and no new regressions found.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147900\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[ROCm][TunableOp] Remove extra transpose characters in hipBLASLt signature. (#147900)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/b13ad1a193f35817ef43c74cc76ba06f7a9c353c"

      {
        "author": "Nichols A. Romero <nick.romero@amd.com>",
        "commit": "fd8ae1a",
        "date": "2025-02-21",
        "message": "[ROCm] gfx940 and gfx941 cleanup (#147394)\n\nRemoving gfx architectures not supported by ROCm.\n\nNOTE: For users wanting to build PyTorch for gfx archs that are *not* supported by the official wheels on download.pytorch.org, you can build PyTorch from source for your desired gfx arch [using the PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH env var](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/README.md#amd-rocm-support).\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147394\nApproved by: https://github.com/jithunnair-amd, https://github.com/jeffdaily",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[ROCm] gfx940 and gfx941 cleanup (#147394)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/fd8ae1aa047b38b83325cc7b8ed8774f9f11008f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Eddie Yan <eddiey@nvidia.com>",
        "commit": "533b884",
        "date": "2025-02-21",
        "message": "[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)\n\nDisabled by default for now behind `TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED=1`\n\nJust wanted to get this out before starting a series of SDPA cleanup PRs---the biggest thing is we don't need the boilerplate around all of the `build_graph_and_tensors*` functions anymore as we can now use the `UID`-style referencing of tensor nodes as was done for the Conv-V8 API backend.\n\nCC @drisspg\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/141178\nApproved by: https://github.com/jbschlosser",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "[cuDNN][SDPA][Nested Tensor] Experimental cuDNN Nested Tensor SDPA Support (forward only) (#141178)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/533b884870acd951e684e0bf551eb76904dec047"
      },
      {
        "author": "vasiliy <vasiliy@fb.com>",
        "commit": "382fbcc",
        "date": "2025-02-20",
        "message": "add the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch (#147466)\n\nSummary:\n\nContinuing the work from https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/146427\n\nAdds the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch, as detailed in\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/146414 . Please see the issue for a detailed definition of the format.  Example of basic functionality:\n\n```python\nimport torch\n\n# round trip\nx0 = torch.randn(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32)\nx1 = x0.to(torch.float8_e8m0fnu)  # RNE rounding\nx2 = x1.to(torch.float32)  # 2 ** exponent\n\n# creation with empty\nx0 = torch.empty(4, 4, dtype=torch.float8_e8m0fnu)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "add the `torch.float8_e8m0fnu` dtype to PyTorch (#147466)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/382fbcc1e43ae5d46ec148bdfd8dcfb73da81b77"
      },
      {
        "author": "Aleksei Nikiforov <aleksei.nikiforov@linux.ibm.com>",
        "commit": "a00d2b5",
        "date": "2025-02-18",
        "message": "s390x: add cleanup for cancelled docker image builds (#147110)\n\nWhen podman image build is cancelled,\na couple of processes are left behind,\nand their existence prevents\nproper shutdown of runner container.\n\nAdd cleanup step at the end of workflow\nusing new option recently introduced in podman:\nhttps://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25102\n\nExample of job preventing s390x worker cleaning up and restarting properly:\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/13289159296/job/37105230728\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147110\nApproved by: https://github.com/h... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
        "title": "s390x: add cleanup for cancelled docker image builds (#147110)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/commit/a00d2b514477b7dee15111810a4c43788de4a5e7"
      },
      {
        "author": "rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "5dab0ae",
        "date": "2025-02-12",
        "message": "[SkipFiles] Some more cleanup (#147013)\n\nThis isn't a no-op but I think it's fine. It changes the case where a\nfunction f1 in a module in MOD_SKIPFILES calls a function f2 in one of\nthe deleted modules. Previously f2 would have been skipped, now f2 gets\ninlined.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147013\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang\nghstack dependencies: #147016, #147012",

type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851:         code: CodeType,\\n852:         one_graph: bool,\\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n857:             stack.enter_context(\\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n860:                 )\\n861:             )\\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\\n864: \\n865:         return (\\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\\n867:         )  # {
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n2226:         return name\\n2227: \\n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266:     \\\"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \\\"\\n2267:     \\\"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\\\"\\n2268: )\\n2269: \\n2270: \\n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\\n2273: ) -> None:\\n2274:     if kind != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2275:         return\\n2276: \\n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\\\"prim\\\", \\\"prims\\\", \\\"aten\\\"}:\\n2279:             return\\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\\n2281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2225-2280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2226:         return name\n2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266:     \"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \"\n2267:     \"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\"\n2268: )\n2269: \n2270: \n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\n2273: ) -> None:\n2274:     if kind != \"call_function\":\n2275:         return\n2276: \n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\"prim\", \"prims\", \"aten\"}:\n2279:             return\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\n2281: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2225-2280"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n817:             package=package,\\n818:         )\\n819: \\n820:         try:\\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n823:                 tracer.run()\\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL815-850\"}",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \\n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n489:         )\\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL470-520\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: \n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\n489:         )\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521: ",
