  "content": "{\"content\":\"430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL430-480\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "430: \n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n432:         # sure...\n433:         self.co_fields = {\n434:             \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n435:             \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n436:             \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n437:         }\n438: \n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n440: \n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451:             #\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: ",

{
  "content": "# This file will contain the implementation of the cleanup for failed tracer outputs\n\n# Based on the task description, I need to:\n# 1. Clean up graph nodes' doubly linked list\n# 2. Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake references\n# 3. Clear FakeTensorMode references\n# 4. Clear fake_tensor_cache\n\n# The reference cycles are:\n# 1. Graph nodes form a doubly linked list that points back to graph\n# 2. FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n\n# Places to modify:\n# 1. torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py - add cleanup_on_exception method\n# 2. torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py - call cleanup_on_exception on failure\n# 3. torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py - ensure cleanup method works\n# 4. torch/_guards.py - already has cleanup call in tracing context\n\n# Let me identify the exact changes needed:\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/cleanup_fix.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# This file will contain the implementation of the cleanup for failed tracer outputs\n\n# Based on the task description, I need to:\n# 1. Clean up graph nodes' doubly linked list\n# 2. Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake references\n# 3. Clear FakeTensorMode references\n# 4. Clear fake_tensor_cache\n\n# The reference cycles are:\n# 1. Graph nodes form a doubly linked list that points back to graph\n# 2. FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n\n# Places to modify:\n# 1. torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py - add cleanup_on_exception method\n# 2. torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py - call cleanup_on_exception on failure\n# 3. torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py - ensure cleanup method works\n# 4. torch/_guards.py - already has cleanup call in tracing context\n\n# Let me identify the exact changes needed:\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/cleanup_fix.py",
  "originalFile": null,

41. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 2260,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 2228
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2228-2260\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: ",

42. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "new_string": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup graphs from failed dynamo tracer outputs.\n        \n        These graphs hold onto reference cycles:\n        1. The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the graph\n        2. FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n        \n        To avoid needing a full garbage collection, manually clean up these reference\n        cycles to allow for immediate garbage collection.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            node.all_input_nodes.clear()\n        \n        # Clear TrackedFake references\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n        \n        # Clear the ShapeEnv references\n        if self.shape_env is not None:\n            self.shape_env.cleanup()\n            # Clear the fake_tensor_cache which may hold references to FakeTensors\n            self.shape_env.fake_tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(",
  "old_string": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(",
  "replace_all": false
}

It owns\\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\\n9: \\n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\\n13: \\n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested "{\"content\":\"3000:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3001:         Installs a global, generating a unique name for it.\\n3002: \\n3003:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\\n3004:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3005:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\\n3006:         name = unique_id(prefix)\\n3007:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n3008:         return name\\n3009: \\n3010:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n3011:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n3012:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n3013:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n3014:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n3015:         self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.clear()\\n3016:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n3017: \\n3018:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n3019:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n3020:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n3021:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n3022:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n3023:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n3024:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n3025:         self.mro_source_cache.clear()\\n3026:         self.guarded_mro_absent_keys.clear()\\n3027:         self.signature_cache.clear()\\n3028:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n3029:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n3030:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n3031:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n3032:         self.leaf_var_creation_order.clear()\\n3033:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n3034:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n3035: \\n3036:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n3037:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n3038:     ) -> None:\\n3039:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n3040: \\n3041:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n3042:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3043:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n3044:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n3045:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n3046:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n3047: \\n3048:     def add_fqn_info_for_inlined_modules(\\n3049:         self, inlined_module: torch.nn.Module, source: Source\\n3050:     ) -> None:\\n3051:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(source.name)\\n3052:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(\\n3053:             name, self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names, self.global_scope\\n3054:         )\\n3055:         self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.add(name)\\n3056: \\n3057:         def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\\n3058:             assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n3059:             new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\\n3060:             new_name = f\\\"{name}.{leaf_name}\\\"\\n3061:             self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\\n3062:             if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\\n3063:                 self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\\n3064:                     OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\\n3065:                 ] = leaf_name\\n3066: \\n3067:         # "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\\n4: \\n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\\n9: \\n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\\n13: \\n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\\n19: - Guard insertion and management\\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: import collections\\n23: import contextlib\\n24: import copy\\n25: import dataclasses\\n26: import functools\\n27: import inspect\\n28: import itertools\\n29: import logging\\n30: import operator\\n31: import re\\n32: import sys\\n33: import time\\n34: import traceback\\n35: import warnings\\n36: import weakref\\n37: from collections.abc import Callable, Generator, Sequence\\n38: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\\n39: from types import CodeType\\n40: from typing import Any, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n41: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\\n42: \\n43: import sympy\\n44: \\n45: import torch._guards\\n46: import torch._logging\\n47: import torch.distributed as dist\\n48: import torch.nn\\n49: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\\n50: from torch import fx, Tensor\\n51: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\\n52: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\\n53: from torch._guards import (\\n54:     CompileContext,\\n55:     CompileId,\\n56:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\\n57:     Source,\\n58:     tracing,\\n59: .variables.functions import ClosureConversionError, VariableTracker\\n166: from .variables.lists import BaseListVariable\\n167: from .variables.misc import NullVariable\\n168: from .variables.nn_module import NNModuleVariable\\n169: from .variables.tensor import (\\n170:     NumpyNdarrayVariable,\\n171:     SymNodeVariable,\\n172:     UnspecializedPythonVariable,\\n173: )\\n174: from .variables.torch_function import TensorWithTFOverrideVariable\\n175: from .variables.user_defined import UserDefinedDictVariable\\n176: \\n177: \\n178: if TYPE_CHECKING:\\n179:     from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import DynamoProfilerState\\n180:     from torch._dynamo.package import CompilePackage\\n181:     from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslatorBase\\n182:     from torch._inductor import _CudagraphAnnotation\\n183:     from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\\n184: \\n185: log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n186: graph_tabular_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"graph\\\")\\n187: graph_code_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"graph_code\\\")\\n188: graph_sizes_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"graph_sizes\\\")\\n189: trace_call_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"trace_call\\\")\\n190: \\n191: RootGuardManager = guards.RootGuardManager\\n192: \\n193: \\n194: # Capture fn pointer at import time\\n195: # This is to guard against trying to mark the iterated tensors\\n196: # as static in case user overrides fn ptr\\n197: og_module_named_buffers_fn_ptr = torch.nn.Module.named_buffers\\n198: og_module_named_parameters_fn_ptr = torch.nn.Module.named_parameters\\n199: \\n200: \\n201: def _wrap_with_inductor_config(\\n202:     compiler_fn: Any, config_patches: dict[str, Any]\\n203: ) -> Callable[..., Any]:\\n204:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n205:     Wrap a compiler function to apply inductor config patches during compilation.\\n206:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n207:     from torch._inductor import config as inductor_config\\n208: \\n209:     def wrapped(gm: Any, example_inputs: Any) -> Any:\\n210:         with inductor_config.patch(config_patches):\\n211:             return compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\\n212: \\n213:     # Preserve function metadata for logging\\n214:     wrapped.__name__ = getattr(compiler_fn, \\\"__name__\\\", \\\"<wrapped>\\\")\\n215:     wrapped.__wrapped__ = compiler_fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n216:     return wrapped\\n217: \\n218: \\n219: @dataclass(frozen=True)\\n220: class AliasingInfo:\\n221:     has_aliasing: bool\\n222:     msg: str\\n223: \\n224: \\n225: @dataclass(frozen=True)\\n226: class MutationInfo:\\n227:     has_mutation: bool\\n228:     msg: str\\n229: \\n230: \\n231: def collect_reachable_grad_fns(\\n232:     tensors_with_sources: list[tuple[torch.Tensor, str | None]],\\n233:     stop_at: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] | None = None,\\n234: ) -> set[torch.autograd.graph.Node]:\\n235:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Collect all grad_fns reachable from tensors' autograd graphs.\\n236: \\n237:     Performs a DFS traversal and collects all visited grad_fns.\\n238:     Optionally stops traversal nodes in stop_at set. This signals the\\n239:     autograd.grad boundary.\\n240: \\n241:     Args:\\n242:         tensors_with_sources: List of (tensor, source_name) tuples to start search from.\\n243:         stop_at: Optional set of grad_fns where traversal should stop (excluded from result).\\n244: \\n245:     Returns:\\n246:         Set of all reachable grad_fns.\\n247:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n248:     if stop_at is None:\\n249:         stop_at = set()\\n250: \\n251:     visited: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\\n252:     stack: list[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = []\\n253: \\n254:     for tensor, _ in tensors_with_sources:\\n255:         if isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):\\n256:             grad_fn = tensor.grad_fn\\n257:             if grad_fn is not None:\\n258:                 stack.append(grad_fn)\\n259: \\n260:     while stack:\\n261:         node = stack.pop()\\n262:         if node in visited:\\n263:             continue\\n264:         # Stop traversal at stop_at nodes and don't include them\\n265:         # in consumed grad_fn list.\\n266:         if node in stop_at:\\n267:             continue\\n268:         visited.add(node)\\n269:         for next_fn, _ in node.next_functions:\\n270:             if next_fn is not None:\\n271:                 stack.append(next_fn)\\n272:     return visited\\n273: \\n274: \\n275: @functools.cache\\n276: def _step_logger() -> Any:\\n277:     return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\\n278: \\n279: \\n280: @dataclass\\n281: class GraphCompileReason:\\n282:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n283: \\n284:     reason: str\\n285:     user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\\n286: \\n287:     # Indicates if this was a graph break reason due to graph break.\\n288:     graph_break: bool = True\\n289: \\n290:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:\\n291:         if self.graph_break:\\n292:             graph_break_reasons.append(self)\\n293: \\n294: \\n295: def _get_gen_rand_values_fn(random_calls: Any) -> Callable[[], list[Any]]:\\n296:     def _gen_rand_values() -> list[Any]:\\n297:         return [fn(*args, **kwargs) for fn, args, kwargs in random_calls]\\n298: \\n299:     return _gen_rand_values\\n300: \\n301: \\n302: class FakeRootModule(torch.nn.Module):\\n303:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Trick the constructor of fx.GraphModule\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n304: \\n305:     def __init__(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\\n306:         super().__init__()\\n307:         for k, v in nn_modules.items():\\n308:             setattr(self, k, v)\\n309: \\n310:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\\n311:         return \\\"FakeRootModule(...)\\\"\\n312: \\n313:     def add_nn_modules(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\\n314:         for k, v in nn_modules.items():\\n315:             setattr(self, k, v)\\n316: \\n317: \\n318: class WrapperBackend:\\n319:     def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:\\n320:         self.backend: CompilerFn = backend\\n321: \\n322:     def __call__(\\n323:         self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]\\n324:     ) -> CompiledFn:\\n325:         self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)\\n326:         self.gm = gm\\n327:         copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)\\n328:         self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)\\n329: \\n330:         if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:\\n331:             return self.gm.forward\\n332: \\n333:         if not config.verify_correctness:\\n334:             return self.candidate\\n335: \\n336:         # if verify_correctness=True\\n337:         try:\\n338:             correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\\n339:             result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\\n340: \\n341:             # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\\n342:             if same(correct, result):\\n343:                 return self.candidate\\n344: \\n345:             raise RuntimeError(f\\\"incorrect results of backend {self}\\\")\\n346: \\n347:         except Exception:\\n348:             log.exception(\\\"error in verify_correctness\\\")\\n349:             raise\\n350:         finally:\\n351:             self.restore()\\n352: \\n353: \\n354: Scope = dict[str, object]\\n355: \\n356: \\n357: @dataclass\\n358: class BytecodeTracingTimings:\\n359:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Accumulated wall-clock time (ns) for major components during Dynamo\\n360:     bytecode tracing that are not related to variable trackers.  Each field\\n361:     is an int accumulator that gets bumped via ``time.time_ns()`` in the\\n362:     corresponding hot-path wrapper.  To add a new timer, add a field here\\n363:     and wire up the wrapper in the relevant function.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n364: \\n365:     get_fake_value_ns: int = 0\\n366:     create_proxy_ns: int = 0\\n367:     wrap_to_fake_tensor_and_record_ns: int = 0\\n368:     variable_builder_call_ns: int = 0\\n369: \\n370:     def report_and_reset(self) -> None:\\n371:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Flush accumulated timings to the bytecode_tracing chromium event\\n372:         and to compilation_time_metrics, then reset all counters.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n373:         chromium_log = get_chromium_event_logger()\\n374:         # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n375:         event_data = {}\\n376:         for f in dataclasses.fields(self):\\n377:             ns_val = getattr(self, f.name)\\n378:             if ns_val > 0:\\n379:                 key = f.name.removesuffix(\\\"_ns\\\")\\n380:                 event_data[f\\\"{key}_time_s\\\"] = ns_val / 1e9\\n381:                 compilation_time_metrics.setdefault(key, []).append(ns_val / 1e9)\\n382:                 setattr(self, f.name, 0)\\n383:         if event_data:\\n384:             chromium_log.try_add_event_data(\\\"bytecode_tracing\\\", **event_data)\\n385: \\n386: \\n387: @dataclass\\n388: class OutputGraphGuardsState:\\n389:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n390:     A base class containing fields that are considered \\\"persistent\\\" when we\\n391:     want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\\n392:     process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\\n393:     the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\\n394:     supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\\n395:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n396: \\n397:     local_scope: Scope\\n398:     global_scope: Scope\\n399:     # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\\n400:     torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\\n401:     guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\\n402:     # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\\n403:     input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\\n404:     dual_level: int\\n405:     functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\\n406:     current_device: Optional[torch.device]\\n407:     global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\\n408:     _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\\n409:     _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\\n410: \\n411:     # Whether or not the guards should be checked for correctness\\n412: \\n413:     export: bool = False\\n414:     skip_guards_check: bool = False\\n415:     export_constraints: bool = False\\n416:     name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\\n417: \\n418:     @property\\n419:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n420:         raise AssertionError(f\\\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\\\")\\n421: \\n422:     @property\\n423:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\\n424:         return self._guards\\n425: \\n426:     @property\\n427:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\\n428:         return self._aotautograd_guards\\n429: \\n430:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> \\\"OutputGraphGuardsState\\\":\\n431:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\\n432:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\\n433:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\\n434:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\\n435:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\\n436:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\\n437:             input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\\n438:             dual_level=self.dual_level,\\n439:             functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\\n440:             current_device=self.current_device,\\n441:             global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\\n442:             name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\\n443:             export=self.export,\\n444:             export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\\n445:             _guards=self.guards,\\n446:             _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\\n447:             skip_guards_check=self.skip_guards_check,\\n448:         )\\n449: \\n450: \\n451: @dataclass\\n452: class StackLocalsMetadata:\\n453:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n454:     Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\\n455:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n456: \\n457:     num_stack: int = 0  # number of stack elements, minus removed NULLs\\n458:     locals_names: dict[str, int] = dc_field(\\n459:         default_factory=dict\\n460:     )  # order of locals codegen'd to the stack\\n461:     stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n462:     locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n463:     stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n464:     stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n465:     locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n466: \\n467: \\n468: # TODO we should expand this to make it work for atribtrary in/out\\n469: @dataclass\\n470: class ExportMetaData:\\n471:     # maps graph input index to its' source which is later\\n472:     # used in export to map to correct user input. In its' flat form,\\n473:     # just looks like GetItem(base=LocalSource(\\\"foo\\\", idx=0))\\n474:     graph_input_idx_to_local_source: dict[int, Source | None] = dc_field(\\n475:         default_factory=dict\\n476:     )\\n477:     # maps user output idx to what type of output it is. There are 3 options:\\n478:     # 1) graph out\\n479:     # 2) user input\\n480:     # 3) constants\\n481:     output_return_type: dict[int, tuple[str, Any]] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)\\n482:     # output spec of the traced function\\n483:     out_spec: Union[torch.utils._pytree.TreeSpec, torch.utils._pytree.LeafSpec] = (\\n484:         torch.utils._pytree._LEAF_SPEC\\n485:     )\\n486:     module_call_spec: dict[\\n487:         str,\\n488:         dict[str, Union[torch.utils._pytree.TreeSpec, torch.utils._pytree.LeafSpec]],\\n489:     ] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)\\n490: \\n491: \\n492: def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n493:     # f_globals[\\\"__builtins__\\\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\\n494:     # implementation detail -\\n495:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\\n496: \\n497:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\\n498:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\\n499:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\\n500: \\n501:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\\n502:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\\n503:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\\n504:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\\n505: \\n506:     f_builtins = global_scope[\\\"__builtins__\\\"]\\n507:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\\n508:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\\n509:     return f_builtins\\n510: \\n511: \\n512: class OutputGraphCommon(OutputGraphGuardsState):\\n513:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n514:     A minimal interface for full graph capture. It is intended to be\\n515:     the target of any tracer that feeds into backends.\\n516: \\n517:     Currently dynamo's OutputGraph is the only known implementation\\n518:     of this interface, used by (aot) precompile and (strict) export.\\n519:     Importantly, that implementation also contains many other fields\\n520:     that are using during tracing but not included in this interface\\n521:     because they are not used once tracing is complete.\\n522: \\n523:     It should be safe to assume that (caching) precompile also uses\\n524:     this interface.\\n525: \\n526:     In the future, we want make_fx, used by (non-strict) export, to\\n527:     also implement this interface.\\n528: \\n529:     The serializable part of this interface is OutputGraphGuardsState.\\n530:     We do not need to serialize other parts; however it will pay to\\n531:     be disciplined about what those other parts are, especially since\\n532:     we want other tracers to be able to meaningfully implement them,\\n533:     and we should generally try to cut them down when possible.\\n534:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n535: \\n536:     def __init__(\\n537:         self,\\n538:         output_graph_guards_state: OutputGraphGuardsState,\\n539:         import_sources: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None,\\n540:         shape_env: Optional[ShapeEnv] = None,\\n541:         export_metadata: Optional[ExportMetaData] = None,\\n542:         tracked_fakes_id_to_source: Optional[dict[int, list[Source]]] = None,\\n543:     ) -> None:\\n544:         super().__init__(\\n545:             output_graph_guards_state.local_scope,\\n546:             output_graph_guards_state.global_scope,\\n547:             output_graph_guards_state.torch_function_mode_stack,\\n548:             output_graph_guards_state.guard_on_key_order,\\n549:             output_graph_guards_state.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\\n550:             output_graph_guards_state.dual_level,\\n551:             output_graph_guards_state.functorch_layers,\\n552:             output_graph_guards_state.current_device,\\n553:             output_graph_guards_state.global_state_guard,\\n554:             output_graph_guards_state._guards,\\n555:             output_graph_guards_state._aotautograd_guards,\\n556:             output_graph_guards_state.export,\\n557:             output_graph_guards_state.skip_guards_check,\\n558:             output_graph_guards_state.export_constraints,\\n559:             output_graph_guards_state.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\\n560:         )\\n561: \\n562:         self.import_sources = import_sources or {}\\n563:         # The following fields are currently known to be used by clients.\\n564:         # In particular, we need:\\n565:         # - shape_env, for building guards\\n566:         # - export_metadata, for un/flattening inputs and outputs\\n567:         # - tracked_fakes_id_to_source, for processing tensor dim constraints\\n568:         self._shape_env = shape_env or ShapeEnv()  # private for inheritance\\n569:         self.export_metadata = export_metadata or ExportMetaData()\\n570:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n571:             tracked_fakes_id_to_source or {}\\n572:         )\\n573: \\n574:     @property\\n575:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n576:         return self._shape_env\\n577: \\n578:     def bypass_package(self, reason: str = \\\"\\\", **kwargs: Any) -> None:\\n579:         # NOTE: currently there are no tests for this but it is reachable\\n580:         # when building guards, so technically necessary to include here.\\n581:         # It is unclear whether we should include packaging altogether.\\n582:         raise NotImplementedError\\n583: \\n584: \\n585: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphCommon):\\n586:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n587:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\\n588:     generated fx.Graph.\\n589: \\n590:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n591:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\\n592:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\\n593:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\\n594:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n595: \\n596:     side_effects: SideEffects\\n597: \\n598:     def __init__(\\n599:         self,\\n600:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n601:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n602:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n603:         export: bool,\\n604:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n605:         frame_state: Any,\\n606:         local_scope: Scope,\\n607:         global_scope: Scope,\\n608:         f_code: CodeType,\\n609:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n610:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n611:         one_graph: bool = False,\\n612:     ) -> None:\\n613:         OutputGraphGuardsState.__init__(\\n614:             self,\\n615:             local_scope,\\n616:             global_scope,\\n617:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n618:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n619:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n620:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n621:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n622:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n623:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n624:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n625:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n626:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n627:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n628:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n629:         )\\n630:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n631:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n632:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n633:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n634:         # List of TensorVariables that are leaf tensors created in-graph\\n635:         # (e.g., nn.Parameter via tracable_create_parameter). These need to be\\n636:         # tracked separately from input_source_to_var for backward() auto-detection.\\n637:         self.leaf_var_creation_order: list[VariableTracker] = []\\n638:         self.export = export\\n639:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n640:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n641:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n642:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n643:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n644:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n645:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n646: \\n647:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n648:         # sure...\\n649:         self.co_fields = {\\n650:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n651:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n652:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n653:         }\\n654: \\n655:         # Cudagraph annotation is set during inlining in\\n656:         # InliningInstructionTranslator.build_inline_tracer when a decorated\\n657:         # function is inlined.\\n658:         self.cudagraph_annotation: Optional[_CudagraphAnnotation] = None\\n659: \\n660:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n661:         self._emit_debugger_breakpoint: bool = False\\n662: \\n663:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n664:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n665:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n666:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n667:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n668:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n669: \\n670:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n671:             # Reference Cycle!\\n672:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n673:             #\\n674:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n675:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n676:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n677:             # the program execution.\\n678:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n679:             # We want to allow capture scalar outputs and allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops when fullgraph=True\\n680:             allow_scalar_outputs=one_graph or config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n681:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=one_graph\\n682:             or config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n683:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n684:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n685:         )\\n686: \\n687:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n688:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n689:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n690: \\n691:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n692:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n693:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n694:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n695:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=bool(self.export),\\n696:                 export=self.export,\\n697:             )\\n698:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n699:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n700:         self.tracing_context.cudagraph_annotation = self.cudagraph_annotation\\n701:         self.traced_code = self.tracing_context.traced_code\\n702:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n703:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n704:         )\\n705:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n706: \\n707:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n708:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n709:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n710:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n711:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n712:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n713:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n714:         )\\n715:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n716:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n717:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n718:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n719:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n720:         self.variable_tracker_cache: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n721:         # Cache for sources resolved via MRO walk, keyed by id(obj).\\n722:         # When the same descriptor (e.g. property) is reached from multiple\\n723:         # subclasses, we reuse the first source to avoid redundant guards.\\n724:         # We thought of rolling this in variable_tracker_cache but here\\n725:         # different sources point to the same object, we also don't want it to\\n726:         # go through the side effects cache because even though these objects\\n727:         # are same, we dont want OBJECT_ALIASING guards on them. For these\\n728:         # objects, we have DICT_CONTAINS absent guards on the mro walk, so there\\n729:         # is no need of the OBJECT_ALIASING guards.\\n730:         self.mro_source_cache: dict[tuple[int, str], DictGetItemSource] = {}\\n731:         # Tracks (id(klass), attr_name) pairs that already have a\\n732:         # DICT_CONTAINS absent guard installed during MRO walks.  When\\n733:         # multiple subclasses share the same intermediate MRO class, we\\n734:         # only need to guard the absence once per (class, attr) pair.\\n735:         self.guarded_mro_absent_keys: set[tuple[int, str]] = set()\\n736:         # Cache for inspect.signature results: function -> VariableTracker\\n737:         self.signature_cache: dict[Any, VariableTracker] = {}\\n738:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n739:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n740:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n741:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n742:         # and restore_graphstate\\n743:         self.timestamp = 0\\n744: \\n745:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n746:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n747: \\n748:         # Not checkpointed\\n749:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n750:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n751: \\n752:         # Profiler state for tracking function trace timings\\n753:         self.profiler_state: Optional[DynamoProfilerState] = None\\n754: \\n755:         self.package = package\\n756:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n757:         # accessed it?\\n758:         #\\n759:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n760:         #   - During export, and\\n761:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n762:         #\\n763:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n764:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n765:         # recording!\\n766:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n767: \\n768:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n769:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n770:         self.should_exit = False\\n771:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n772: \\n773:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n774:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n775: \\n776:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n777:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n778:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n779:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n780:         # back for backend errors.\\n781:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n782: \\n783:         # Tracks ALL grad_fn nodes that are consumed by torch.autograd.grad(outputs, inputs).\\n784:         # This is the set of all nodes reachable from outputs' grad_fns, excluding inputs' grad_fns\\n785:         # (since autograd.grad stops at inputs without consuming them).\\n786:         # Used to detect returning tensors connected to consumed grad_fns (would cause\\n787:         # \\\"backward through graph a second time\\\" error in aot_autograd).\\n788:         self.autograd_grad_consumed_grad_fns: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\\n789: \\n790:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n791:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n792:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n793: \\n794:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n795:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n796:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n797: \\n798:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n799:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n800:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n801:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n802:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n803:         self.save_global_state()\\n804: \\n805:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n806:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n807:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n808: \\n809:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n810:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n811:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n812:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n813:         self.random_calls: list[\\n814:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\\n815:         ] = []\\n816:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\\n817: \\n818:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\\n819:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\\n820: \\n821:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\\n822:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\\n823:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\\n824:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\\n825: \\n826:         # pyrefly: ignore [bad-override]\\n827:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\\n828:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n829:         )\\n830: \\n831:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n832: \\n833:         self.bytecode_tracing_timings = BytecodeTracingTimings()\\n834: \\n835:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n836:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\\n837:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\\n838:         # context manager inside a compiled region\\n839:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\\n840:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\\n841:         )\\n842: \\n843:         # mangled alias -> module fqn name\\n844:         self.import_sources: dict[str, str] = {}\\n845: \\n846:         self.export_metadata = ExportMetaData()\\n847: \\n848:         # Set of inlined unspecialized modules names to generate the\\n849:         # dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn mapping.\\n850:         self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\\n851: \\n852:         self.attr_source_cache: dict[tuple[Source, str], AttrSource] = {}\\n853: \\n854:     def get_chained_attr_source(self, base: Source, path: str) -> AttrSource:\\n855:         parts = path.split(\\\".\\\")\\n856:         key = (base, parts[0])\\n857:         if key not in self.attr_source_cache:\\n858:             self.attr_source_cache[key] = AttrSource(base, parts[0])\\n859:         result = self.attr_source_cache[key]\\n860:         for part in parts[1:]:\\n861:             key = (result, part)\\n862:             if key not in self.attr_source_cache:\\n863:                 self.attr_source_cache[key] = AttrSource(result, part)\\n864:             result = self.attr_source_cache[key]\\n865:         return result\\n866: \\n867:     def get_chained_param_buffer_source(\\n868:         self, base: Source, path: str\\n869:     ) -> \\\"ParamBufferSource\\\":\\n870:         parts = path.rsplit(\\\".\\\", 1)\\n871:         if len(parts) == 1:\\n872:             return ParamBufferSource(base, path)\\n873:         intermediate_base = self.get_chained_attr_source(base, parts[0])\\n874:         return ParamBufferSource(intermediate_base, parts[1])\\n875: \\n876:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\\n877:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\\n878:             dynamo_timed(\\n879:                 \\\"bytecode_tracing\\\",\\n880:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n881:             )\\n882:         )\\n883:         # Start profiler timing for the root function\\n884:         if config.dynamo_profiler:\\n885:             from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import DynamoProfilerState\\n886: \\n887:             if self.profiler_state is None:\\n888:                 self.profiler_state = DynamoProfilerState()\\n889:             code = self.root_tx.f_code\\n890:             self.profiler_state.push(\\n891:                 code.co_name,\\n892:                 code.co_filename,\\n893:                 code.co_firstlineno,\\n894:                 time.time_ns(),\\n895:             )\\n896: \\n897:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\\n898:         self.bytecode_tracing_timings.report_and_reset()\\n899:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\\n900:         # Record profiler timing for the root function and dump stats\\n901:         if config.dynamo_profiler and self.profiler_state is not None:\\n902:             from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import FunctionTraceTiming\\n903: \\n904:             stack_entry = self.profiler_state.pop()\\n905:             trace_end_ns = time.time_ns()\\n906:             if stack_entry is not None:\\n907:                 cumtime_ns = trace_end_ns - stack_entry.start_time_ns\\n908:                 tottime_ns = cumtime_ns - stack_entry.child_time_ns\\n909:                 timing = FunctionTraceTiming(\\n910:                     func_name=stack_entry.func_name,\\n911:                     filename=stack_entry.filename,\\n912:                     firstlineno=stack_entry.firstlineno,\\n913:                     cumtime_ns=cumtime_ns,\\n914:                     tottime_ns=tottime_ns,\\n915:                     bytecode_count=len(self.root_tx.f_code.co_code),\\n916:                     inline_depth=0,\\n917:                     caller_func_name=None,\\n918:                     caller_filename=None,\\n919:                     caller_firstlineno=None,\\n920:                     is_primitive_call=stack_entry.is_primitive_call,\\n921:                     call_stack=(),\\n922:                 )\\n923:                 self.profiler_state.record_timing(timing)\\n924: \\n925:             # Dump profiler stats\\n926:             output_file = None\\n927:             if isinstance(config.dynamo_profiler, str):\\n928:                 output_file = config.dynamo_profiler\\n929:             self.profiler_state.dump_stats(output_file)\\n930: \\n931:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\\n932:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\\n933:         return self.install_global(\\\"__builtins_dict__\\\", f_builtins)\\n934: \\n935:     def add_backward_state_hook(\\n936:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \\\"hook\\\"\\n937:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\\n938:         name = f\\\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\\\"\\n939:         assert name not in self.backward_state\\n940:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\\n941:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\\n942: \\n943:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n944:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\\n945:             if self.export:\\n946:                 unimplemented(\\n947:                     gb_type=\\\"backward_state does not support export\\\",\\n948:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n949:                     explanation=\\\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\\\",\\n950:                     hints=[],\\n951:                 )\\n952:             example_value = BackwardState()\\n953:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\\n954:                 \\\"dynamo_backward_state\\\",\\n955:                 type(example_value),\\n956:                 example_value,\\n957:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\\n958:             )\\n959:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\\n960:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\\n961:         return self.backward_state_proxy\\n962: \\n963:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\\n964:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\\n965:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\\n966:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\\n967:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\\n968: \\n969:         self.guards.add(\\n970:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\\n971:         )\\n972: \\n973:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\\n974: \\n975:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\\n976: \\n977:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GLOBAL_STATE))\\n978:         self.guards.add(\\n979:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\\n980:         )\\n981: \\n982:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\\n983:         if ci is not None:\\n984:             self.guards.add(\\n985:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\\n986:             )\\n987:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n988:             self.guards.add(\\n989:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\\n990:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\\n991:                 )\\n992:             )\\n993: \\n994:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\\n995:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n996:             return None\\n997: \\n998:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\\n999:         are_inline_hooks = (\\n1000:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1-1000\"}"
57. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

are_inline_hooks(hooks):\\n1004:             return None\\n1005: \\n1006:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\\n1007:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\\n1008:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\\n1009: \\n1010:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\\n1011:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n1012:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\\\",\\n1013:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\\n1014:         )\\n1015:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n1016:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\\\",\\n1017:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\\n1018:
