[pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\\n1022: \\n1023:     def synthetic_graph_input(\\n1024:         self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\\n1025:     ) -> VariableTracker:\\n1026:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1027:         call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\\n1028:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1029:         example_value = fn(*args)\\n1030:         varname = self.new_var()\\n1031:         cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\\n1032:         cg.add_push_null(\\n1033:             lambda: cg.load_import_from(\\n1034:                 fn.__module__,\\n1035:                 fn.__name__,\\n1036:             )\\n1037:         )\\n1038:         cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\\n1039:         cg.call_function(len(args), False)\\n1040:         cg.store(varname)\\n1041:         self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\\n1042:         source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\\n1043:         result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\\n1044:         # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\\n1045:         result = result.realize()\\n1046:         TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\\n1047:             source\\n1048:         )\\n1049:         return result\\n1050: \\n1051:     def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\\n1052:         self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\\n1053: \\n1054:     def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\\n1055:         for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\\n1056:             hook()\\n1057:         self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\\n1058: \\n1059:     @property\\n1060:     def root_tracer(self) -> \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\":\\n1061:         return self.tracers[0]\\n1062: \\n1063:     @property\\n1064:     def current_tracer(self) -> \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\":\\n1065:         return self.tracers[-1]\\n1066: \\n1067:     def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\\n1068:         # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\\n1069:         return len(self.tracers) == 1\\n1070: \\n1071:     @property\\n1072:     def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\\n1073:         return self.current_tracer.graph\\n1074: \\n1075:     # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\\n1076:     @graph.setter\\n1077:     def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\\n1078:         self.current_tracer.graph = value\\n1079: \\n1080:     @property\\n1081:     def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\\n1082:         return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\\n1083: \\n1084:     @property\\n1085:     def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\\n1086:         return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\\n1087: \\n1088:     @property\\n1089:     def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \\\"LazyProxy\\\"]]:\\n1090:         return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\\n1091: \\n1092:     # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\\n1093:     # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\\n1094:     # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\\n1095:     # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\\n1096:     # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\\n1097: \\n1098:     def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n1099:         return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\\n1100: \\n1101:     def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\\n1102:         return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n1103: \\n1104:     def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\\n1105:         return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n1106: \\n1107:     @contextlib.contextmanager\\n1108:     def subtracer(\\n1109:         self,\\n1110:         source_target: Optional[Target],\\n1111:         prior_tracer: Optional[\\\"SubgraphTracer\\\"],\\n1112:         description: Optional[str] = None,\\n1113:     ) -> Generator[\\\"SubgraphTracer\\\", None, None]:\\n1114:         new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\\n1115:         try:\\n1116:             if prior_tracer:\\n1117:                 # Lineage MUST stay preserved\\n1118:                 assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\\n1119:             new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\\n1120:             tracer = (\\n1121:                 prior_tracer\\n1122:                 if prior_tracer\\n1123:                 else SubgraphTracer(\\n1124:                     self,\\n1125:                     parent=self.current_tracer,\\n1126:                     source_target=source_target,\\n1127:                     is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\\n1128:                     description=description,\\n1129:                 )\\n1130:             )\\n1131:             self.tracers.append(tracer)\\n1132:             yield tracer\\n1133:         finally:\\n1134:             new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\\n1135:             self.tracers.pop()\\n1136: \\n1137:     @property\\n1138:     def output(self) -> \\\"OutputGraph\\\":\\n1139:         return self\\n1140: \\n1141:     @property\\n1142:     def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\\n1143:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n1144:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\\n1145: \\n1146:     @property\\n1147:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n1148:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n1149:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n1150:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\\n1151: \\n1152:     @property\\n1153:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\\n1154:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\\n1155: \\n1156:     @property\\n1157:     def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n1158:         return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\\n1159: \\n1160:     @property\\n1161:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\\n1162:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\\n1163: \\n1164:     def save_global_state(\\n1165:         self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\\n1166:     ) -> None:\\n1167:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1168:         Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\\n1169:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1170:         global_state = cast(\\n1171:             dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\\n1172:             (\\n1173:                 out\\n1174:                 if out is not None\\n1175:                 else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\\n1176:             ),\\n1177:         )\\n1178: \\n1179:         global_state[\\\"grad_enabled\\\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\\n1180: \\n1181:         global_state[\\\"autocast_enabled\\\"] = (\\n1182:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n1183:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n1184:         )\\n1185:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_enabled\\\"] = (\\n1186:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1187:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1188:         )\\n1189:         global_state[\\\"autocast_gpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n1190:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n1191:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n1192:         )\\n1193:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n1194:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1195:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n1196:         )\\n1197:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cache_enabled\\\"] = (\\n1198:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\\n1199:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\\n1200:         )\\n1201: \\n1202:     def push_tx(self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\") -> None:\\n1203:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\\n1204: \\n1205:     def pop_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n1206:         return self._current_tx.pop()\\n1207: \\n1208:     @property\\n1209:     def current_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n1210:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\\n1211: \\n1212:     def resolve_source_value(self, source: Source) -> Any:\\n1213:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1214:         Resolve the runtime value a Source points to using root_tx's frame.\\n1215: \\n1216:         Useful to inspect the python object associated with the source during\\n1217:         debugging. Will also be used by invoke subgraph caching later on.\\n1218:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1219:         return source.get_value(\\n1220:             {\\\"G\\\": self.root_tx.f_globals, \\\"L\\\": self.root_tx.f_locals},\\n1221:             {},\\n1222:             {},  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1223:         )\\n1224: \\n1225:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\\n1226:         return count_calls(self.graph)\\n1227: \\n1228:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\\n1229:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\\n1230: \\n1231:     def has_outputs(self) -> bool:\\n1232:         return len([x for x in self.graph.nodes if x.op == \\\"output\\\"]) > 0\\n1233: \\n1234:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\\n1235:         assert keys\\n1236:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\\n1237:         for k in keys.split(\\\".\\\"):\\n1238:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\\n1239:                 obj = obj[k]\\n1240:             else:\\n1241:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\\n1242:         return obj\\n1243: \\n1244:     def new_var(self, name: str = \\\"tmp\\\") -> str:\\n1245:         existing = set(self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"])\\n1246:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\\n1247:         while True:\\n1248:             var = f\\\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\\\"\\n1249:             if var not in existing:\\n1250:                 self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (var,)\\n1251:                 return var\\n1252: \\n1253:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\\n1254:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1255:         if name not in self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n1256:             self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n1257: \\n1258:     @staticmethod\\n1259:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\\n1260:         # create a new unique name\\n1261:         name = \\\"_\\\".join(map(str, names))\\n1262:         # Strip _buffers[..]/_parameters[..]/_modules[..] names\\n1263:         name = re.sub(\\n1264:             r\\\"\\\\._(?:modules|parameters|buffers)\\\\[(['\\\\\\\"])([^'\\\\\\\"\\\\]]+)\\\\1\\\\]\\\", r\\\".\\\\2\\\", name\\n1265:         )\\n1266:         # Replace getattr(a, b) with a.b\\n1267:         name = re.sub(\\n1268:             r\\\"getattr\\\\(\\\\s*([^,]+?)\\\\s*,\\\\s*(['\\\\\\\"])([^'\\\\\\\"]+)\\\\2\\\\s*\\\\)\\\", r\\\"\\\\1.\\\\3\\\", name\\n1269:         )\\n1270:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\\n1271:         name = re.sub(r\\\"^[GL]\\\\['?(.*?)'?\\\\]$\\\", r\\\"\\\\1\\\", name)\\n1272:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\\n1273:         name = re.sub(r\\\"\\\\[(\\\\d+)\\\\]\\\", r\\\"_\\\\g<1>\\\", name)\\n1274:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\\n1275:         name = re.sub(r\\\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\\\", \\\"_\\\", name)\\n1276: \\n1277:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\\n1278:             name = \\\"sub\\\" + name\\n1279: \\n1280:         return name\\n1281: \\n1282:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\\n1283:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\\n1284:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n1285:         # Check if the module already exists, if it does, return the already\\n1286:         # added proxy. This is important for executorch tests.\\n1287:         if isinstance(attr_value, torch.nn.Module):\\n1288:             for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items():\\n1289:                 if mod is attr_value:\\n1290:                     proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", name, (), {})\\n1291:                     return proxy\\n1292: \\n1293:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\\n1294:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\\n1295:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.\\n1296:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\\n1297:         proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", attr_name, (), {})\\n1298:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\\n1299:         return proxy\\n1300: \\n1301:     def register_attr_or_module(\\n1302:         self,\\n1303:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\\n1304:         *names: Any,\\n1305:         **options: Any,\\n1306:     ) -> VariableTracker:\\n1307:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\\n1308:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\\n1309:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\\n1310:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\\n1311: \\n1312:         options = dict(options)\\n1313:         assert \\\"source\\\" in options\\n1314:         source = options[\\\"source\\\"]\\n1315:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\\n1316: \\n1317:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\\n1318:             tracer = self.current_tracer\\n1319:             if not self.is_root_tracer():\\n1320:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\\n1321:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\\n1322:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\\n1323:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\\n1324: \\n1325:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\\n1326:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\\n1327:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\\n1328:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\\n1329:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\\n1330:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\\n1331:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\\n1332: \\n1333:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1334:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n1335:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\\n1336: \\n1337:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\\n1338:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\\n1339:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1340:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\\n1341:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\\n1342: \\n1343:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \\\"guarded\\\" and not isinstance(\\n1344:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\\n1345:                 ):\\n1346:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\\n1347:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\\n1348:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\\n1349: \\n1350:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\\n1351:                     self.root_tx,\\n1352:                     tracer.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1353:                     example_value=target,\\n1354:                     **options,\\n1355:                 )\\n1356: \\n1357:                 # Track the object so to avoid duplicate registration in case of\\n1358:                 # different sources pointing to the same tensor object.\\n1359:                 vt = self.root_tx.output.side_effects.track_object_existing(target, vt)\\n1360: \\n1361:                 assert \\\"tensor_dict\\\" not in vt.as_proxy().node.meta\\n1362:                 # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n1363:                 vt.as_proxy().node.meta[\\\"tensor_dict\\\"] = _extract_tensor_dict(target)\\n1364: \\n1365:                 return vt\\n1366: \\n1367:         elif isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\\n1368:             assert isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module)\\n1369: \\n1370:             if source:\\n1371:                 install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.NN_MODULE))\\n1372: \\n1373:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1374:                     # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n1375:                     return NNModuleVariable(type(target), module_key, target, **options)\\n1376: \\n1377:             else:\\n1378:                 # This is Dynamo created graph module, e.g., graph module coming\\n1379:                 # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\\n1380:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\\n1381:                 # tracker.\\n1382:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1383:                     # pyrefly: ignore[bad-argument-type]\\n1384:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\\n1385: \\n1386:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\\n1387:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\\n1388:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\\n1389:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\\n1390:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\\n1391:             # own storage\\n1392:             # alas, this is like this for now\\n1393: \\n1394:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1395:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(\\n1396:                     self.root_tx,\\n1397:                     self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1398:                     sym_num=target,\\n1399:                     **options,\\n1400:                 )\\n1401: \\n1402:             # HACKY CODE REGION END\\n1403:         else:\\n1404: \\n1405:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1406:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\\n1407:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target\\n1408:                 return VariableTracker.build(\\n1409:                     self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1410:                     target,\\n1411:                     ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),\\n1412:                 )\\n1413: \\n1414:         for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():\\n1415:             if v is target:\\n1416:                 # it already exists\\n1417:                 return wrap_name(k)\\n1418: \\n1419:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\\n1420:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\\n1421:         self.nn_modules[name] = target\\n1422:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\\n1423: \\n1424:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\\n1425:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n1426:                 new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\\n1427:                 new_name = f\\\"{name}.{leaf_name}\\\"\\n1428:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\\n1429:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\\n1430:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\\n1431:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\\n1432:                     ] = leaf_name\\n1433: \\n1434:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\\n1435:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\\n1436:             if hasattr(target, \\\"_parameters\\\"):\\n1437:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\\n1438:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n1439:             if hasattr(target, \\\"_buffers\\\"):\\n1440:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():\\n1441:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n1442: \\n1443:         return wrap_name(name)\\n1444: \\n1445:     def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\\n1446:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\"\\n1447:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\\n1448:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\\n1449:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\\\"self\\\")\\n1450:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\\n1451:         if not stolen_list_names:\\n1452:             return [], {}\\n1453: \\n1454:         alias_insts = []\\n1455:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\\n1456: \\n1457:         queue = [\\n1458:             *tx.stack,\\n1459:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\\n1460:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\\n1461:         ]\\n1462: \\n1463:         while queue:\\n1464:             x = queue.pop()\\n1465:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\\n1466:                 assert isinstance(x.items, list)\\n1467:                 queue += x.items\\n1468:                 continue\\n1469: \\n1470:             if not (\\n1471:                 (\\n1472:                     x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\\n1473:                     or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\\n1474:                 )\\n1475:                 and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\\n1476:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\\n1477:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\\n1478:             ):\\n1479:                 continue\\n1480: \\n1481:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\\n1482:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\\n1483:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\\n1484:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\\n1485: \\n1486:         # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n1487:         visited = {}\\n1488:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\\n1489:         for arg in self.graphargs:\\n1490:             if not (\\n1491:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)\\n1492:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\\n1493:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\\n1494:             ):\\n1495:                 continue\\n1496: \\n1497:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\\n1498:             list_name = arg.source.local_name\\n1499:             assert list_name in self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]\\n1500:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\\n1501:                 # Skip if already handled.\\n1502:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\\n1503:                     continue\\n1504: \\n1505:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\\n1506:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\\n1507:                 assert x.source is not None\\n1508:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1509:                 if list_idx not in visited:\\n1510:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\\n1511:                         f\\\"{list_name}_ref\\\"\\n1512:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\\n1513: \\n1514:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\\n1515:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\\n1516:                     alias_insts.extend(\\n1517:                         [\\n1518:                             create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=list_name),\\n1519:                             create_load_const(list_idx),\\n1520:                             create_binary_subscr(),\\n1521:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=alias_name),\\n1522:                         ]\\n1523:                     )\\n1524: \\n1525:                 # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\\n1526:                 assert x.source is not None\\n1527:                 old_source = x.source\\n1528:                 overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\\n1529: \\n1530:         # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\\n1531:         # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\\n1532:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\\n1533:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\\n1534:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\\n1535: \\n1536:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1537:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", stack_pops: int\\n1538:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1539:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1540:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\\n1541: \\n1542:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\\n1543: \\n1544:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\\n1545:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\\n1546:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\\n1547: \\n1548:         Returns:\\n1549:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\\n1550:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\\n1551:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\\n1552:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1553:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\\n1554: \\n1555:         stack_values = []\\n1556:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\\n1557: \\n1558:         def ctx_exit_check(var: VariableTracker) -> None:\\n1559:             if type.__instancecheck__(variables.WithExitFunctionVariable, var):\\n1560:                 raise AssertionError(\\n1561:                     \\\"Attempted to reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable outside the stack\\\"\\n1562:                 )\\n1563: \\n1564:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\\n1565:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\\n1566:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\\n1567:             # Allow lazy constants through for values being returned (top of stack)\\n1568:             allow_lazy_constant = len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops\\n1569:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(\\n1570:                 value, allow_lazy_constant=allow_lazy_constant\\n1571:             )\\n1572:             # Do not allow non-stack WithExitFunctionVariable reconstructions\\n1573:             if not isinstance(value, variables.WithExitFunctionVariable):\\n1574:                 VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, value)\\n1575:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\\n1576:             if allow_lazy_constant:\\n1577:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1578:                 continue\\n1579:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\\n1580:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\\n1581:             else:\\n1582:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1583:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1584:                 target_values = (\\n1585:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\\n1586:                 )\\n1587:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\\n1588:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\\n1589:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\\n1590: \\n1591:         meta.num_stack = len(stack_values)\\n1592: \\n1593:         cell_and_freevars = set(tx.cellvars() + tx.freevars())\\n1594: \\n1595:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\\n1596:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\\n1597:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\\n1598:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\\n1599:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\\n1600:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\\n1601:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\\n1602:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\\n1603:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\\n1604:             # Do not reconstruct WithExitFunctionVariable!\\n1605:             VariableTracker.visit(ctx_exit_check, v)\\n1606:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\\n1607:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\\n1608:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\\n1609:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\\n1610:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\\n1611:             # Do not include top-frame unmodified locals here - otherwise, the compiled graph may\\n1612:             # erroneously include them as part of the return. We manually codegen them afterward.\\n1613:             if (\\n1614:                 isinstance(v.source, LocalSource)\\n1615:                 and v.source.local_name == k\\n1616:                 and tx is self.root_tx\\n1617:             ):\\n1618:                 continue\\n1619:             # Do not load cell/free vars\\n1620:             if k in cell_and_freevars:\\n1621:                 continue\\n1622:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\\n1623:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n1624:                 # NOTE: do not use isinstance, since it realizes lazy VT's\\n1625:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\\n1626:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\\n1627:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\\n1628:                     continue\\n1629:             else:\\n1630:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\\n1631:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\\n1632:             meta.locals_names[k] = len(meta.locals_names)\\n1633:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1634:                 target_values = (\\n1635:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\\n1636:                 )\\n1637:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\\n1638:             stack_values.append(v)\\n1639: \\n1640:         return stack_values, meta\\n1641: \\n1642:     def compile_subgraph(\\n1643:         self,\\n1644:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1645:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\\n1646:         stack_pops: int = 0,\\n1647:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1648:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1649:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\\n1650:             - Call the compiled subgraph\\n1651:             - Apply side effects\\n1652:             - Codegen stack and locals\\n1653:             - Store the locals\\n1654: \\n1655:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\\n1656:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\\n1657:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\\n1658:         any dropped NULLs.\\n1659: \\n1660:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\\n1661:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1662: \\n1663:         assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1664: \\n1665:         if not config.nested_graph_breaks:\\n1666:             # expect to only compile 1 frame\\n1667:             assert self.root_tx is tx\\n1668: \\n1669:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\\n1670:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\\n1671: \\n1672:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\\n1673:         self.should_exit = True\\n1674: \\n1675:         log.debug(\\\"COMPILING GRAPH due to %s\\\", reason)\\n1676: \\n1677:         # prefix instructions (Python 3.11+)\\n1678:         prefix_insts: list[Instruction] = []\\n1679:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n1680:             for inst in self.root_tx.prefix_insts:\\n1681:                 if inst.opname == \\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\":\\n1682:                     prefix_insts.append(\\n1683:                         create_instruction(\\n1684:                             \\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\",\\n1685:                             arg=len(self.root_tx.code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"]),\\n1686:                         )\\n1687:                     )\\n1688:                 else:\\n1689:                     prefix_insts.append(copy.copy(inst))\\n1690: \\n1691:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\\n1692:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame (1),\\n1693:         # first element corresponds to current frame (N)\\n1694:         all_stack_values = []\\n1695:         all_stack_locals_metas = []\\n1696:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\\n1697:         while cur_tx is not None:\\n1698:             # this should have been checked by the caller\\n1699:             assert all(block.can_restore() for block in cur_tx.block_stack)\\n1700: \\n1701:             stack_values, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1702:                 cur_tx, stack_pops if cur_tx is tx else 0\\n1703:             )\\n1704:             all_stack_values.append(stack_values)\\n1705:             all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)\\n1706: \\n1707:             # Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\\n1708:             for block in reversed(cur_tx.block_stack):\\n1709:                 block.exit(cur_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\\n1710: \\n1711:             cur_tx = cur_tx.parent\\n1712: \\n1713:         # \\\"Garbage collect the heap\\\".\\n1714:         self.side_effects.prune_dead_object_new(tx)\\n1715: \\n1716:         self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)\\n1717: \\n1718:         if self._emit_debugger_breakpoint:\\n1719:             from .bytecode_transformation import create_breakpoint\\n1720: \\n1721:             self.add_output_instructions(create_breakpoint())\\n1722:             self._emit_debugger_breakpoint = False\\n1723: \\n1724:         assert not (self.pregraph_bytecode and self.export), (\\n1725:             \\\"export does not support pregraph_bytecode\\\"\\n1726:         )\\n1727:         self.add_output_instructions(self.pregraph_bytecode)\\n1728: \\n1729:         alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\\n1730:             self.root_tx\\n1731:         )\\n1732:         self.add_output_instructions(alias_insts)\\n1733: \\n1734:         self.cleanup_graph()\\n1735: \\n1736:         # Use nn.Module \\\"proxies\\\" in the constructed GraphModule so that\\n1737:         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.\\n1738:         # This prevents a strong ref cycle where Dynamo created code holds on to references\\n1739:         # to modules that also have Dynamo code cache invalidation checks.\\n1740:         # When cache invalidation runs, the generated GM will be invalidated, which also deletes\\n1741:         # the proxies.\\n1742:         nn_modules_proxies = {\\n1743:             name: nn_module_proxy(mod) for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items()\\n1744:         }\\n1745:         root = FakeRootModule(nn_modules_proxies)\\n1746: \\n1747:         from .decorators import disable\\n1748: \\n1749:         # to handle random calls\\n1750:         if len(self.random_calls) > 0:\\n1751:             random_calls_instructions = []\\n1752:             self.random_values_var = self.new_var(\\\"random_values\\\")\\n1753:             rand_fn = disable(\\n1754:                 _get_gen_rand_values_fn(self.random_calls),\\n1755:                 reason=\\\"do not trace into Dynamo rng recovery function\\\",\\n1756:             )\\n1757:             rand_fn_name = self.install_global(\\\"__gen_rand_values\\\", rand_fn)\\n1758:             codegen = PyCodegen(\\n1759:                 self.root_tx, root, overridden_sources=overridden_sources\\n1760:             )\\n1761:             random_calls_instructions.extend(\\n1762:                 codegen.load_function_name(rand_fn_name, True)\\n1763:             )\\n1764:             random_calls_instructions.extend(create_call_function(0, False))\\n1765:             random_calls_instructions.append(\\n1766:                 codegen.create_store(self.random_values_var),\\n1767:             )\\n1768:             self.add_output_instructions(random_calls_instructions)\\n1769: \\n1770:         # Codegen stack convention before the unsupported instruction\\n1771:         # NOTE: in these comment blocks, \\\"locals\\\" EXCLUDE free and cell vars.\\n1772:         # NOTE: stack/locals/cells must be codegen'd BEFORE the unsupported instruction, since the latter\\n1773:         # can arbitrarily mutate the former.\\n1774:         # [frame N cells, .., frame 1 cells],\\n1775:         # [\\n1776:         #   frame N locals,\\n1777:         #   frame N-1 stack + locals,\\n1778:         #   ...,\\n1779:         #   frame 1 stack + locals,\\n1780:         # ], frame N stack\\n1781: \\n1782:         # see symbolic_convert.py for\\n1783:         # codegen stack convention after the unsupported instruction\\n1784:         # NOTE: cells will be loaded into continuation functions directly by symbolic_convert\\n1785: \\n1786:         # this determines the order that values are codegen'd to the stack\\n1787:         stack_values_flat = [val for vals in all_stack_values for val in vals]\\n1788:         stored_graph_output_var = False\\n1789:         graph_output_var = None\\n1790: \\n1791:         # call compiled fx graph and codegen all values - stack and locals\\n1792:         if (\\n1793:             self.root_tx is tx  # single frame\\n1794:             and stack_values_flat\\n1795:             and all(\\n1796:                 not isinstance(\\n1797:                     v,\\n1798:                     (\\n1799:                         UnspecializedPythonVariable,\\n1800:                         NumpyNdarrayVariable,\\n1801:                         TensorWithTFOverrideVariable,\\n1802:                     ),\\n1803:                 )\\n1804:                 and not (isinstance(v, SymNodeVariable) and v.python_type() is float)\\n1805:                 for v in stack_values_flat\\n1806:             )\\n1807:             and all(x.is_tensor() for x in stack_values_flat)\\n1808:             and len(set(stack_values_flat)) == len(stack_values_flat)\\n1809:             and self.side_effects.is_empty()\\n1810:             and not tx.debug_locals\\n1811:             and not self.backward_state\\n1812:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].stack_null_idxes\\n1813:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].locals_null_keys\\n1814:         ):\\n1815:             # optimization to generate better code in a common case\\n1816: \\n1817:             # codegen cells\\n1818:             # no side effects, so no new cells created - no need to call side_effects.codegen_save_tempvars\\n1819:             cell_cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\\n1820:             self.codegen_cells(tx, cell_cg)\\n1821:             self.add_output_instructions(\\n1822:                 [\\n1823:                     # load in reverse since UNPACK_SEQUENCE will reverse\\n1824:                     *self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(\\n1825:                         tx, list(reversed(stack_values_flat)), root\\n1826:                     ),\\n1827:                     *cell_cg.get_instructions(),\\n1828:                     *create_swap(2),\\n1829:                     create_instruction(\\\"UNPACK_SEQUENCE\\\", arg=len(stack_values_flat)),\\n1830:                 ]\\n1831:             )\\n1832:             # function output will be moved to the correct places below\\n1833:         else:\\n1834:             graph_output_var = self.new_var(\\\"graph_out\\\")\\n1835:             # load stack values in a flat manner - we will codegen bytecode to place them correctly\\n1836:             # according to our convention above\\n1837:             pass1 = PyCodegen(\\n1838:                 self.root_tx,\\n1839:                 root,\\n1840:                 graph_output_var,\\n1841:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\\n1842:             )\\n1843:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass1, False)\\n1844: \\n1845:             # Use `pass1.uses` to selectively cache multi-user variables into a\\n1846:             # temporary local source. This (a). speeds up loading VTs with long\\n1847:             # chained source, and (b). avoids redundantly saving single-user VT\\n1848:             # into a temporary local.\\n1849:             tempvars = {}  # type: ignore[var-annotated]\\n1850:             for val, count in pass1.uses.items():\\n1851:                 # If it's already a local source, no need to cache it\\n1852:                 if count > 1 and not istype(val, (SyntheticLocalSource, LocalSource)):\\n1853:                     tempvars[val] = None\\n1854:             pass2 = PyCodegen(\\n1855:                 self.root_tx,\\n1856:                 root,\\n1857:                 graph_output_var,\\n1858:                 tempvars=tempvars,\\n1859:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\\n1860:             )\\n1861:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass2, True)\\n1862: \\n1863:             if (\\n1864:                 torch._dynamo.config.log_graph_in_out_metadata\\n1865:                 and stack_values_flat\\n1866:                 and len(stack_values_flat) == 1\\n1867:             ):\\n1868:                 vt = stack_values_flat[0]\\n1869:                 if (\\n1870:                     isinstance(vt, torch._dynamo.variables.NamedTupleVariable)\\n1871:                     and vt.tuple_cls\\n1872:                     is torch._dynamo.functional_export.ExportTracerOutput\\n1873:                 ):\\n1874:                     flat_returns = vt.items[0]\\n1875:                     out_spec = vt.items[1]\\n1876:                     assert isinstance(\\n1877:                         flat_returns, torch._dynamo.variables.ListVariable\\n1878:                     )\\n1879: \\n1880:                     vt_to_graph_out_idx: dict[VariableTracker, int] = {}\\n1881:                     for value in pass2.graph_outputs.values():\\n1882:                         assert isinstance(value, torch._dynamo.codegen.GraphOutputEntry)\\n1883:                         variable: VariableTracker = value.variable\\n1884:                         vt_to_graph_out_idx[variable] = value.index\\n1885: \\n1886:                     for idx, vt in enumerate(flat_returns.items):\\n1887:                         if vt in vt_to_graph_out_idx:\\n1888:                             self.export_metadata.output_return_type[idx] = (\\n1889:                                 \\\"graph_out\\\",\\n1890:                                 vt_to_graph_out_idx[vt],\\n1891:                             )\\n1892:                         elif (\\n1893:                             vt.source is not None\\n1894:                             and (source := getattr(vt.source, \\\"base\\\", None))  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1895:                             and source.is_input\\n1896:                         ):\\n1897:                             self.export_metadata.output_return_type[idx] = (\\n1898:                                 \\\"input\\\",\\n1899:                                 vt.source,\\n1900:                             )\\n1901:                         elif vt.is_python_constant():\\n1902:                             self.export_metadata.output_return_type[idx] = (\\n1903:                                 \\\"constant\\\",\\n1904:                                 vt.as_python_constant(),\\n1905:                             )\\n1906:                         else:\\n1907:                             raise AssertionError(\\n1908:                                 f\\\"Encountered unrecognized type {vt} at output {idx}\\\"\\n1909:                             )\\n1910:                     try:\\n1911:                         self.export_metadata.out_spec = out_spec.as_python_constant()\\n1912:                     except ClosureConversionError as e:\\n1913:                         unimplemented(\\n1914:                             gb_type=\\\"nested function with non-constructible closure in output\\\",\\n1915:                             context=f\\\"as_python_constant for out_spec {out_spec}\\\",\\n1916:                             explanation=(\\n1917:                                 \\\"Cannot return a nested function with closure from a compiled function. \\\"\\n1918:                                 \\\"Dynamo failed to construct the function defined in the compiled region with closure objects.\\\"\\n1919:                             ),\\n1920:                             hints=[\\n1921:                                 \\\"Define the function at module scope instead of inside another function \\\",\\n1922:                                 \\\"Ensure that all closure variables are constants.\\\",\\n1923:                             ],\\n1924:                             from_exc=e,\\n1925:                         )\\n1926: \\n1927:             output = []\\n1928:             if count_calls(self.graph) != 0 or len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\\n1929:                 output.extend(\\n1930:                     self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(tx, pass2.graph_output_vars(), root)\\n1931:                 )\\n1932: \\n1933:                 if len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\\n1934:                     output.append(pass2.create_store(graph_output_var))\\n1935:                     stored_graph_output_var = True\\n1936:                 else:\\n1937:                     output.append(create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\"))\\n1938:             else:\\n1939:                 # NB: Important to run compiler collective even when there is\\n1940:                 # a graph break\\n1941:                 self.run_compiler_collective()\\n1942:             self.add_output_instructions(output + pass2.get_instructions())\\n1943: \\n1944:         # store all stack and locals for each frame\\n1945:         # current state of the stack:\\n1946:         # all cells,\\n1947:         # *(frame N stack), *(frame N locals),\\n1948:         # ...,\\n1949:         # *(frame 1 stack), *(frame 1 locals)\\n1950: \\n1951:         self.add_output_instructions(\\n1952:             [\\n1953:                 create_instruction(\\n1954:                     \\\"BUILD_LIST\\\",\\n1955:                     arg=len(stack_values_flat) - all_stack_locals_metas[0].num_stack,\\n1956:                 ),\\n1957:             ]\\n1958:         )\\n1959: \\n1960:         # current state of the stack:\\n1961:         # all cells,\\n1962:         # *(frame N stack), [\\n1963:         #     *(frame N locals),\\n1964:         #     *(frame N-1 stack), *(frame N-1 locals),\\n1965:         #     ...\\n1966:         #     *(frame 1 stack), *(frame 1 locals),\\n1967:         # ]\\n1968:         # iterate current frame (N) to root frame (1)\\n1969:         # sliding window over frame stack/locals\\n1970:         start_idx = 0\\n1971:         end_idx = 0\\n1972:         for i, meta in enumerate(all_stack_locals_metas):\\n1973:             # do not pack frame N's stack into the value list\\n1974:             n_vals = len(meta.locals_names)\\n1975:             if i != 0:\\n1976:                 n_vals += meta.num_stack\\n1977:             if n_vals == 0:\\n1978:                 self.add_output_instructions(\\n1979:                     [\\n1980:                         create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=0),\\n1981:                         *create_swap(2),\\n1982:                     ]\\n1983:                 )\\n1984:                 # [], stack_values_flat\\n1985:             else:\\n1986:                 end_idx += n_vals\\n1987:                 self.add_output_instructions(\\n1988:                     [\\n1989:                         create_dup_top(),\\n1990:                         *create_binary_slice(start_idx, end_idx),\\n1991:                         *create_swap(2),\\n1992:                     ]\\n1993:                 )\\n1994:                 start_idx += n_vals\\n1995:                 # stack_values_flat[x:y], stack_values_flat\\n1996: \\n1997:             # add root frame's unmodified locals here\\n1998:             if i == len(all_stack_locals_metas) - 1:\\n1999:                 root_cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\\n2000:                 unmodified_locals_names: dict[str, int] = {}\\n2001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1000-2000\"}"

"{\"content\":\"3000:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3001:         Installs a global, generating a unique name for it.\\n3002: \\n3003:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\\n3004:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3005:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\\n3006:         name = unique_id(prefix)\\n3007:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n3008:         return name\\n3009: \\n3010:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n3011:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n3012:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n3013:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n3014:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n3015:         self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.clear()\\n3016:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n3017: \\n3018:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n3019:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n3020:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n3021:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n3022:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n3023:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n3024:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n3025:         self.mro_source_cache.clear()\\n3026:         self.guarded_mro_absent_keys.clear()\\n3027:         self.signature_cache.clear()\\n3028:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n3029:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n3030:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n3031:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n3032:         self.leaf_var_creation_order.clear()\\n3033:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n3034:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n3035: \\n3036:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n3037:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n3038:     ) -> None:\\n3039:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n3040: \\n3041:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n3042:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3043:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n3044:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n3045:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n3046:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n3047: \\n3048:     def add_fqn_info_for_inlined_modules(\\n3049:         self, inlined_module: torch.nn.Module, source: Source\\n3050:     ) -> None:\\n3051:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(source.name)\\n3052:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(\\n3053:             name, self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names, self.global_scope\\n3054:         )\\n3055:         self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.add(name)\\n3056: \\n3057:         def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\\n3058:             assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n3059:             new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\\n3060:             new_name = f\\\"{name}.{leaf_name}\\\"\\n3061:             self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\\n3062:             if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\\n3063:                 self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\\n3064:                     OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\\n3065:                 ] = leaf_name\\n3066: \\n3067:         # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\\n3068:         # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\\n3069:         if hasattr(inlined_module, \\\"_parameters\\\"):\\n3070:             if (\\n3071:                 callable(inlined_module.named_parameters)\\n3072:                 and inlined_module.named_parameters.__func__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n3073:                 is og_module_named_parameters_fn_ptr\\n3074:             ):\\n3075:                 for leaf_name, _ in inlined_module.named_parameters():\\n3076:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n3077:         if hasattr(inlined_module, \\\"_buffers\\\"):\\n3078:             if (\\n3079:                 callable(inlined_module.named_buffers)\\n3080:                 and inlined_module.named_buffers.__func__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n3081:                 is og_module_named_buffers_fn_ptr\\n3082:             ):\\n3083:                 for leaf_name, _ in inlined_module.named_buffers():\\n3084:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n3085: \\n3086: \\n3087: class DynamoTracerOutput:\\n3088:     error_on_graph_break: bool\\n3089:     is_tracing_resume_prologue: bool\\n3090:     output_graph: Optional[OutputGraph]\\n3091:     # output_graph_for_cleanup is set even when there's an error, to allow\\n3092:     # cleanup of graph nodes to break reference cycles\\n3093:     output_graph_for_cleanup: Optional[OutputGraph]\\n3094:     closure: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n3095:     f_globals: dict[str, Any]\\n3096: \\n3097:     def __init__(\\n3098:         self, tracer: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", error: Optional[Any] = None\\n3099:     ) -> None:\\n3100:         self.error_on_graph_break = tracer.error_on_graph_break\\n3101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL3000-3100\"}"

62. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fetch_file.py << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\n# Since we have sg_read_file limit, let's fetch in chunks\nchunks = [\n    (1, 1000),\n    (1000, 2000),\n    (2000, 3000),\n    (3000, 3100),\n    (3100, 4219),  # End of file at 4219 lines\n]\n\n# We'll need to use the sourcegraph API or download the file directly\n# Let's try to download from GitHub raw content\n\nimport urllib.request\nimport ssl\n\n# Create SSL context that doesn't verify certificates (for testing only)\nssl_context = ssl.create_default_context()\nssl_context.check_hostname = False\nssl_context.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE\n\nurl = \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pytorch/pytorch/HEAD/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py\"\ntry:\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(url, context=ssl_context) as response:\n        content = response.read().decode('utf-8')\n        with open('/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py', 'w') as f:\n            f.write(content)\n        print(f\"Downloaded {len(content)} bytes\")\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"Error: {e}\")\nEOF\npython /tmp/fetch_file.py\n"
}
Output
"Error: Exit code 127\n/bin/bash: line 70: python: command not found\n\n/bin/bash: line 70: python: command not found"

type: ignore[assignment]\n  3014\u2192        self.nn_modules.clear()\n  3015\u2192        self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.clear()\n  3016\u2192        self.param_name_to_source = None\n  3017\u2192\n  3018\u2192        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n  3019\u2192            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n  3020\u2192                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n  3021\u2192        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n  3022\u2192        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n  3023\u2192        self.side_effects.clear()\n  3024\u2192        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n  3025\u2192        self.mro_source_cache.clear()\n  3026\u2192        self.guarded_mro_absent_keys.clear()\n  3027\u2192        self.signature_cache.clear()\n  3028\u2192        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n  3029\u2192        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n  3030\u2192        self.tracing_context.clear()\n  3031\u2192        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n  3032\u2192        self.leaf_var_creation_order.clear()\n  3033\u2192        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n  3034\u2192        self.backward_state.clear()\n  3035\u2192\n  3036\u2192    def add_graph_finalizer(\n  3037\u2192        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n  3038\u2192    ) -> None:\n  3039\u2192        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n  3040\u2192\n  3041\u2192    def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n  3042\u2192        \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n  3043\u2192        if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n  3044\u2192            return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n  3045\u2192        assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n  3046\u2192        return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n  3047\u2192\n  3048\u2192    def add_fqn_info_for_inlined_modules(\n  3049\u2192        self, inlined_module: torch.nn.Module, source: Source\n  3050\u2192    ) -> None:\n  3051\u2192        name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(source.name)\n  3052\u2192        name = get_unique_name_wrt(\n  3053\u2192            name, self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names, self.global_scope\n  3054\u2192        )\n  3055\u2192        self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.add(name)\n  3056\u2192\n  3057\u2192        def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\n  3058\u2192            assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n  3059\u2192            new_source = self.get_chained_param_buffer_source(source, leaf_name)\n  3060\u2192            new_name = f\"{name}.{leaf_name}\"\n  3061\u2192            self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\n  3062\u2192            if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\n  3063\u2192                self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\n  3064\u2192                    OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name)\n  3065\u2192                ] = leaf_name\n  3066\u2192\n  3067\u2192        # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\n  3068\u2192        # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\n  3069\u2192        if hasattr(inlined_module, \"_parameters\"):\n  3070\u2192            if (\n  3071\u2192                callable(inlined_module.named_parameters)\n  3072\u2192                and inlined_module.named_parameters.__func__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n  3073\u2192                is og_module_named_parameters_fn_ptr\n  3074\u2192            ):\n  3075\u2192                for leaf_name, _ in inlined_module.named_parameters():\n  3076\u2192                    register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n  3077\u2192        if hasattr(inlined_module, \"_buffers\"):\n  3078\u2192            if (\n  3079\u2192                callable(inlined_module.named_buffers)\n  3080\u2192                and inlined_module.named_buffers.__func__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n  3081\u2192                is og_module_named_buffers_fn_ptr\n  3082\u2192            ):\n  3083\u2192                for leaf_name, _ in inlined_module.named_buffers():\n  3084\u2192                    register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n  3085\u2192\n  3086\u2192\n  3087\u2192class DynamoTracerOutput:\n  3088\u2192    error_on_graph_break: bool\n  3089\u2192    is_tracing_resume_prologue: bool\n  3090\u2192    output_graph: Optional[OutputGraph]\n  3091\u2192    # output_graph_for_cleanup is set even when there's an error, to allow\n  3092\u2192    # cleanup of graph nodes to break reference cycles\n  3093\u2192    output_graph_for_cleanup: Optional[OutputGraph]\n  3094\u2192    closure: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\n  3095\u2192    f_globals: dict[str, Any]\n  3096\u2192\n  3097\u2192    def __init__(\n  3098\u2192        self, tracer: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", error: Optional[Any] = None\n  3099\u2192    ) -> None:\n  3100\u2192        self.error_on_graph_break = tracer.error_on_graph_break\n  3101\u2192        self.is_tracing_resume_prologue = tracer.is_tracing_resume_prologue\n  3102\u2192        self.closure = tracer.closure\n  3103\u2192        self.f_globals = tracer.f_globals\n  3104\u2192        self.output_graph_for_cleanup = tracer.output\n  3105\u2192        if error:\n  3106\u2192            self.output_graph = None\n  3107\u2192        else:\n  3108\u2192            self.output_graph = tracer.output\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 66. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "new_string": "        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)",
  "old_string": "        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
"The file /workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py has been updated successfully."

"  3010\u2192    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n  3011\u2192        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n  3012\u2192        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n  3013\u2192        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n  3014\u2192        self.nn_modules.clear()\n  3015\u2192        self.used_inlined_inbuilt_modules_names.clear()\n  3016\u2192        self.param_name_to_source = None\n  3017\u2192\n  3018\u2192        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n  3019\u2192            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n  3020\u2192                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n  3021\u2192        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n  3022\u2192        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n  3023\u2192        self.side_effects.clear()\n  3024\u2192        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n  3025\u2192        self.mro_source_cache.clear()\n  3026\u2192        self.guarded_mro_absent_keys.clear()\n  3027\u2192        self.signature_cache.clear()\n  3028\u2192        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n  3029\u2192        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n  3030\u2192        self.tracing_context.clear()\n  3031\u2192        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n  3032\u2192        self.leaf_var_creation_order.clear()\n  3033\u2192        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n  3034\u2192        self.backward_state.clear()\n  3035\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 73. Bash @ -

{
  "command": "sed -n '3030,3065p' /workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py"

Output
"        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.leaf_var_creation_order.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n\n    def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n        if node.op == \"placeholder\":"

{
  "description": "Modify convert_frame.py to call cleanup_on_exception on tracer failures",
  "prompt": "I need to modify torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py to add cleanup_on_exception calls when the tracer fails.\n\nThe key locations are:\n1. In the _run_transform function around line 833 (in the except Exception block where tracer.run() exceptions are caught)\n2. Also potentially in other exception handlers in the same function\n\nThe changes should:\n1. Call tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception() when an exception occurs during tracing\n2. Do this before re-raising the exception\n3. Maintain the existing exception handling logic\n\nPlease read the convert_frame.py file from sourcegraph and make these modifications, then write the file to /workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py.\n\nThe code to add should be right after the except Exception: line at line 833, something like:\n```python\nexcept Exception:\n    tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()\n    if translation_validation_enabled():\n        bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n    raise\n```\n",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}

The changes add `tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()` calls to three exception handlers:\n\n#### 1. **UnspecializeRestartAnalysis Exception Handler (Lines 845-848)**\n```python\nexcept exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\n    tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()  # ADDED\n    speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n    raise\n```\n\n#### 2. **Restart Analysis Exception Handler (Lines 849-855)**\n```python\nexcept (\n    exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n    exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n    exc.SkipFrame,\n):\n    tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()  # ADDED\n    raise\n```\n\n#### 3. **General Exception Handler (Lines 856-860)**\n```python\nexcept Exception:\n    tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()  # ADDED\n    if translation_validation_enabled():\n        bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n    raise\n```\n\n### Key Points\n\n- **Location**: All three modifications are in the `run_tracer()` nested function within the `trace_frame` function\n- **Timing**: Each `cleanup_on_exception()` call is placed immediately after the `except` clause, before any other logic\n- **Preservation of Logic**: The existing exception handling logic is fully maintained - `speculation_log.clear()` still happens before re-raising, and `translation_validation_enabled()` bisect logic is preserved\n- **File Validity**: The modified file passes Python syntax validation\n\n### File Details\n\n- **File Path**: `/workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py`\n- **Total Lines**: 2350\n- **Lines Modified**: 3 exception handlers (added 1 line each)\n- **Status**: Valid Python code, ready for use",

type: ignore[has-type]\\n819:     exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\\n820:     tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n821:         instructions,\\n822:         code,\\n823:         locals,\\n824:         globals,\\n825:         builtins,\\n826:         closure,\\n827:         tf_mode_stack,\\n828:         code_options,\\n829:         compiler_fn,\\n830:         one_graph,\\n831:         export,\\n832:         export_constraints,\\n833:         frame_state=frame_state,\\n834:         speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n835:         exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n836:         distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n837:         package=package,\\n838:     )\\n839: \\n840:     def run_tracer() -> None:\\n841:         try:\\n842:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n843:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n844:                 tracer.run()\\n845:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n846:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n847:             raise\\n848:         except (\\n849:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n850:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n851:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n852:         ):\\n853:             raise\\n854:         except Exception:\\n855:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n856:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n857:             raise\\n858:         finally:\\n859:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n860:             tracer.f_locals = {}\\n861: \\n862:     try:\\n863:         run_tracer()\\n864:         tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer)\\n865:         output = tracer_output.output_graph\\n866:         assert output is not None\\n867:         assert output.output_instructions\\n868:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n869:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n870:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n871:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n872:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n873:     except Exception as e:\\n874:         e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer, error=True)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n875:         raise\\n876:     return tracer_output\\n877: \\n878: \\n879: @dataclass\\n880: class DynamoOutput:\\n881:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n882:     Represents the core data returned from a single dynamo run, including:\\n883:       - Guards, wrapped inside tracer_output.output_graph.guards\\n884:       - Generated bytecode\\n885:       - Other information needed for compilation.\\n886:     This data structure should capture all the \\\"interesting\\\" information dynamo\\n887:     produces on the frontend side before it enters user backend.\\n888:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n889: \\n890:     tracer_output: DynamoTracerOutput\\n891:     bytecode: types.CodeType\\n892:     last_attempt_start_time: Optional[float]\\n893: \\n894:     def build_guards(\\n895:         self,\\n896:         code: types.CodeType,\\n897:         hooks: Optional[Hooks] = None,\\n898:         save: bool = False,\\n899:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry] = None,\\n900:         strict_error: bool = False,\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL800-900\"}"

Please refer\\n786:     to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.\\n787:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n788:     handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)\\n789:     _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook\\n790:     return handle\\n791: \\n792: \\n793: # TODO - We want to run preserve_node_meta context manager here, but the CI\\n794: # fails (its unclear if the failures were flaky)\\n795: # @torch.fx.traceback.preserve_node_meta()\\n796: @preserve_global_state\\n797: def trace_frame(\\n798:     code: types.CodeType,\\n799:     globals: dict[str, object],\\n800:     locals: dict[str, object],\\n801:     builtins: dict[str, object],\\n802:     closure: tuple[CellType],\\n803:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n804:     tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n805:     one_graph: bool,\\n806:     speculation_log: SpeculationLog,\\n807:     instructions: list[Instruction],\\n808:     code_options: dict[str, object],\\n809:     *,\\n810:     export: bool = False,\\n811:     export_constraints: Any | None = None,\\n812:     frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\\n813:     distributed_state: Optional[DistributedState] = None,\\n814:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n815: ) -> DynamoTracerOutput:\\n816:     from torch.fx.experimental.validator import bisect, translation_validation_enabled\\n817: \\n818:     speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n819:     exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\\n820:     tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n821: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL780-820\"}"

"{\"content\":\"840:     def run_tracer() -> None:\\n841:         try:\\n842:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n843:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n844:                 tracer.run()\\n845:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n846:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n847:             raise\\n848:         except (\\n849:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n850:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n851:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n852:         ):\\n853:             raise\\n854:         except Exception:\\n855:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n856:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n857:             raise\\n858:         finally:\\n859:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n860:             tracer.f_locals = {}\\n861: \\n862:     try:\\n863:         run_tracer()\\n864:         tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer)\\n865:         output = tracer_output.output_graph\\n866:         assert output is not None\\n867:         assert output.output_instructions\\n868:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n869:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n870:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n871:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n872:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n873:     except Exception as e:\\n874:         e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer, error=True)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n875:         raise\\n876:     return tracer_output\\n877: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL840-876\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\\n3: frames into FX graphs. It handles:\\n4: \\n5: - Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\\n6: - Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\\n7: - Cache management for recompilation\\n8: - Error handling and fallback mechanisms\\n9: \\n10: Key classes:\\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\\n15: \\n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\\n18: \\n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\\n22: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n23: \\n24: from __future__ import annotations\\n25: \\n26: import collections\\n27: import contextlib\\n28: import cProfile\\n29: import dataclasses\\n30: import dis\\n31: import functools\\n32: import gc\\n33: import importlib\\n34: import inspect\\n35: import itertools\\n36: import logging\\n37: import os\\n38: import pstats\\n39: import random\\n40: import subprocess\\n41: import sys\\n42: import tempfile\\n43: import threading\\n44: import time\\n45: import traceback\\n46: import types\\n47: import typing\\n48: import unittest.mock as mock\\n49: import weakref\\n50: from dataclasses import dataclass\\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1-50\"}"

It handles:\\n4: \\n5: - Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\\n6: - Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\\n7: - Cache management for recompilation\\n8: - Error handling and fallback mechanisms\\n9: \\n10: Key classes:\\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\\n15: \\n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\\n18: \\n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\\n22: .variables.torch_function import torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr\\n174: \\n175: \\n176: np: Optional[ModuleType]\\n177: try:\\n178:     import numpy as np\\n179: except ModuleNotFoundError:\\n180:
