torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"bytecode\\\")\\n197: graph_break_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \\\"graph_breaks\\\")\\n198: \\n199: \\n200: compile_lock = threading.RLock()\\n201: \\n202: _T = TypeVar(\\\"_T\\\")\\n203: _P = ParamSpec(\\\"_P\\\")\\n204: \\n205: \\n206: class TODO_UNKNOWN:\\n207:     pass\\n208: \\n209: \\n210: def _clear_fake_mode_weakrefs(\\n211:     fake_mode: Optional[torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode],\\n212: ) -> None:\\n213:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Clear WeakIdRef entries from a FakeTensorMode's describer.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n214:     if fake_mode is None:\\n215:         return\\n216:     describer = fake_mode.fake_tensor_converter.meta_converter.describer\\n217:     describer.lookup_tensor.clear()\\n218:     describer.lookup_storage.clear()\\n219: \\n220: \\n221: class Tracker:\\n222:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n223:         self.seen: list[ReferenceType[CodeType]] = []\\n224:         self.seen_ids: set[int] = set()\\n225: \\n226:     def add(self, strong_obj: CodeType) -> None:\\n227:         idx = id(strong_obj)\\n228:         if idx not in self.seen_ids:\\n229:             obj = weakref.ref(strong_obj, lambda _: self.seen_ids.remove(idx))\\n230:             self.seen.append(obj)\\n231:             self.seen_ids.add(idx)\\n232: \\n233:     def __contains__(self, item: CodeType) -> bool:\\n234:         return id(item) in self.seen_ids\\n235: \\n236:     def clear(self) -> None:\\n237:         self.seen.clear()\\n238:         self.seen_ids.clear()\\n239: \\n240: \\n241: input_codes = Tracker()\\n242: output_codes = Tracker()\\n243: \\n244: initial_global_state: Optional[GlobalStateGuard] = None\\n245: \\n246: \\n247: @functools.wraps(original_forward_from_src)\\n248: def fx_forward_from_src_skip_result(\\n249:     src: str, globals: dict[str, Any], co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None\\n250: ) -> FunctionType:\\n251:     # we monkey patch FX to prevent infinite loop of trying to convert\\n252:     # our generated code\\n253:     result = original_forward_from_src(src, globals, co_fields)\\n254:     skip_code(result.__code__)\\n255:     return result\\n256: \\n257: \\n258: def log_dynamo_start(code: CodeType, skip: int = 0) -> list[str]:\\n259:     convert_frame_intern = structured.intern_string(__file__)\\n260:     captured_tb = CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=4 + skip).summary()\\n261:     frames_interned = structured.from_traceback(captured_tb)\\n262:     # Extract and filter the stack\\n263:     stack = list(\\n264:         itertools.takewhile(\\n265:             lambda f: f[\\\"filename\\\"] != convert_frame_intern,\\n266:             frames_interned,\\n267:         )\\n268:     ) + [\\n269:         {\\n270:             \\\"line\\\": code.co_firstlineno,\\n271:             \\\"name\\\": code.co_name,\\n272:             \\\"filename\\\": structured.intern_string(code.co_filename),\\n273:         }\\n274:     ]\\n275:     # Initialize the ChromiumEventLogger on start\\n276:     torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n277:         \\\"dynamo_start\\\",\\n278:         lambda: {\\\"stack\\\": stack},\\n279:     )\\n280: \\n281:     # Capture stack separately without using from_traceback to get the actual filenames\\n282:     stack_strings = [\\n283:         f\\\"Line: {frame.lineno}, Name: {frame.name}, Filename: {frame.filename}\\\"\\n284:         for frame in captured_tb\\n285:         if frame.filename != convert_frame_intern\\n286:     ] + [\\n287:         f\\\"Line: {code.co_firstlineno}, Name: {code.co_name}, Filename: {code.co_filename}\\\"\\n288:     ]\\n289:     return stack_strings\\n290: \\n291: \\n292: def preserve_global_state(fn: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n293:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n294:     Context manager to:\\n295:         1) Save/restore torch.is_grad_enabled() state\\n296:         2) Save/restore python random state\\n297:         3) Save/restore torch random state\\n298:         4) Monkey patch torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\\n299:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n300: \\n301:     @functools.wraps(fn)\\n302:     def _fn(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\\n303:         guards = GlobalStateGuard()\\n304:         prior_grad_mode = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n305: \\n306:         # Just in case we get left in a bad dispatch state we want to restore\\n307:         # it. This can happen because the dispatch bits aren't a true\\n308:         # stack/counter - so we can't just increment/decrement them as we enter\\n309:         # and leave.\\n310:         with (\\n311:             torch._C._PreserveDispatchKeyGuard(),\\n312:             maybe_disable_inference_mode(),\\n313:             maybe_disable_inference_mode_for_fake_prop(),\\n314:         ):\\n315:             prior_inference_mode = torch.is_inference_mode_enabled()\\n316:             prior_deterministic = torch.are_deterministic_algorithms_enabled()\\n317:             prior_warn_only = torch.is_deterministic_algorithms_warn_only_enabled()\\n318:             prior_mobile_allocator_state = (\\n319:                 torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\\n320:             )\\n321:             py_rng_state = random.getstate()\\n322:             prior_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\\n323:             torch_rng_state = torch.random.get_rng_state()\\n324:             cuda_rng_state = None\\n325:             if torch.cuda.is_available():\\n326:                 with torch._C.DisableTorchFunction():\\n327:                     cuda_rng_state = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()\\n328:             cuda_matmul_fp32_prec = torch._C._get_fp32_precision_getter(\\n329:                 \\\"cuda\\\", \\\"matmul\\\"\\n330:             )\\n331:             prior_fwd_from_src = torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\\n332:             torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = fx_forward_from_src_skip_result\\n333:             cleanup = setup_compile_debug()\\n334:             exit_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n335:             exit_stack.enter_context(\\n336:                 torch.fx._symbolic_trace._maybe_revert_all_patches()\\n337:             )\\n338:             exit_stack.enter_context(torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr)\\n339:             reset_user_object_tracking()\\n340:             try:\\n341:                 return fn(*args, **kwargs)\\n342:             finally:\\n343:                 cleanup.close()\\n344:                 assert torch._C._len_torch_function_stack() == 0, (\\n345:                     \\\"Torch function mode stack state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\\\"\\n346:                 )\\n347:                 exit_stack.close()\\n348:                 torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prior_grad_mode)\\n349:                 torch.autograd.grad_mode._enter_inference_mode(prior_inference_mode)\\n350:                 torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(\\n351:                     prior_deterministic, warn_only=prior_warn_only\\n352:                 )\\n353:                 random.setstate(py_rng_state)\\n354:                 torch.random.set_rng_state(torch_rng_state)\\n355:                 torch.set_default_dtype(prior_dtype)\\n356:                 curr_mobile_allocator_state = (\\n357:                     torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\\n358:                 )\\n359:                 if prior_mobile_allocator_state != curr_mobile_allocator_state:\\n360:                     torch._C._unset_default_mobile_cpu_allocator()\\n361:                 if cuda_rng_state is not None:\\n362:                     with torch._C.DisableTorchFunction():\\n363:                         torch.cuda.set_rng_state(cuda_rng_state)\\n364:                 torch._C._set_fp32_precision_setter(\\n365:                     \\\"cuda\\\", \\\"matmul\\\", cuda_matmul_fp32_prec\\n366:                 )\\n367:                 torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = prior_fwd_from_src\\n368:                 assert guards.check(), (\\n369:                     f\\\"Global {guards.reason()}state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\\\"\\n370:                 )\\n371: \\n372:     _fn._torchdynamo_orig_backend = fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n373:     return _fn\\n374: \\n375: \\n376: @TorchPatcher.suppress_torch_distributed_warnings\\n377: def has_tensor_in_frame(frame: DynamoFrameType) -> bool:\\n378:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Check if the frame has torch.* related bits\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n379:     # Check if the function was decorated using torch._dynamo.optimize\\n380:     if frame.f_code in always_optimize_code_objects:\\n381:         return True\\n382: \\n383:     # Check if there is global import of torch.*\\n384:     for co_name in frame.f_code.co_names:\\n385:         if co_name in frame.f_globals:\\n386:             obj = frame.f_globals[co_name]\\n387:             if isinstance(obj, ModuleType) and (\\n388:                 obj.__name__.startswith(\\\"torch.\\\") or obj is torch\\n389:             ):\\n390:                 return True\\n391:             # ... or a global import of numpy.*\\n392:             if np and config.trace_numpy and (obj is np or is_numpy(obj)):\\n393:                 return True\\n394: \\n395:     seen_ids: dict[int, bool] = {}\\n396: \\n397:     def has_tensor(obj: object) -> bool:\\n398:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Recursively check if the obj has a tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n399:         obj_id = id(obj)\\n400:         if obj_id in seen_ids:\\n401:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n402:         seen_ids[obj_id] = False\\n403: \\n404:         if isinstance(obj, (torch.Tensor, torch.nn.Module)) or (\\n405:             istype(obj, type) and issubclass(obj, torch.nn.Module)\\n406:         ):\\n407:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\\n408:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n409:         elif (\\n410:             config.trace_numpy\\n411:             and np\\n412:             and (istype(obj, np.ndarray) or isinstance(obj, np.generic))\\n413:         ):\\n414:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\\n415:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n416:         elif istype(obj, (list, tuple)):\\n417:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in obj)\\n418:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n419:         elif istype(obj, dict):\\n420:             # Some packages like pytest can be updated during runtime. So, make a\\n421:             # copy of values to avoid issues like \\\"RuntimeError: dictionary\\n422:             # changed size during iteration\\\"\\n423:             values = list(obj.values())\\n424:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in values)\\n425:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n426:         elif istype(obj, (str, int, float, type(None), bool)):\\n427:             seen_ids[obj_id] = False\\n428:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n429:         elif is_namedtuple(obj) and hasattr(obj, \\\"_fields\\\"):\\n430:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(getattr(obj, v)) for v in obj._fields)\\n431:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n432:         else:\\n433:             # if config.debug:\\n434:             #     print(\\n435:             #         f\\\"Assuming that object of type {type(obj)} does not have a tensor\\\"\\n436:             #     )\\n437:             return False\\n438: \\n439:     # Check if the passed arguments are of type Tensor\\n440:     for value in frame.f_locals.values():\\n441:         if has_tensor(value):\\n442:             return True\\n443: \\n444:     log.debug(\\n445:         \\\"skipping because no torch.* %s \\\\\\n446:             %s %s\\\",\\n447:         frame.f_code.co_name,\\n448:         frame.f_code.co_filename,\\n449:         frame.f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n450:     )\\n451: \\n452:     return False\\n453: \\n454: \\n455: def exception_handler(\\n456:     e: Exception,\\n457:     code: CodeType,\\n458:     frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\\n459:     export: bool = False,\\n460: ) -> None:\\n461:     record_filename = None\\n462:     if hasattr(e, \\\"exec_record\\\"):\\n463:         record_filename = gen_record_file_name(e, code)\\n464:         write_record_to_file(record_filename, e.exec_record)\\n465:         e.record_filename = record_filename  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n466: \\n467:     augment_exc_message(e, export=export)\\n468: \\n469: \\n470: FRAME_COUNTER = 0\\n471: FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER: typing.Counter[Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]] = (\\n472:     collections.Counter()\\n473: )\\n474: \\n475: \\n476: def maybe_cprofile(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n477:     if config.cprofile:\\n478:         return cprofile_wrapper(func)\\n479:     return func\\n480: \\n481: \\n482: def cprofile_wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n483:     @functools.wraps(func)\\n484:     def profile_wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\\n485:         trace_id = CompileContext.current_trace_id()\\n486:         assert trace_id, \\\"Trace id is None\\\"\\n487:         profile_path = Path(\\n488:             os.path.join(\\n489:                 tempfile.gettempdir(),\\n490:                 f\\\"{func.__name__}_{str(trace_id).replace('/', '_')}.profile\\\",\\n491:             )\\n492:         )\\n493:         prof = cProfile.Profile()\\n494:         try:\\n495:             start_ts = time.time()\\n496:             # runcall calls prof.enable() and prof.disable(), so do NOT call\\n497:             # enable outside. This leads to issues like\\n498:             # ValueError: Another profiling tool is already active\\n499:             # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n500:             retval = prof.runcall(func, *args, **kwargs)\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1-500\"}"

skip: int = 0,\\n620:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n621:         increment_frame()\\n622:         code = frame.f_code\\n623: \\n624:         cache_size = compute_cache_size(frame, cache_entry)\\n625: error stats\\n641:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n642:         if code.co_name == \\\"__setattr__\\\":\\n643:             # setattr could be tricky to handle generally,\\n644:             # but also not likely useful to compile- skip the whole frame\\n645:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n646:         if code.co_name == \\\"__init__\\\" and code.co_filename.startswith(\\n647: ConvertFrameReturn()\\n658: \\n659:         if (\\n660:             code.co_name == \\\"<lambda>\\\"\\n661:             and code.co_filename == \\\"<string>\\\"\\n662:             and not bool(frame.f_builtins)\\n663:         ):\\n664:             # namedtuple subclass constructor. Empty builtins cause issue with\\n665:             # len keyword in LIST_LEN guard.\\n666:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n667: \\n668:         if is_generator(code):\\n669:             unimplemented(\\n670:                 gb_type=\\\"Attempt to trace generator\\\",\\n671:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n672:                 explanation=\\\"Generators cannot be compiled directly with `torch.compile`.\\\",\\n673:                 hints=[\\n674:                     \\\"Call a generator from inside of a non-generator Python function and \\\"\\n675:                     \\\"compile that function instead.\\\",\\n676:                     *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\\n677:                 ],\\n678:             )\\n679: \\n680:         if not has_tensor_in_frame(frame):\\n681:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n682: \\n683:         # Save the previous initial_global_state to handle nested compilations\\n700:         # (e.g., compiled autograd running during graph execution can trigger\\n701:         # nested compilations that would otherwise overwrite the outer state)\\n702:         prev_initial_global_state = initial_global_state\\n703:         initial_global_state = GlobalStateGuard()\\n704: \\n705:         compile_id = get_compile_id(frame_state)\\n706:         frame_id = compile_id.frame_id\\n707: \\n708:         signpost_event(\\n709:             \\\"dynamo\\\",\\n710:             \\\"_convert_frame_assert._compile\\\",\\n711:             {\\n712:                 \\\"co_name\\\": code.co_name,\\n713:                 \\\"frame_id\\\": frame_id,\\n714:                 \\\"compile_id\\\": str(compile_id),\\n715:                 \\\"co_filename\\\": code.co_filename,\\n716:                 \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": code.co_firstlineno,\\n717:                 \\\"cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\\n718:                 \\\"accumulated_cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\\n719:             },\\n720:         )\\n721: \\n722:         # tracer.run()\\n845:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n846:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n847:             raise\\n848:         except (\\n849: Exception:\\n855:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n856:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n857:             raise\\n858:         finally:\\n859:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n860:             tracer.f_locals = {}\\n861: \\n862:     try:\\n863:         run_tracer()\\n864:         tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer)\\n865:         output = tracer_output.output_graph\\n866:         assert output is not None\\n867:         assert output.output_instructions\\n868:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n869:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n870:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n871:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n872:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n873:     except Exception as e:\\n874:         e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer, error=True)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n875:         raise\\n876:     return tracer_output\\n877: \\n878: \\n879: @dataclass\\n880: class DynamoOutput:\\n881:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n882:     Represents the core data returned from a single dynamo run, including:\\n883:       - Guards, wrapped inside tracer_output.output_graph.guards\\n884:       - Generated bytecode\\n885:       - Other information needed for compilation.\\n886:     This data structure should capture all the \\\"interesting\\\" information dynamo\\n887:     produces on the frontend side before it enters user backend.\\n888: strict_error: bool = False,\\n901:     ) -> CheckFunctionManager:\\n902:         output_graph = self.tracer_output.output_graph\\n903:         assert output_graph is not None\\n904:         return CheckFunctionManager(\\n905:             code,\\n906:             output_graph,\\n907:             cache_entry,\\n908:             hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\\n909:             hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\\n910:             save_guards=save,\\n911:             strict_error=strict_error,\\n912:         )\\n913: \\n914:     def graph_capture_output(\\n915: self.tracer_output.output_graph\\n920:         assert output_graph is not None\\n921:         return GraphCaptureOutput(\\n922:             OutputGraphCommon(\\n923:                 output_graph.dump_guards_state(),\\n924:                 output_graph.import_sources,\\n925:                 output_graph.shape_env,\\n926:                 output_graph.export_metadata,\\n927: self.bytecode,\\n932:             self.tracer_output.closure,\\n933:             argdefs,\\n934:             kwdefaults,\\n935:             self.tracer_output.f_globals,\\n936:         )\\n937: \\n938: \\n939: @dataclass\\n940: class BackendInput:\\n941:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n942:     Represents core data structure that dynamo will pass to a backend, including:\\n943:       - Graph module\\n944:       - Example inputs\\n945:       - The FakeTensorMode used for compiling graph.\\n946:     This data structure should capture all the information dynamo produces\\n947:     on for the user backend.\\n948:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n949: \\n950:     backend_id: str\\n951:     graph_module: torch.fx.GraphModule\\n952:     example_inputs: Any\\n953:     fake_mode: torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode\\n954:     tensor_to_context: WeakIdKeyDictionary\\n955: \\n956: \\n957: @dataclass(frozen=True)\\n958: class GraphRuntimeEnv:\\n959:     bytecode: types.CodeType\\n960:     import_sources: dict[str, str]\\n961:     used_globals: dict[str, Any]\\n962:     closure: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n963:     argdefs: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n964: backend_id: str,\\n970:         compiled_fn: Callable[..., Any],\\n971:         *,\\n972:         extra_globals: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n973:     ) -> Callable[..., Any]:\\n974:         import_sources = {\\n975:             alias: importlib.import_module(module_name)\\n976:             for alias, module_name in self.import_sources.items()\\n977:         }\\n978:         f_globals = {\\n979:             **import_sources,\\n980:             **self.used_globals,\\n981:             **(extra_globals or {}),\\n982:             backend_id: compiled_fn,\\n983:         }\\n984: \\n985:          f_globals,\\n991:             closure=self.closure,\\n992:             argdefs=self.argdefs,\\n993:         )\\n994:         if self.kwdefaults:\\n995:             fn.__kwdefaults__ = self.kwdefaults\\n996:         return fn\\n997: \\n998:     def _check_external_refs(self, f_globals: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n999:         # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n1000:         missing_refs = []\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL500-1000\"}"

\\n1005:         if missing_refs:\\n1006:             raise RuntimeError(\\n1007:                 f\\\"Missing required external references: {missing_refs}. \\\"\\n1008:                 \\\"Please load AOT compiled function with `f_globals=<enclosing global scope>`\\\"\\n1009:             )\\n1010: \\n1011: \\n1012: @dataclass\\n1013: class GraphCaptureOutput:\\n1014:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1015:     Minimal version of DynamoOutput\\n1016:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1017: \\n1018:     output_graph: OutputGraphCommon\\n1019:     import_sources: dict[str, str]\\n1020:     traced_code: list[CodeType]\\n1021:     bytecode: CodeType\\n1022:     closure: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n1023:     argdefs: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n1024:     kwdefaults: Optional[dict[str, Any]]\\n1025:     f_globals: dict[str, Any]\\n1026: \\n1027:     def build_guards(\\n1028:         self,\\n1029:         code: types.CodeType,\\n1030:         hooks: Optional[Hooks] = None,\\n1031:         save: bool = False,\\n1032:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry] = None,\\n1033:         strict_error: bool = False,\\n1034:     ) -> CheckFunctionManager:\\n1035:         return CheckFunctionManager(\\n1036:             code,\\n1037:             self.output_graph,\\n1038:             cache_entry,\\n1039:             hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\\n1040:             hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\\n1041:             save_guards=save,\\n1042:             strict_error=strict_error,\\n1043:         )\\n1044: \\n1045:     def get_runtime_env(self) -> GraphRuntimeEnv:\\n1046:         from torch._dynamo.source import get_global_source_name\\n1047: \\n1048:         used_globals = {}\\n1049:         for (\\n1050:             source\\n1051:         ) in self.output_graph.export_metadata.graph_input_idx_to_local_source.values():\\n1052:             global_name = get_global_source_name(source)\\n1053:             if global_name is None:\\n1054:                 continue\\n1055:             if global_name in self.f_globals:\\n1056:                 used_globals[global_name] = self.f_globals[global_name]\\n1057: \\n1058:         # Scan bytecode for all external references\\n1059:         external_refs = self._get_external_refs(self.bytecode)\\n1060: \\n1061:         return GraphRuntimeEnv(\\n1062:             bytecode=self.bytecode,\\n1063:             import_sources=self.import_sources,\\n1064:             used_globals=used_globals,\\n1065:             closure=self.closure,\\n1066:             argdefs=self.argdefs,\\n1067:             kwdefaults=self.kwdefaults,\\n1068:             external_refs=external_refs,\\n1069:         )\\n1070: \\n1071:     @staticmethod\\n1072:     def _get_external_refs(bytecode: types.CodeType) -> set[str]:\\n1073:         import dis\\n1074: \\n1075:         external_refs: set[str] = set()\\n1076: \\n1077:         # Get all instructions from the bytecode\\n1078:         for instruction in dis.get_instructions(bytecode):\\n1079:             # LOAD_GLOBAL loads a global variable or a builtin\\n1080:             if instruction.opname == \\\"LOAD_GLOBAL\\\":\\n1081:                 if instruction.argval:\\n1082:                     external_refs.add(instruction.argval)\\n1083:             # LOAD_NAME loads a name (used in module-level code, less common in functions)\\n1084:             elif instruction.opname == \\\"LOAD_NAME\\\":\\n1085:                 if instruction.argval:\\n1086:                     external_refs.add(instruction.argval)\\n1087: \\n1088:         return external_refs\\n1089: \\n1090: \\n1091: @dataclass\\n1092: class CaptureOutput:\\n1093:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1094:     CaptureOutput should represent all the information produced from torch\\n1095:     compiler for a single graph capture. This intends to be consumed by\\n1096:     various compiler frontends so that we can share as much compiler internals\\n1097:     as possible and avoid great divergence between different stacks.\\n1098:     This data structure should eventually contain all the information compiler\\n1099:     produces as more refactors happens to converge different compiler\\n1100:     frontends.\\n1101:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1102: \\n1103:     graph_capture_output: GraphCaptureOutput\\n1104:     # BackendInput can be None when dynamo didn't compile any graph (no tensor op)\\n1105:     backend_input: Optional[BackendInput]\\n1106: \\n1107:     def forward_callable(\\n1108:         self,\\n1109:         *,\\n1110:         compiled_fn: Optional[Callable[..., Any]] = None,\\n1111:         extra_globals: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n1112:     ) -> Callable[..., Any]:\\n1113:         runtime_env = self.graph_capture_output.get_runtime_env()\\n1114:         assert self.backend_input is not None\\n1115:         backend_id = self.backend_input.backend_id\\n1116:         compiled_fn = compiled_fn or self.backend_input.graph_module\\n1117:         return runtime_env.forward_callable(\\n1118:             backend_id,\\n1119:             compiled_fn,\\n1120:             extra_globals=extra_globals,\\n1121:         )\\n1122: \\n1123: \\n1124: def get_traced_fn(mod: Any) -> tuple[FunctionType, Optional[object]]:\\n1125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1126:     Utility function to get the function to trace, and optionally a bound self\\n1127:     object, from a callable (nn.Module, function, or method).\\n1128:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1129:     import inspect\\n1130: \\n1131:     if isinstance(mod, torch.nn.Module):\\n1132:         resolved_forward = mod.forward\\n1133:         if hasattr(resolved_forward, \\\"__self__\\\"):\\n1134:             # pyrefly: ignore [missing-attribute]\\n1135:             resolved_forward = resolved_forward.__func__\\n1136: \\n1137:         resolved_call = mod.__call__\\n1138:         if hasattr(resolved_call, \\\"__self__\\\"):\\n1139:             resolved_call = resolved_call.__func__\\n1140: \\n1141:         # Mirrored from NNModuleVariable.call_function:\\n1142:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/torch/_dynamo/variables/nn_module.py#L1035\\n1143:         if (\\n1144:             len(mod._forward_pre_hooks) == 0\\n1145:             and len(mod._forward_hooks) == 0\\n1146:             and len(torch.nn.modules.module._global_forward_pre_hooks) == 0\\n1147:             and len(torch.nn.modules.module._global_forward_hooks) == 0\\n1148:             and len(mod._backward_pre_hooks) == 0\\n1149:             and len(mod._backward_hooks) == 0\\n1150:             and len(torch.nn.modules.module._global_backward_pre_hooks) == 0\\n1151:             and len(torch.nn.modules.module._global_backward_hooks) == 0\\n1152:             and resolved_forward != torch.nn.Module.forward  # has forward impl\\n1153:             and resolved_call == torch.nn.Module.__call__  # no custom __call__ impl\\n1154:         ):\\n1155:             # We cannot trace __call__ by default because it will break\\n1156:             # the legacy dynamo export. If we want to revisit this,\\n1157:             # feel free to remove this path and try unittests in\\n1158:             # test_strict_export_v2.py\\n1159:             mod = mod.forward\\n1160:         elif isinstance(mod, torch.fx.GraphModule):\\n1161:             mod = mod._call_impl\\n1162:         else:\\n1163:             mod = mod.__call__\\n1164: \\n1165:     if hasattr(mod, \\\"__self__\\\"):\\n1166:         return mod.__func__, mod.__self__\\n1167:     elif inspect.isfunction(mod):\\n1168:         return mod, None\\n1169:     else:\\n1170:         raise RuntimeError(f\\\"Unsupported model code type {mod}\\\")\\n1171: \\n1172: \\n1173: def _get_signature(fn: Any) -> inspect.Signature:\\n1174:     return inspect.signature(fn, follow_wrapped=False)\\n1175: \\n1176: \\n1177: def _get_frame(\\n1178:     mod: Any,\\n1179:     args: tuple[Any, ...],\\n1180:     kwargs: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n1181: ) -> FrameInfo:\\n1182:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1183:     Create a frame to trace, given a model, args, and optional kwargs.\\n1184:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1185:     import builtins\\n1186: \\n1187:     fn, self_opt = get_traced_fn(mod)\\n1188:     if self_opt is not None:\\n1189:         args = (self_opt,) + args\\n1190:     if kwargs is None:\\n1191:         kwargs = {}\\n1192: \\n1193:     signature = _get_signature(fn)\\n1194:     bound_arguments = signature.bind(*args, **kwargs)\\n1195:     bound_arguments.apply_defaults()\\n1196:     f_locals = bound_arguments.arguments\\n1197: \\n1198:     closure = fn.__closure__ or ()\\n1199:     freevars = fn.__code__.co_freevars\\n1200:     if freevars or closure:\\n1201:         assert len(closure) == len(freevars)\\n1202:         f_locals.update(\\n1203:             {name: cell.cell_contents for name, cell in zip(freevars, closure)}\\n1204:         )\\n1205: \\n1206:     return FrameInfo(\\n1207:         fn.__code__,\\n1208:         fn.__globals__,\\n1209:         f_locals,\\n1210:         builtins.__dict__,\\n1211:         closure=fn.__closure__ or (),  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1212:         argdefs=fn.__defaults__,\\n1213:         kwdefaults=fn.__kwdefaults__,\\n1214:     )\\n1215: \\n1216: \\n1217: def fullgraph_capture(\\n1218:     mod: Any,\\n1219:     args: tuple[Any, ...],\\n1220:     kwargs: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n1221:     *,\\n1222:     constraints: Optional[list[Constraint]] = None,\\n1223:     _is_export_deprecated_do_not_use: bool = False,\\n1224: ) -> CaptureOutput:\\n1225:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1226:     This API captures a full graph for a model, given example inputs to trace with.\\n1227: \\n1228:     Specifically, it takes a callable (nn.Module, method, or function), args, and\\n1229:     optional kwargs, and returns Dynamo-captured graph along with other important\\n1230:     compile-time information. This serves as the common graph-capture mechanism\\n1231:     for different torch compiler AOT frontends (e.g. AOT precompile, export).\\n1232: \\n1233:     Note that this API doesn't apply context managers like metrics context,\\n1234:     and the expectation is that the caller will apply them depending\\n1235:     on the use case.\\n1236: \\n1237:     The CaptureOutput is separated into two parts:\\n1238:     1. Frontend specific information, which includes:\\n1239:         - guards\\n1240:         - generated bytecode\\n1241:         - other information tracked by OutputGraphCommon.\\n1242:     2. Backend specific information (indexed by unique backend id) such as:\\n1243:         - fx graph\\n1244:         - example inputs\\n1245:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1246:     frame = _get_frame(mod, args, kwargs)\\n1247: \\n1248:     with compile_context(CompileContext(get_compile_id({}))):\\n1249:         return _fullgraph_capture_frame(\\n1250:             frame,\\n1251:             constraints=constraints,\\n1252:             _is_export_deprecated_do_not_use=_is_export_deprecated_do_not_use,\\n1253:         )\\n1254: \\n1255: \\n1256: @dataclass\\n1257: class FrameInfo:\\n1258:     code: types.CodeType\\n1259:     globals: dict[str, object]\\n1260:     locals: dict[str, object]\\n1261:     builtins: dict[str, object]\\n1262:     closure: tuple[CellType]\\n1263:     argdefs: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]]\\n1264:     kwdefaults: Optional[dict[str, Any]]\\n1265: \\n1266: \\n1267: def _fullgraph_capture_frame(\\n1268:     frame: FrameInfo,\\n1269:     *,\\n1270:     constraints: Optional[list[Constraint]] = None,\\n1271:     _is_export_deprecated_do_not_use: bool = False,\\n1272: ) -> CaptureOutput:\\n1273:     from torch._guards import TracingContext\\n1274: \\n1275:     backend_input: Optional[BackendInput] = None\\n1276: \\n1277:     def fullgraph_compiler(\\n1278:         gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]\\n1279:     ) -> torch.fx.GraphModule:\\n1280:         nonlocal backend_input\\n1281:         tracing_context = TracingContext.get()\\n1282:         fake_mode = tracing_context.fake_mode\\n1283:         tensor_to_context = tracing_context.tensor_to_context\\n1284:         assert fake_mode is not None\\n1285:         assert isinstance(gm.meta[\\\"backend_id\\\"], str)\\n1286:         backend_input = BackendInput(\\n1287:             gm.meta[\\\"backend_id\\\"], gm, example_inputs, fake_mode, tensor_to_context\\n1288:         )\\n1289:         return gm\\n1290: \\n1291:     try:\\n1292:         dynamo_output = compile_frame(\\n1293:             frame.code,\\n1294:             frame.globals,\\n1295:             frame.locals,\\n1296:             frame.builtins,\\n1297:             frame.closure,\\n1298:             compiler_fn=fullgraph_compiler,\\n1299:             export=_is_export_deprecated_do_not_use,\\n1300:             export_constraints=constraints,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1301:             one_graph=True,\\n1302:             restart_reasons=set(),\\n1303:         )\\n1304:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py#L831\\n1305:     except (Unsupported, UncapturedHigherOrderOpError) as e:\\n1306:         augment_exc_message(e)\\n1307:         if config.verbose:\\n1308:             raise\\n1309:         # strip internal tracebacks from causes\\n1310:         cur_exn: BaseException = e\\n1311:         while cur_exn.__cause__ is not None:\\n1312:             cur_exn.__cause__.with_traceback(None)\\n1313:             cur_exn = cur_exn.__cause__\\n1314: \\n1315:         raise e.with_traceback(None) from e.__cause__  # User compiler error\\n1316: \\n1317:     return CaptureOutput(\\n1318:         dynamo_output.graph_capture_output(frame.argdefs, frame.kwdefaults),\\n1319:         backend_input,\\n1320:     )\\n1321: \\n1322: \\n1323: # Called by eval_frame_cpp.cpp in order to raise an error if Dynamo attempts to compile_frame\\n1324: def get_fail_callback(callback: ConvertFrameProtocol) -> ConvertFrameProtocol:\\n1325:     fail_callback = getattr(callback, \\\"_dynamo_fail_callback\\\", None)\\n1326:     if fail_callback is not None:\\n1327:         return fail_callback\\n1328: \\n1329:     def compile_frame_error(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> NoReturn:\\n1330:         raise RuntimeError(\\n1331:             \\\"Dynamo: expected not to compile nested code - this happens because \\\"\\n1332:             \\\"a Dynamo callback was triggered and succeeded in compiling \\\"\\n1333:             \\\"when running fullgraph=True compiled code.\\\"\\n1334:         )\\n1335: \\n1336:     def fail_callback(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n1337:         with mock.patch(__name__ + \\\".compile_frame\\\", compile_frame_error):\\n1338:             return callback(*args, **kwargs)\\n1339: \\n1340:     # pyrefly: ignore [missing-attribute]\\n1341:     callback._dynamo_fail_callback = fail_callback\\n1342:     return fail_callback\\n1343: \\n1344: \\n1345: def compile_frame(  # type: ignore[return]\\n1346:     code: types.CodeType,\\n1347:     globals: dict[str, object],\\n1348:     locals: dict[str, object],\\n1349:     builtins: dict[str, object],\\n1350:     closure: tuple[CellType],\\n1351:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n1352:     one_graph: bool,\\n1353:     restart_reasons: set[str],\\n1354:     *,\\n1355:     export: bool = False,\\n1356:     export_constraints: Any | None = None,\\n1357:     frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\\n1358:     distributed_state: Optional[DistributedState] = None,\\n1359:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n1360:     # pyrefly: ignore [bad-return]\\n1361: ) -> DynamoOutput:\\n1362:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1363:     A helper function taking a frame and backend, then return the generated bytecode\\n1364:     and guards as a common data structure.\\n1365:     This is a shared interface for multiple compiler frontends (e.g. torch.compile,\\n1366:     torch.export) that needs to capture a graph out of python code.\\n1367:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1368:     # This is shared across restarts\\n1369:     speculation_log = SpeculationLog()\\n1370: \\n1371:     def transform(\\n1372:         instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\\n1373:     ) -> DynamoTracerOutput:\\n1374:         tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\\n1375:             torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\\n1376:         )\\n1377:         tracer_output = trace_frame(\\n1378:             code,\\n1379:             globals,\\n1380:             locals,\\n1381:             builtins,\\n1382:             closure,\\n1383:             compiler_fn,\\n1384:             tf_mode_stack,\\n1385:             one_graph,\\n1386:             speculation_log,\\n1387:             instructions,\\n1388:             code_options,\\n1389:             export=export,\\n1390:             export_constraints=export_constraints,\\n1391:             frame_state=frame_state,\\n1392:             distributed_state=distributed_state,\\n1393:             package=package,\\n1394:         )\\n1395: \\n1396:         assert tracer_output is not None\\n1397:         return tracer_output\\n1398: \\n1399:     last_attempt_start_time = None\\n1400:     for attempt in itertools.count():\\n1401:         CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\\n1402: \\n1403:         try:\\n1404:             with dynamo_timed(f\\\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n1405:                 bytecode, tracer_output = transform_code_object(code, transform)\\n1406:                 assert tracer_output is not None\\n1407:                 return DynamoOutput(\\n1408:                     tracer_output=tracer_output,\\n1409:                     bytecode=bytecode,\\n1410:                     last_attempt_start_time=last_attempt_start_time,\\n1411:                 )\\n1412:         except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\\n1413:             if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\\n1414:                 TensorifyState.clear()\\n1415:             log.info(\\n1416:                 \\\"Restarting analysis due to %s\\\",\\n1417:                 LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\\n1418:             )\\n1419:             # Clean up the failed tracer output's graph to break reference cycles\\n1420:             failed_tracer_output = getattr(e, \\\"_torch_dynamo_tracer_output\\\", None)\\n1421:             if failed_tracer_output:\\n1422:                 failed_tracer_output._cleanup_output_graph()\\n1423:             # If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\\n1424:             restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\\n1425:             # We now have a new \\\"last attempt\\\", reset the clock\\n1426:             last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\\n1427:             if attempt > 100:\\n1428:                 unimplemented(\\n1429:                     gb_type=\\\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\\\",\\n1430:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n1431:                     explanation=\\\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \\\"\\n1432:                     \\\"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \\\"\\n1433:                     \\\"worth the performance gain.\\\",\\n1434:                     hints=[],\\n1435:                 )\\n1436:         except exc.SkipFrame as e:\\n1437:             if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\\n1438:                 TensorifyState.clear()\\n1439:             # Clean up the failed tracer output's graph to break reference cycles\\n1440:             failed_tracer_output = getattr(e, \\\"_torch_dynamo_tracer_output\\\", None)\\n1441:             if failed_tracer_output:\\n1442:                 failed_tracer_output._cleanup_output_graph()\\n1443:             log.debug(  # noqa: G200\\n1444:                 \\\"Received signal to skip frame (without graph break): %s %s \\\\\\n1445:                 %s %s\\\",\\n1446:                 e,\\n1447:                 code.co_name,\\n1448:                 code.co_filename,\\n1449:                 code.co_firstlineno,\\n1450:             )\\n1451:             raise\\n1452: \\n1453: \\n1454: def _compile(\\n1455:     code: CodeType,\\n1456:     globals: dict[str, object],\\n1457:     locals: dict[str, object],\\n1458:     builtins: dict[str, object],\\n1459:     closure: tuple[CellType],\\n1460:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n1461:     one_graph: bool,\\n1462:     export: bool,\\n1463:     export_constraints: Any | None,\\n1464:     hooks: Hooks,\\n1465:     cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n1466:     cache_size: CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\\n1467:     frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\\n1468:     frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\\n1469:     *,\\n1470:     compile_id: CompileId,\\n1471:     skip: int = 0,\\n1472:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n1473:     # Can be used to record things for the caller, both\\n1474:     # in the case of normal and exception code paths\\n1475:     convert_frame_box: Optional[ConvertFrameBox] = None,\\n1476: ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n1477:     from torch.fx.experimental.validator import (\\n1478:         BisectValidationException,\\n1479:         ValidationException,\\n1480:     )\\n1481: \\n1482:     # Only nonlocal defs here please!\\n1483:     # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\\n1484:     dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\\n1485: \\n1486:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n1487:     def compile_inner(\\n1488:         code: CodeType, one_graph: bool, hooks: Hooks\\n1489:     ) -> tuple[ConvertFrameReturn, Optional[DynamoTracerOutput]]:\\n1490:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n1491:             stack.enter_context(\\n1492:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n1493:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n1494:                 )\\n1495:             )\\n1496:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n1497:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks)\\n1498: \\n1499:         return (\\n1500:             ConvertFrameReturn(),\\n1501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1000-1500\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1500:             ConvertFrameReturn(),\\n1501:             None,\\n1502:         )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\\n1503: \\n1504:     @maybe_cprofile\\n1505:     def _compile_inner(\\n1506:         code: CodeType,\\n1507:         one_graph: bool,\\n1508:         hooks: Hooks,\\n1509:     ) -> tuple[ConvertFrameReturn, DynamoTracerOutput]:\\n1510:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\\n1511:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\\n1512: \\n1513:         def log_bytecode(\\n1514:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\\n1515:         ) -> None:\\n1516:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n1517:                 bytecode_log.debug(\\n1518:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\\n1519:                 )\\n1520: \\n1521:         log_bytecode(\\n1522:             \\\"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\\\",\\n1523:             code.co_name,\\n1524:             code.co_filename,\\n1525:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n1526:             code,\\n1527:         )\\n1528:         # Dump the ORIGINAL bytecode of resumption frame into TORCH_TRACE\\n1529:         # log file, so that it is parsed by tlparse tool.\\n1530:         is_resumption_frame = \\\"torch_dynamo_resume_in\\\" in code.co_name\\n1531:         if is_resumption_frame:\\n1532:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n1533:                 \\\"artifact\\\",\\n1534:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\\n1535:                     \\\"name\\\": code.co_name + \\\"_ORIGINAL_BYTECODE\\\",\\n1536:                     \\\"encoding\\\": \\\"string\\\",\\n1537:                 },\\n1538:                 payload_fn=lambda: dis.Bytecode(code).dis(),\\n1539:             )\\n1540:         out_code = None\\n1541:         try:\\n1542:             dynamo_output = compile_frame(\\n1543:                 code,\\n1544:                 globals,\\n1545:                 locals,\\n1546:                 builtins,\\n1547:                 closure,\\n1548:                 compiler_fn,\\n1549:                 one_graph,\\n1550:                 restart_reasons,\\n1551:                 export=export,\\n1552:                 export_constraints=export_constraints,\\n1553:                 frame_state=frame_state,\\n1554:                 distributed_state=distributed_state,\\n1555:                 package=package,\\n1556:             )\\n1557:         except exc.SkipFrame as e:\\n1558:             if one_graph:\\n1559:                 log.debug(\\\"No graph captured with export/fullgraph=True\\\")\\n1560:             assert e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output is not None\\n1561:             return ConvertFrameReturn(), e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output\\n1562: \\n1563:         assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n1564:             \\\"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\\\"\\n1565:         )\\n1566:         out_code = dynamo_output.bytecode\\n1567:         tracer_output = dynamo_output.tracer_output\\n1568:         if dynamo_output.last_attempt_start_time is not None:\\n1569:             last_attempt_start_time = dynamo_output.last_attempt_start_time\\n1570: \\n1571:         assert out_code is not None\\n1572:         log_bytecode(\\n1573:             \\\"MODIFIED BYTECODE\\\",\\n1574:             code.co_name,\\n1575:             code.co_filename,\\n1576:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n1577:             out_code,\\n1578:         )\\n1579:         # Dump the MODIFIED bytecode of resumption frame into TORCH_TRACE\\n1580:         # log file, so that it is parsed by tlparse tool.\\n1581:         if is_resumption_frame:\\n1582:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n1583:                 \\\"artifact\\\",\\n1584:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\\n1585:                     \\\"name\\\": code.co_name + \\\"_MODIFIED_BYTECODE\\\",\\n1586:                     \\\"encoding\\\": \\\"string\\\",\\n1587:                 },\\n1588:                 payload_fn=lambda: dis.Bytecode(out_code).dis(),\\n1589:             )\\n1590: \\n1591:         for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\\n1592:             with dynamo_timed(f\\\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n1593:                 hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\\n1594:                 if hook_output is not None:\\n1595:                     out_code = hook_output\\n1596: \\n1597:         orig_code_map[out_code] = code\\n1598:         output_codes.add(out_code)\\n1599:         dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\\n1600:         assert tracer_output.output_graph is not None\\n1601:         output = tracer_output.output_graph\\n1602: \\n1603:         from .bytecode_debugger import BREAKPOINT_MARKER\\n1604: \\n1605:         if BREAKPOINT_MARKER in out_code.co_consts:\\n1606:             from torch._C._dynamo.eval_frame import register_breakpoint_code\\n1607: \\n1608:             register_breakpoint_code(out_code)\\n1609: \\n1610:         # Tests for new code objects.\\n1611:         # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\\n1612:         # Only test once the code object is created.\\n1613:         # They are not tested during runtime.\\n1614: \\n1615:         def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\\n1616:             import inspect\\n1617: \\n1618:             return (\\n1619:                 code.co_argcount\\n1620:                 + code.co_kwonlyargcount\\n1621:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\\n1622:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\\n1623:             )\\n1624: \\n1625:         assert out_code is not None\\n1626: \\n1627:         total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\\n1628:         total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\\n1629:         msg = \\\"arg mismatch: \\\"\\n1630:         msg += f\\\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \\\"\\n1631:         msg += f\\\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\\\"\\n1632:         assert (\\n1633:             code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\\n1634:             == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\\n1635:         ), msg\\n1636: \\n1637:         msg = \\\"free var mismatch: \\\"\\n1638:         msg += f\\\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \\\"\\n1639:         msg += f\\\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\\\"\\n1640:         assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\\n1641: \\n1642:         msg = \\\"cell var mismatch: \\\"\\n1643:         msg += f\\\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \\\"\\n1644:         msg += f\\\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\\\"\\n1645:         assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\\n1646: \\n1647:         # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\\n1648:         # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\\n1649:         # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\\n1650:         # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\\n1651:         # are extra graphs now.\\n1652: \\n1653:         if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\\n1654:             return ConvertFrameReturn(), tracer_output\\n1655: \\n1656:         assert output.guards is not None\\n1657:         CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\\n1658:         nonlocal cache_entry\\n1659:         with dynamo_timed(\\\"build_guards\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n1660:             check_fn = dynamo_output.build_guards(\\n1661:                 code,\\n1662:                 hooks=hooks,\\n1663:                 save=package is not None,\\n1664:                 cache_entry=cache_entry,\\n1665:             )\\n1666: \\n1667:         if package is not None:\\n1668:             assert check_fn.guards_state is not None\\n1669:             package.add_guarded_code(check_fn.guards_state, out_code)\\n1670:             package.add_inlined_source(output.tracing_context.traced_code)\\n1671:             package.update_device_type(output.current_tracer.graph)\\n1672: \\n1673:         compile_id_str = str(compile_id) if compile_id is not None else \\\"Unknown\\\"\\n1674:         annotation_str = \\\"Torch-Compiled Region: \\\" + compile_id_str\\n1675:         guarded_code = GuardedCode(\\n1676:             out_code,\\n1677:             check_fn.guard_manager,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1678:             compile_id,\\n1679:             annotation_str,\\n1680:         )\\n1681: \\n1682:         if not output.is_empty_graph() and hooks.guard_export_fn is not None:\\n1683:             # We should not run the guard_export_fn when Dynamo does not\\n1684:             # generate any graph. This can happen in export when TorchDynamo\\n1685:             # generated bytecode has some reconstruction logic for mutated\\n1686:             # variables which can trigger TorchDynamo on the children frames but\\n1687:             # they are benign and do not generate any new graphs.\\n1688:             hooks.guard_export_fn(output.guards)\\n1689: \\n1690:         return wrap_guarded_code(guarded_code), tracer_output\\n1691: \\n1692:     metrics_context = get_metrics_context()\\n1693:     code_context = (\\n1694:         package.code_context(code) if package is not None else contextlib.nullcontext()\\n1695:     )\\n1696:     with (\\n1697:         _use_lazy_graph_module(config.use_lazy_graph_module),\\n1698:         compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)),\\n1699:         chromium_event_timed(\\n1700:             \\\"dynamo\\\", reset_event_log_on_exit=True, log_pt2_compile_event=True\\n1701:         ),\\n1702:         _WaitCounter(\\\"pytorch.wait_counter.entire_forward_compile\\\").guard(),\\n1703:         metrics_context,\\n1704:         dynamo_timed(\\n1705:             \\\"_compile.compile_inner\\\",\\n1706:             phase_name=\\\"entire_frame_compile\\\",\\n1707:             dynamo_compile_column_us=\\\"dynamo_cumulative_compile_time_us\\\",\\n1708:         ),\\n1709:         code_context,\\n1710:     ):\\n1711:         restart_reasons: set[str] = set()\\n1712:         if compile_pg := get_compile_pg():\\n1713:             distributed_state = DistributedState(compile_pg, LocalState())\\n1714:         else:\\n1715:             distributed_state = None\\n1716: \\n1717:         # Check recompilations\\n1718:         recompile_reason: Optional[str] = None\\n1719:         if is_recompilation(cache_size) and frame:\\n1720:             reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\\n1721:                 cache_entry, frame, innermost_fn(compiler_fn)\\n1722:             )\\n1723:             recompile_reason = (\\n1724:                 \\\"Unable to find recompilation reasons\\\" if not reasons else reasons[0]\\n1725:             )\\n1726:         # Recheck for recompilation, for when inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set to False\\n1727:         inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = False\\n1728:         if not config.inline_inbuilt_nn_modules and frame:\\n1729:             inbuilt_nn_reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\\n1730:                 cache_entry, frame, innermost_fn(compiler_fn), skip_logging=True\\n1731:             )\\n1732:             inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason = (\\n1733:                 None if not inbuilt_nn_reasons else inbuilt_nn_reasons[0]\\n1734:             )\\n1735: \\n1736:             if (\\n1737:                 inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason is not None\\n1738:                 and \\\"[inline-inbuilt-nn-modules-candidate]\\\"\\n1739:                 in inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason\\n1740:             ):\\n1741:                 inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = True\\n1742: \\n1743:         # Set if the recompile is a candidate for inline_inbuilt_nn_modules\\n1744:         # regardless of whether inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set or not\\n1745:         metrics_context.update_outer(\\n1746:             {\\n1747:                 \\\"recompile_reason\\\": recompile_reason,\\n1748:                 \\\"inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate\\\": inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate,\\n1749:             }\\n1750:         )\\n1751: \\n1752:         recompile_user_contexts = get_hook_for_recompile_user_context()\\n1753:         if recompile_user_contexts:\\n1754:             # cap each user context to N chars for data retention purposes. N=256\\n1755:             # is chosen to be large enough to capture the most important info.\\n1756:             user_contexts_msg = {\\n1757:                 user_context()[:256] for user_context in recompile_user_contexts\\n1758:             }\\n1759:             metrics_context.set(\\\"recompile_user_contexts\\\", user_contexts_msg)\\n1760: \\n1761:         exceeded, limit_type = exceeds_recompile_limit(cache_size, compile_id)\\n1762:         if exceeded:\\n1763: \\n1764:             def format_func_info(code: CodeType) -> str:\\n1765:                 return f\\\"'{code.co_name}' ({code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno})\\\"\\n1766: \\n1767:             # NS: Don't add period at the end of string, as it'll be added to URL\\n1768:             # rendering it incorrect\\n1769:             log.warning(\\n1770:                 \\\"torch._dynamo hit config.%s (%s)\\\\n\\\"\\n1771:                 \\\"   function: %s\\\\n\\\"\\n1772:                 \\\"   last reason: %s\\\\n\\\"\\n1773:                 'To log all recompilation reasons, use TORCH_LOGS=\\\"recompiles\\\".\\\\n'\\n1774:                 \\\"To diagnose recompilation issues, see %s\\\",\\n1775:                 limit_type,\\n1776:                 getattr(config, limit_type),\\n1777:                 format_func_info(code),\\n1778:                 recompile_reason,\\n1779:                 troubleshooting_url,\\n1780:             )\\n1781: \\n1782:             def raise_unimplemented_cache_limit_exceeded() -> NoReturn:\\n1783:                 unimplemented(\\n1784:                     gb_type=\\\"Dynamo recompile limit exceeded\\\",\\n1785:                     context=f\\\"Limit type: {limit_type}\\\",\\n1786:                     explanation=\\\"Dynamo attempted to recompile the code object too many times, \\\"\\n1787:                     f\\\"exceeding the {limit_type} cache size limit (currently set to {getattr(config, limit_type)}). \\\"\\n1788:                     \\\"Excessive recompilations can degrade \\\"\\n1789:                     \\\"performance due to the compilation overhead of each recompilation.\\\",\\n1790:                     hints=[\\n1791:                         \\\"To monitor recompilations, enable TORCH_LOGS=recompiles. \\\"\\n1792:                         \\\"If recompilations are expected, consider \\\"\\n1793:                         f\\\"increasing torch._dynamo.config.{limit_type} to an appropriate value.\\\",\\n1794:                         f\\\"See {troubleshooting_url} for tips on dealing with recompilations.\\\",\\n1795:                     ],\\n1796:                 )\\n1797: \\n1798:             try:\\n1799:                 raise_unimplemented_cache_limit_exceeded()\\n1800:             except Unsupported as e:\\n1801:                 if config.fail_on_recompile_limit_hit:\\n1802:                     raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\\n1803:                         \\\"Hard failure due to fail_on_recompile_limit_hit\\\"\\n1804:                     ) from e\\n1805:                 elif one_graph:\\n1806:                     raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\\n1807:                         \\\"Hard failure due to fullgraph=True\\\"\\n1808:                     ) from e\\n1809:                 else:\\n1810:                     # Set frame execution strategy to RUN_ONLY for this recompile limit case\\n1811:                     e.frame_exec_strategy = FrameExecStrategy(\\n1812:                         FrameAction.RUN_ONLY, FrameAction.RUN_ONLY\\n1813:                     )\\n1814:                     raise\\n1815: \\n1816:         log.debug(\\n1817:             \\\"torchdynamo start compiling %s %s:%s, stack (elided %s frames):\\\\n%s\\\",\\n1818:             code.co_name,\\n1819:             code.co_filename,\\n1820:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n1821:             skip + 2,\\n1822:             # -2: omit current frame, omit contextlib decorator\\n1823:             \\\"\\\".join(CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=2 + skip).format()),\\n1824:         )\\n1825:         # -4: -2 as above, plus trace_structured frames\\n1826:         #\\n1827:         # NB: the frame looks like this:\\n1828:         #\\n1829:         # # handled by skip argument\\n1830:         # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:1069 in catch_errors\\n1831:         # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:910 in _convert_frame\\n1832:         # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:464 in _convert_frame_assert\\n1833:         # torch/_utils_internal.py:70 in wrapper_function\\n1834:         #\\n1835:         # # 2 current frame and context lib\\n1836:         # env/lib/python3.10/contextlib.py:79 in inner\\n1837:         # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:776 in _compile\\n1838:         #\\n1839:         # # 2 extra here\\n1840:         # torch/_logging/_internal.py:1064 in trace_structured\\n1841:         # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:780 in <lambda>\\n1842:         stack_trace = log_dynamo_start(code, skip)\\n1843:         start_time_ns = time.time_ns()\\n1844:         fail_type: Optional[str] = None\\n1845:         fail_reason: Optional[str] = None\\n1846:         exception_stack_trace: Optional[list[str]] = None\\n1847:         fail_user_frame_filename: Optional[str] = None\\n1848:         fail_user_frame_lineno: Optional[int] = None\\n1849:         torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.clear()\\n1850:         guarded_code = None\\n1851:         tracer_output = None\\n1852:         try:\\n1853:             guarded_code, tracer_output = compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks)\\n1854: \\n1855:             # NB: We only put_code_state in success case.  Success case here\\n1856:             # does include graph breaks; specifically, if a graph break still\\n1857:             # resulted in a partially compiled graph, we WILL return here.  An\\n1858:             # Unsupported exception will only bubble to the top level if we\\n1859:             # are unable to compile the frame at all.  In this case, there's\\n1860:             # no point in uploading the code state, because we will always\\n1861:             # fail exactly the same way even without the update.  (It's useful\\n1862:             # to upload for graph break though, because this can prevent\\n1863:             # extra graph break compilations.)\\n1864:             put_code_state()\\n1865:             if (\\n1866:                 tracer_output\\n1867:                 and (output_graph := tracer_output.output_graph)\\n1868:                 and output_graph.has_outputs()\\n1869:             ):\\n1870:                 log_frame_dynamic_whitelist(code)\\n1871:                 if recompile_reason and \\\"size mismatch at index\\\" in recompile_reason:\\n1872:                     _log_size_mismatch_recompile()\\n1873: \\n1874:             return guarded_code\\n1875:         except Exception as e:\\n1876:             # NB: e's msg is mutated here to add user stack, but we DON'T want\\n1877:             # that stack in the Scuba logged fail_reason. So we grab the fail\\n1878:             # info here and add it to the metrics context below.\\n1879:             fail_type = type(e).__qualname__\\n1880:             fail_reason = str(e)\\n1881:             exception_stack_trace = [traceback.format_exc()]\\n1882:             exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)\\n1883:             # NB: this is the post-mutation exception\\n1884:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n1885:                 \\\"artifact\\\",\\n1886:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\\n1887:                     \\\"name\\\": \\\"dynamo_error\\\",\\n1888:                     \\\"encoding\\\": \\\"string\\\",\\n1889:                 },\\n1890:                 payload_fn=lambda: traceback.format_exc(),\\n1891:             )\\n1892:             fail_user_frame_filename, fail_user_frame_lineno = exc.get_exc_message(\\n1893:                 e, compile_id\\n1894:             )\\n1895:             tracer_output = getattr(e, \\\"_torch_dynamo_tracer_output\\\", None)\\n1896:             if isinstance(\\n1897:                 e,\\n1898:                 (\\n1899:                     Unsupported,\\n1900:                     TorchRuntimeError,\\n1901:                     BackendCompilerFailed,\\n1902:                     AssertionError,\\n1903:                     ConstraintViolationError,\\n1904:                     GuardOnDataDependentSymNode,\\n1905:                     ValidationException,\\n1906:                     UncapturedHigherOrderOpError,\\n1907:                     BisectValidationException,\\n1908:                     ShortenTraceback,\\n1909:                     PackageError,\\n1910:                     ResumePrologueTracingError,\\n1911:                 ),\\n1912:             ):\\n1913:                 raise\\n1914:             else:\\n1915:                 # Rewrap for clarity\\n1916:                 raise InternalTorchDynamoError(\\n1917:                     f\\\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\\\"\\n1918:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\\n1919:         finally:\\n1920:             # === WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\\n1921:             # If you commit a bug here, it will suppress writing to\\n1922:             # dynamo_compile table, and we will not have telemetry.\\n1923:             # Be extra careful when making changes here!\\n1924: \\n1925:             if torch._dynamo.config.run_gc_after_compile:\\n1926:                 with dynamo_timed(\\\"gc\\\", dynamo_compile_column_us=\\\"gc_time_us\\\"):\\n1927:                     log.info(\\\"run_gc_after_compile: running gc\\\")\\n1928:                     gc.collect(1)\\n1929: \\n1930:             output = None\\n1931:             if tracer_output:\\n1932:                 output = tracer_output.output_graph\\n1933:             if output:\\n1934:                 # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n1935:                 output.local_scope = {}\\n1936:                 # tracer should already be None, keep an extra check here just in case.\\n1937:                 if tracer := output.root_tx:\\n1938:                     # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\\n1939:                     tracer.f_locals = {}\\n1940: \\n1941:             from .utils import curr_frame\\n1942: \\n1943:             frame_key = str(curr_frame)\\n1944:             if fail_reason is None and output is not None:\\n1945:                 guard_count = len(output.guards)\\n1946:                 shape_env_guard_count = len(output.shape_env.guards)\\n1947:                 graph_op_count = output.count_calls()\\n1948:                 graph_node_count = len(output.graph.nodes)\\n1949:                 graph_node_shapes = output.get_graph_sizes_structured()\\n1950:                 graph_input_count = len(output.placeholders)\\n1951:                 non_compliant_ops = {op.__qualname__ for op in output.non_compliant_ops}\\n1952:                 compliant_custom_ops = {\\n1953:                     op.__qualname__ for op in output.compliant_custom_ops\\n1954:                 }\\n1955:                 torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.log()\\n1956:             else:\\n1957:                 guard_count = None\\n1958:                 shape_env_guard_count = None\\n1959:                 graph_op_count = None\\n1960:                 graph_node_count = None\\n1961:         compliant_custom_ops = set({})\\n1966:                 restart_reasons = set()\\n1967:                 # If compilation failed, the entire time is wasted\\n1968:                 dynamo_time_before_restart = (time.time_ns() - start_time_ns) / 1e9\\n1969: \\n1970:             metrics = {\\n1971:                 \\\"frame_key\\\": frame_key,\\n1972:                 \\\"co_name\\\": code.co_name,\\n1973:                 \\\"co_filename\\\": code.co_filename,\\n1974:                 \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": code.co_firstlineno,\\n1975:                 \\\"cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\\n1976:                 \\\"accumulated_cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\\n1977:                 \\\"guard_count\\\": guard_count,\\n1978:                 \\\"shape_env_guard_count\\\": shape_env_guard_count,\\n1979:                 \\\"graph_op_count\\\": graph_op_count,\\n1980:                 \\\"graph_node_count\\\": graph_node_count,\\n1981:                 \\\"graph_input_count\\\": graph_input_count,\\n1982:                 \\\"fail_type\\\": fail_type,\\n1983:                 \\\"fail_reason\\\": fail_reason,\\n1984:                 \\\"fail_user_frame_filename\\\": fail_user_frame_filename,\\n1985:                 \\\"fail_user_frame_lineno\\\": fail_user_frame_lineno,\\n1986:                 \\\"non_compliant_ops\\\": non_compliant_ops,\\n1987:                 \\\"compliant_custom_ops\\\": compliant_custom_ops,\\n1988:                 \\\"restart_reasons\\\": restart_reasons,\\n1989:                 \\\"dynamo_time_before_restart_s\\\": dynamo_time_before_restart,\\n1990:                 \\\"has_guarded_code\\\": guarded_code is not None,\\n1991:                 \\\"specialize_float\\\": config.specialize_float,\\n1992:                 \\\"is_forward\\\": True,\\n1993:                 \\\"dynamo_compile_time_before_restart_us\\\": to_int_us(\\n1994:                     dynamo_time_before_restart\\n1995:                 ),\\n1996:                 \\\"stack_trace\\\": stack_trace,\\n1997:                 \\\"graph_node_shapes\\\": str(graph_node_shapes),\\n1998:                 \\\"exception_stack_trace\\\": exception_stack_trace,\\n1999:             }\\n2000:
