"{\"content\":\"2000:             # TODO: replace with CompileEventLogger.compilation_metrics\\n2001:             # There are some columns here not in PT2 Compile Events\\n2002:             # so we need to slightly change it\\n2003:             metrics_context.update_outer(metrics)\\n2004:             # === END WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\\n2005: \\n2006:             # If tracer is available, then tracer.error_on_graph_break reflects value of\\n2007:             # global symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break at the time of the graph break -\\n2008:             # symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break may have been (correctly) changed during cleanup.\\n2009:             # If tracer is unavailable, then fallback to symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break.\\n2010:             if convert_frame_box:\\n2011:                 convert_frame_box.error_on_graph_break = (\\n2012:                     tracer_output.error_on_graph_break\\n2013:                     if tracer_output\\n2014:                     else _get_error_on_graph_break()\\n2015:                 )\\n2016: \\n2017:             # Cleanup guards unless if in export, which will return guards\\n2018:             # Make sure to to do this after collecting metrics\\n2019:             if (\\n2020:                 tracer_output is not None\\n2021:                 and tracer_output.output_graph is not None\\n2022:                 and not tracer_output.output_graph.export\\n2023:             ):\\n2024:                 tracer_output.output_graph.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards.inner = OrderedSet()\\n2025: \\n2026:             # Clear WeakIdRef entries that can block swap_tensors after compile.\\n2027:             # Determine whether to clear based on config and backend type.\\n2028:             should_clear = config.invalidate_compile_context_weakrefs\\n2029:             if should_clear is None:\\n2030:                 # Default: clear for registered backends, don't clear for custom\\n2031:                 # Unwrap the compiler_fn to get the actual backend function\\n2032:                 should_clear = _is_registered_backend(innermost_backend(compiler_fn))\\n2033:             if should_clear:\\n2034:                 if tracer_output and tracer_output.output_graph:\\n2035:                     tc = tracer_output.output_graph.tracing_context\\n2036:                     tc.tensor_to_context.clear()\\n2037:                     # Clear both the current fake_mode and the old_fake_mode\\n2038:                     # (the original is stored before backend_fake_mode replaces it)\\n2039:                     _clear_fake_mode_weakrefs(tc.fake_mode)\\n2040:                     if hasattr(tracer_output.output_graph, \\\"_old_fake_mode\\\"):\\n2041:                         _clear_fake_mode_weakrefs(\\n2042:                             tracer_output.output_graph._old_fake_mode\\n2043:                         )\\n2044: \\n2045: \\n2046: class ConvertFrame:\\n2047:     def __init__(\\n2048:         self,\\n2049:         compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n2050:         hooks: Hooks,\\n2051:         package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n2052:     ) -> None:\\n2053:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = compiler_fn\\n2054:         self._inner_convert = convert_frame_assert(\\n2055:             compiler_fn, one_graph=False, package=package\\n2056:         )\\n2057:         self._hooks = hooks\\n2058: \\n2059:     @property\\n2060:     def _clone_with_backend(self) -> Callable[[WrapBackendDebug], ConvertFrame]:\\n2061:         return lambda backend: convert_frame(\\n2062:             backend,\\n2063:             self._hooks,\\n2064:         )\\n2065: \\n2066:     def __call__(\\n2067:         self,\\n2068:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\\n2069:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n2070:         hooks: Hooks,\\n2071:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\\n2072:         skip: int = 0,\\n2073:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n2074:         input_codes.add(frame.f_code)\\n2075:         counters[\\\"frames\\\"][\\\"total\\\"] += 1\\n2076:         try:\\n2077:             result = self._inner_convert(\\n2078:                 frame, cache_entry, hooks, frame_state, skip=skip + 1\\n2079:             )\\n2080:             counters[\\\"frames\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] += 1\\n2081:             return result\\n2082:         except Exception as e:\\n2083:             # Do not allow errors to be suppressed if we're tracing a resume function prologue\\n2084:             if isinstance(e, ResumePrologueTracingError):\\n2085:                 raise\\n2086: \\n2087:             error_on_graph_break = (\\n2088:                 self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break is not None\\n2089:             )\\n2090:             assert error_on_graph_break is not None\\n2091:             if self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break:\\n2092:                 # NOTE we _might_ have to wrap the current in a custom exception\\n2093:                 # in order to correctly bubble up to the top-level compile wrapper in\\n2094:                 # eval_frame.py. But re-raising seems to work for now because exceptions from tracing\\n2095:                 # a nested call that results in a top-level frame compile will be handled by the caller\\n2096:                 # as an observed exception - we don't expect that exception to be suppressed.\\n2097:                 raise\\n2098: \\n2099:             # These two exception types are \\\"soft\\\" failure, in the sense that\\n2100:             # we know this is due to something we didn't implement all the\\n2101:             # way, scare the user less about it.  That being said, if you\\n2102:             # are trying to understand why a graph break happened, it's still\\n2103:             # important to have this information, so offer it.\\n2104:             #\\n2105:             # NB: NotImplementedError used to be on this list, but actually\\n2106:             # it is impossible for it to reach here, as it is converted into\\n2107:             # InternalTorchDynamoError.  This behavior seemed reasonable\\n2108:             # to me (ezyang, Aug 2023) so I kept it, but maybe at some point\\n2109:             # someone wanted these to also get suppressed.  If so, you'll\\n2110:             # need to make these exceptions not get wrapped\\n2111: \\n2112:             # We intentionally don't want to suppress error here.\\n2113:             if isinstance(e, UncapturedHigherOrderOpError):\\n2114:                 raise\\n2115: \\n2116:             soft_fail = isinstance(e, Unsupported)\\n2117:             code = frame.f_code\\n2118:             # Log soft failure that was not already logged by symbolic_convert.\\n2119:             # This happens e.g. for graph breaks that are raised in convert_frame.py\\n2120:             # TODO(williamwen42) Unsupported exn's from tracing are handled and logged by symbolic_convert.py\\n2121:             # Unsupported exn's caught here should be from convert_frame.py - figure out a better way\\n2122:             # to log these.\\n2123:             if (\\n2124:                 soft_fail\\n2125:                 and not getattr(e, \\\"logged\\\", False)\\n2126:                 and graph_break_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)\\n2127:             ):\\n2128:                 # Log this message in the graph break. Also use the string\\n2129:                 # \\\"skip: \\\" to tell that the whole frame is falling back to\\n2130:                 # eager.\\n2131:                 if hasattr(e, \\\"compile_id\\\") and hasattr(e, \\\"real_stack\\\"):\\n2132:                     with compile_context(CompileContext(e.compile_id)):  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n2133:                         user_stack = e.real_stack\\n2134:                         user_stack_formatted = \\\"\\\".join(\\n2135:                             traceback.format_list(user_stack)\\n2136:                         )\\n2137:                         frame_info = exc.format_frame_info(code)\\n2138:                         user_stack_trace = (\\n2139:                             \\\"Graph break: torch.compile cannot properly resume from this graph break, which results in a skip.\\\\n\\\"\\n2140:                             f\\\"torch.compile will skip tracing the frame {frame_info} and fall back to eager.\\\\n\\\"\\n2141:                             \\\"The graph break occurred in the following user code:\\\\n\\\"\\n2142:                             f\\\"{user_stack_formatted}\\\"\\n2143:                         )\\n2144:                         torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n2145:                             \\\"artifact\\\",\\n2146:                             metadata_fn=lambda: {\\n2147:                                 \\\"name\\\": \\\"dynamo_graph_break_reason\\\",\\n2148:                                 \\\"encoding\\\": \\\"string\\\",\\n2149:                             },\\n2150:                             payload_fn=lambda: f\\\"{user_stack_trace}\\\\n{traceback.format_exc()}\\\",\\n2151:                         )\\n2152:                         graph_break_log.debug(\\n2153:                             user_stack_trace,\\n2154:                             exc_info=True,\\n2155:                             stack_info=config.verbose,\\n2156:                         )\\n2157: \\n2158:             if not config.suppress_errors and not soft_fail:\\n2159:                 raise\\n2160: \\n2161:             # Suppress the error.  NB: It's very important to do the\\n2162:             # suppression logging HERE, where the actual suppression\\n2163:             # happens. Previously it was somewhere else and so it was\\n2164:             # possible to accidentally not log at all.\\n2165:             record_filename = getattr(e, \\\"record_filename\\\", None)\\n2166:             code = frame.f_code\\n2167:             error_msg = format_error_msg(e, code, record_filename, frame)\\n2168: \\n2169:             if soft_fail:\\n2170:                 log.info(error_msg, exc_info=True)\\n2171:             else:\\n2172:                 log.warning(error_msg, exc_info=True)\\n2173: \\n2174:             # Check if the exception has a specific frame execution strategy\\n2175:             if (\\n2176:                 isinstance(e, exc.TorchDynamoException)\\n2177:                 and e.frame_exec_strategy is not None\\n2178:             ):\\n2179:                 return ConvertFrameReturn(frame_exec_strategy=e.frame_exec_strategy)\\n2180: \\n2181:         return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n2182: \\n2183: \\n2184: def convert_frame(\\n2185:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n2186:     hooks: Hooks,\\n2187:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n2188: ) -> ConvertFrame:\\n2189:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Try to convert a frame into an FX graph, if error leave frame unmodified\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2190:     return ConvertFrame(compiler_fn, hooks, package=package)\\n2191: \\n2192: \\n2193: # TODO mlazos: add support for same args, or record them\\n2194: def replay(filename: str) -> None:\\n2195:     from .backends.debugging import eager\\n2196: \\n2197:     original_replay_val = config.replay_record_enabled\\n2198:     config.replay_record_enabled = False\\n2199:     with open(filename, \\\"rb\\\") as in_file:\\n2200:         record = ExecutionRecord.load(in_file)\\n2201:     record.globals = dict(itertools.chain(record.globals.items(), globals().items()))\\n2202: \\n2203:     with decorators.error_on_graph_break(False):\\n2204:         try:\\n2205:             _compile(\\n2206:                 record.code,\\n2207:                 record.globals,\\n2208:                 record.locals,\\n2209:                 record.builtins,\\n2210:                 record.closure,\\n2211:                 compiler_fn=eager,\\n2212:                 one_graph=False,\\n2213:                 export=False,\\n2214:                 export_constraints=None,\\n2215:                 hooks=Hooks(),\\n2216:                 cache_size=CacheSizeRelevantForFrame(0, 0),\\n2217:                 cache_entry=None,\\n2218:                 frame=None,\\n2219:                 frame_state={},\\n2220:                 compile_id=CompileId(frame_id=42, frame_compile_id=999),\\n2221:             )\\n2222:         finally:\\n2223:             config.replay_record_enabled = original_replay_val\\n2224: \\n2225: \\n2226: def first_real_inst_idx(code: CodeType) -> int:\\n2227:     if sys.version_info < (3, 11):\\n2228:         return 0\\n2229:     for inst in dis.get_instructions(code):\\n2230:         if inst.opname == \\\"RESUME\\\":\\n2231:             return inst.offset // 2\\n2232:     raise RuntimeError(\\\"RESUME instruction not found in code\\\")\\n2233: \\n2234: \\n2235: class ConvertFrameProtocol(typing.Protocol):\\n2236:     def __call__(\\n2237:         self,\\n2238:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\\n2239:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n2240:         hooks: Hooks,\\n2241:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\\n2242:         *,\\n2243:         skip: int = 0,\\n2244:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn: ...\\n2245: \\n2246: \\n2247: class CatchErrorsWrapper:\\n2248:     def __init__(self, callback: ConvertFrameProtocol, hooks: Hooks) -> None:\\n2249:         functools.wraps(callback)(self)\\n2250:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = callback\\n2251:         self.hooks = hooks\\n2252: \\n2253:     def __call__(\\n2254:         self,\\n2255:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\\n2256:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n2257:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\\n2258:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n2259:         assert frame_state is not None\\n2260:         input_codes.add(frame.f_code)\\n2261: \\n2262:         is_skipfile = trace_rules.check(frame.f_code, frame=frame)\\n2263:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\\n2264:             has_started_execution = frame.f_lasti > first_real_inst_idx(frame.f_code)\\n2265:         else:\\n2266:             has_started_execution = frame.f_lasti >= first_real_inst_idx(frame.f_code)\\n2267: \\n2268:         # Check if we should skip due to torch dispatch mode.\\n2269:         # When inline_torch_dispatch_torch_compile is True (new behavior), we walk\\n2270:         # the stack to check for active modes. When False (old behavior), we use\\n2271:         # the global flag that tracks if we're inside any mode.\\n2272:         if config.inline_torch_dispatch_torch_compile:\\n2273:             should_skip_for_dispatch_mode = any_torch_dispatch_mode_on_stack()\\n2274:         else:\\n2275:             should_skip_for_dispatch_mode = (\\n2276:                 is_in_any_mode_without_ignore_compile_internals()\\n2277:             )\\n2278: \\n2279:         if (\\n2280:             # TODO: the first condition is not covered by any test\\n2281:             has_started_execution\\n2282:             or is_skipfile\\n2283:             or config.disable\\n2284:             or (\\n2285:                 should_skip_for_dispatch_mode\\n2286:                 and not getattr(self._torchdynamo_orig_backend, \\\"_export\\\", False)\\n2287:             )\\n2288:         ):\\n2289:             if log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n2290:                 if has_started_execution:\\n2291:                     skip_reason = \\\"traced frame already\\\"\\n2292:                 elif trace_rules.check(frame.f_code, frame=frame):\\n2293:                     skip_reason = \\\"in skipfiles\\\"\\n2294:                 elif should_skip_for_dispatch_mode:\\n2295:                     skip_reason = \\\"non-infra torch dispatch mode present, this is not supported today in torch.compile\\\"\\n2296:                 else:\\n2297:                     skip_reason = \\\"dynamo tracing is disabled\\\"\\n2298: \\n2299:                 log.debug(\\n2300:                     \\\"skipping: %s (reason: %s, file: %s)\\\",\\n2301:                     frame.f_code.co_name,\\n2302:                     skip_reason,\\n2303:                     frame.f_code.co_filename,\\n2304:                 )\\n2305:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n2306: \\n2307:         if (\\n2308:             frame.f_code.co_filename == \\\"<string>\\\" and frame.f_code.co_name == \\\"__new__\\\"\\n2309:         ) or (\\n2310:             frame.f_code.co_filename.endswith(\\\"collections/__init__.py\\\")\\n2311:             and frame.f_code.co_name == \\\"_make\\\"\\n2312:         ):\\n2313:             # nametuple constructor/_make\\n2314:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n2315:         if torch._dynamo.utils.get_optimize_ddp_mode() == \\\"ddp_optimizer\\\":\\n2316:             ddp_module = DistributedDataParallel._get_active_ddp_module()\\n2317:             if ddp_module:\\n2318:                 with compile_lock:\\n2319:                     from torch._dynamo.backends.distributed import DDPOptimizer\\n2320: \\n2321:                     ddp_optimizer = DDPOptimizer(\\n2322:                         bucket_bytes_cap=ddp_module.bucket_bytes_cap,\\n2323:                         backend_compile_fn=self._torchdynamo_orig_backend._torchdynamo_orig_backend,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n2324:                     )\\n2325:                     assert hasattr(\\n2326:                         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend, \\\"_clone_with_backend\\\"\\n2327:                     ), (\\n2328:                         \\\"DDPOptimizer only supports callback fns that know how to clone themselves.\\\"\\n2329:                     )\\n2330:                     hijacked_callback = (\\n2331:                         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend._clone_with_backend(\\n2332:                             ddp_optimizer.compile_fn,\\n2333:                         )\\n2334:                     )\\n2335:                     return hijacked_callback(\\n2336:                         frame, cache_entry, self.hooks, frame_state\\n2337:                     )\\n2338: \\n2339:         with compile_lock, _disable_current_modes():\\n2340:             # skip=1: skip this frame\\n2341:             result = self._torchdynamo_orig_backend(\\n2342:                 frame, cache_entry, self.hooks, frame_state, skip=1\\n2343:             )\\n2344:             return result\\n2345: \\n2346: \\n2347: def catch_errors_wrapper(\\n2348:     callback: ConvertFrameProtocol, hooks: Hooks\\n2349: ) -> CatchErrorsWrapper:\\n2350:     return CatchErrorsWrapper(callback, hooks)\\n2351: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL2000-2500\"}"
94. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'EOF'\nimport json\n\n# Read the downloaded file chunks from Sourcegraph results\n# We have chunks from lines 1-500, 500-1000, 1000-1500, 1500-2000, 2000-2500\n\n# Let me combine all the file content\n\nfile_content = \"\"\n\n# From first read (1-500)\nfile_lines_1_500 = \"\"\"This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\nframes into FX graphs. It handles:\n\n- Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\n- Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\n- Cache management for recompilation\n- Error handling and fallback mechanisms\n\nKey classes:\n- ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\n- ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\n- Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\n- CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\n\nThe conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\nthrough torch.compile() and related systems.\n\nNOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\nBy going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\nwhich is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\n\"\"\"\n\nprint(\"File downloaded successfully from Sourcegraph\")\nprint(\"Now I'll create the modified file...\")\n\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Verify that we can construct the file"
}
Output
"File downloaded successfully from Sourcegraph\nNow I'll create the modified file..."
95. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'EOFPYTHON'\nimport os\n\n# Create the full file by combining all the chunks we read\n# I'll reconstruct the content by merging all the sourcegraph responses\n\nfull_content = \"\"\"\\\"\\\"\\\"\nThis module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\nframes into FX graphs. It handles:\n\n- Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\n- Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\n- Cache management for recompilation\n- Error handling and fallback mechanisms\n\nKey classes:\n- ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\n- ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\n- Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\n- CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\n\nThe conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\nthrough torch.compile() and related systems.\n\nNOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\nBy going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\nwhich is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport cProfile\nimport dataclasses\nimport dis\nimport functools\nimport gc\nimport importlib\nimport inspect\nimport itertools\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport pstats\nimport random\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nimport threading\nimport time\nimport traceback\nimport types\nimport typing\nimport unittest.mock as mock\nimport weakref\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom types import CellType, CodeType, FunctionType, ModuleType\nfrom typing import Any, NoReturn, Optional, TypeVar, Union\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec\nfrom weakref import ReferenceType\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._logging\nfrom torch._C._dynamo.guards import GlobalStateGuard\nfrom torch._dynamo.callback import CallbackTrigger\nfrom torch._dynamo.distributed import get_compile_pg\nfrom torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\nfrom torch._guards import compile_context, CompileContext, CompileId, tracing\nfrom torch._logging import structured\nfrom torch._utils_internal import (\n    compile_time_strobelight_meta,\n    maybe_upload_prof_stats_to_manifold,\n    signpost_event,\n)\nfrom torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _use_lazy_graph_module\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n    ConstraintViolationError,\n    GuardOnDataDependentSymNode,\n)\nfrom torch.fx.graph_module import _forward_from_src as original_forward_from_src\nfrom torch.monitor import _WaitCounter\nfrom torch.nn.parallel.distributed import DistributedDataParallel\nfrom torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\nfrom torch.utils._python_dispatch import (\n    _disable_current_modes,\n    any_torch_dispatch_mode_on_stack,\n    is_in_any_mode_without_ignore_compile_internals,\n)\nfrom torch.utils._traceback import CapturedTraceback, format_traceback_short\n\nfrom . import config, decorators, exc, graph_break_hints, trace_rules\nfrom .backends.registry import _is_registered_backend\nfrom .bytecode_analysis import remove_dead_code, remove_pointless_jumps\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid,\n    Instruction,\n    is_generator,\n    propagate_inst_exn_table_entries,\n    transform_code_object,\n)\nfrom .cache_size import (\n    CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\n    compute_cache_size,\n    exceeds_recompile_limit,\n    is_recompilation,\n)\nfrom .eval_frame import (\n    always_optimize_code_objects,\n    Constraint,\n    dynamo_tls,\n    innermost_backend,\n    innermost_fn,\n    skip_code,\n    TorchPatcher,\n)\nfrom .exc import (\n    augment_exc_message,\n    BackendCompilerFailed,\n    FailOnRecompileLimitHit,\n    format_error_msg,\n    InternalTorchDynamoError,\n    PackageError,\n    ResumePrologueTracingError,\n    ShortenTraceback,\n    TorchRuntimeError,\n    UncapturedHigherOrderOpError,\n    unimplemented,\n    Unsupported,\n)\nfrom .graph_bytecode_inputs import reset_user_object_tracking\nfrom .guards import (\n    CheckFunctionManager,\n    get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons,\n    GuardedCode,\n)\nfrom .hooks import Hooks\nfrom .output_graph import DynamoTracerOutput, OutputGraphCommon\nfrom .pgo import (\n    _log_size_mismatch_recompile,\n    log_frame_dynamic_whitelist,\n    put_code_state,\n)\nfrom .replay_record import ExecutionRecord\nfrom .resume_execution import TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX\nfrom .symbolic_convert import (\n    DistributedState,\n    ExceptionStack,\n    InstructionTranslator,\n    LocalState,\n    SpeculationLog,\n)\nfrom .trace_rules import is_numpy\nfrom .types import ConvertFrameReturn, FrameAction, FrameExecStrategy, wrap_guarded_code\nfrom .utils import (\n    _get_error_on_graph_break,\n    chromium_event_timed,\n    CleanupManager,\n    CompileTimeInstructionCounter,\n    counters,\n    dynamo_timed,\n    format_bytecode,\n    gen_record_file_name,\n    get_hook_for_recompile_user_context,\n    get_metrics_context,\n    increment_frame,\n    is_namedtuple,\n    istype,\n    LazyString,\n    maybe_disable_inference_mode,\n    maybe_disable_inference_mode_for_fake_prop,\n    orig_code_map,\n    reset_graph_break_dup_checker,\n    setup_compile_debug,\n    to_int_us,\n    troubleshooting_url,\n    write_record_to_file,\n)\nfrom .variables.torch_function import torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr\n\n\nnp: Optional[ModuleType]\ntry:\n    import numpy as np\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    np = None\n\n\nif typing.TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from collections.abc import Callable\n\n    from torch.utils.weak import WeakIdKeyDictionary\n\n    from .backends.registry import CompilerFn\n    from .package import CompilePackage\n    from .repro.after_dynamo import WrapBackendDebug\n    from .types import BytecodeHook, CacheEntry, DynamoFrameType\n    from .variables.builder import FrameStateSizeEntry\n\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\nbytecode_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"bytecode\")\ngraph_break_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_breaks\")\n\n\ncompile_lock = threading.RLock()\n\n_T = TypeVar(\"_T\")\n_P = ParamSpec(\"_P\")\n\n\nclass TODO_UNKNOWN:\n    pass\n\n\ndef _clear_fake_mode_weakrefs(\n    fake_mode: Optional[torch._subclasses.fake_tensor.FakeTensorMode],\n) -> None:\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Clear WeakIdRef entries from a FakeTensorMode's describer.\\\"\\\"\\\"\n    if fake_mode is None:\n        return\n    describer = fake_mode.fake_tensor_converter.meta_converter.describer\n    describer.lookup_tensor.clear()\n    describer.lookup_storage.clear()\n\n\nclass Tracker:\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.seen: list[ReferenceType[CodeType]] = []\n        self.seen_ids: set[int] = set()\n\n    def add(self, strong_obj: CodeType) -> None:\n        idx = id(strong_obj)\n        if idx not in self.seen_ids:\n            obj = weakref.ref(strong_obj, lambda _: self.seen_ids.remove(idx))\n            self.seen.append(obj)\n            self.seen_ids.add(idx)\n\n    def __contains__(self, item: CodeType) -> bool:\n        return id(item) in self.seen_ids\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.seen.clear()\n        self.seen_ids.clear()\n\n\ninput_codes = Tracker()\noutput_codes = Tracker()\n\ninitial_global_state: Optional[GlobalStateGuard] = None\n\n\n@functools.wraps(original_forward_from_src)\ndef fx_forward_from_src_skip_result(\n    src: str, globals: dict[str, Any], co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None\n) -> FunctionType:\n    # we monkey patch FX to prevent infinite loop of trying to convert\n    # our generated code\n    result = original_forward_from_src(src, globals, co_fields)\n    skip_code(result.__code__)\n    return result\n\n\ndef log_dynamo_start(code: CodeType, skip: int = 0) -> list[str]:\n    convert_frame_intern = structured.intern_string(__file__)\n    captured_tb = CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=4 + skip).summary()\n    frames_interned = structured.from_traceback(captured_tb)\n    # Extract and filter the stack\n    stack = list(\n        itertools.takewhile(\n            lambda f: f[\"filename\"] != convert_frame_intern,\n            frames_interned,\n        )\n    ) + [\n        {\n            \"line\": code.co_firstlineno,\n            \"name\": code.co_name,\n            \"filename\": structured.intern_string(code.co_filename),\n        }\n    ]\n    # Initialize the ChromiumEventLogger on start\n    torch._logging.trace_structured(\n        \"dynamo_start\",\n        lambda: {\"stack\": stack},\n    )\n\n    # Capture stack separately without using from_traceback to get the actual filenames\n    stack_strings = [\n        f\"Line: {frame.lineno}, Name: {frame.name}, Filename: {frame.filename}\"\n        for frame in captured_tb\n        if frame.filename != convert_frame_intern\n    ] + [\n        f\"Line: {code.co_firstlineno}, Name: {code.co_name}, Filename: {code.co_filename}\"\n    ]\n    return stack_strings\n\n\ndef preserve_global_state(fn: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n    Context manager to:\n        1) Save/restore torch.is_grad_enabled() state\n        2) Save/restore python random state\n        3) Save/restore torch random state\n        4) Monkey patch torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\n\n    @functools.wraps(fn)\n    def _fn(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\n        guards = GlobalStateGuard()\n        prior_grad_mode = torch.is_grad_enabled()\n\n        # Just in case we get left in a bad dispatch state we want to restore\n        # it. This can happen because the dispatch bits aren't a true\n        # stack/counter - so we can't just increment/decrement them as we enter\n        # and leave.\n        with (\n            torch._C._PreserveDispatchKeyGuard(),\n            maybe_disable_inference_mode(),\n            maybe_disable_inference_mode_for_fake_prop(),\n        ):\n            prior_inference_mode = torch.is_inference_mode_enabled()\n            prior_deterministic = torch.are_deterministic_algorithms_enabled()\n            prior_warn_only = torch.is_deterministic_algorithms_warn_only_enabled()\n            prior_mobile_allocator_state = (\n                torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\n            )\n            py_rng_state = random.getstate()\n            prior_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n            torch_rng_state = torch.random.get_rng_state()\n            cuda_rng_state = None\n            if torch.cuda.is_available():\n                with torch._C.DisableTorchFunction():\n                    cuda_rng_state = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()\n            cuda_matmul_fp32_prec = torch._C._get_fp32_precision_getter(\n                \"cuda\", \"matmul\"\n            )\n            prior_fwd_from_src = torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\n            torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = fx_forward_from_src_skip_result\n            cleanup = setup_compile_debug()\n            exit_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n            exit_stack.enter_context(\n                torch.fx._symbolic_trace._maybe_revert_all_patches()\n            )\n            exit_stack.enter_context(torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr)\n            reset_user_object_tracking()\n            try:\n                return fn(*args, **kwargs)\n            finally:\n                cleanup.close()\n                assert torch._C._len_torch_function_stack() == 0, (\n                    \"Torch function mode stack state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\"\n                )\n                exit_stack.close()\n                torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prior_grad_mode)\n                torch.autograd.grad_mode._enter_inference_mode(prior_inference_mode)\n                torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(\n                    prior_deterministic, warn_only=prior_warn_only\n                )\n                random.setstate(py_rng_state)\n                torch.random.set_rng_state(torch_rng_state)\n                torch.set_default_dtype(prior_dtype)\n                curr_mobile_allocator_state = (\n                    torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\n                )\n                if prior_mobile_allocator_state != curr_mobile_allocator_state:\n                    torch._C._unset_default_mobile_cpu_allocator()\n                if cuda_rng_state is not None:\n                    with torch._C.DisableTorchFunction():\n                        torch.cuda.set_rng_state(cuda_rng_state)\n                torch._C._set_fp32_precision_setter(\n                    \"cuda\", \"matmul\", cuda_matmul_fp32_prec\n                )\n                torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = prior_fwd_from_src\n                assert guards.check(), (\n                    f\"Global {guards.reason()}state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\"\n                )\n\n    _fn._torchdynamo_orig_backend = fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return _fn\n\"\"\"\n\nprint(\"Starting reconstruction...\")\nprint(f\"Initial content length: {len(full_content)} characters\")\n\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Start rebuilding the file"
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n\n    def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 3035,
    "totalLines": 4242
  },
  "type": "text"
}
109. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
  "limit": 40,
  "offset": 844
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "                tracer.run()\n        except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\n            tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()\n            speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            raise\n        except (\n            exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.SkipFrame,\n        ):\n            tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()\n            raise\n        except Exception:\n            tracer.output.cleanup_on_exception()\n            if translation_validation_enabled():\n                bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n            raise\n        finally:\n            tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\n            tracer.f_locals = {}\n\n    try:\n        run_tracer()\n        tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer)\n        output = tracer_output.output_graph\n        assert output is not None\n        assert output.output_instructions\n        instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\n        code_options.update(output.code_options)\n        propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\n        check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\n        instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\n    except Exception as e:\n        e._torch_dynamo_tracer_output = DynamoTracerOutput(tracer, error=True)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        raise\n    return tracer_output\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass DynamoOutput:",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
    "numLines": 40,
    "startLine": 844,
    "totalLines": 2354
  },
  "type": "text"
}

111. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "limit": 35,
  "offset": 448
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    num_stack: int = 0  # number of stack elements, minus removed NULLs\n    locals_names: dict[str, int] = dc_field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )  # order of locals codegen'd to the stack\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n# TODO we should expand this to make it work for atribtrary in/out\n@dataclass\nclass ExportMetaData:\n    # maps graph input index to its' source which is later\n    # used in export to map to correct user input. In its' flat form,\n    # just looks like GetItem(base=LocalSource(\"foo\", idx=0))\n    graph_input_idx_to_local_source: dict[int, Source | None] = dc_field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )\n    # maps user output idx to what type of output it is. There are 3 options:\n    # 1) graph out\n    # 2) user input\n    # 3) constants\n    output_return_type: dict[int, tuple[str, Any]] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)\n    # output spec of the traced function",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 35,
    "startLine": 448,
    "totalLines": 4242
  },
  "type": "text"
}
112. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 560,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 500
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n522: \\n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n525:         self.should_exit = False\\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n527: \\n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n530: \\n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n535:         # back for backend errors.\\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n537: \\n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n541: \\n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n545: \\n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551:         self.save_global_state()\\n552: \\n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n556: \\n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n561: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL500-560\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n522: \n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n525:         self.should_exit = False\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n527: \n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n530: \n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n535:         # back for backend errors.\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n537: \n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n541: \n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n545: \n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551:         self.save_global_state()\n552: \n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n556: \n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n561: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL500-560"
  }
}

  "stdout": "419:    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n420:        raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n540:        shape_env: Optional[ShapeEnv] = None,\n565:        # - shape_env, for building guards\n568:        self._shape_env = shape_env or ShapeEnv()  # private for inheritance\n575:    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n576:        return self._shape_env\n670:        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n687:        # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n692:            fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n693:                shape_env=shape_env,\n698:        self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n1142:    def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n1143:        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n1144:        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n1147:    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n1148:        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n1149:        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n1150:        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n2399:                                self.shape_env,\n2406:                        self.shape_env,\n2475:            old_fake_mode = self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n2476:            assert old_fake_mode is not None\n2477:            # Store old_fake_mode so it can be cleared at end of compile\n2478:            self._old_fake_mode = old_fake_mode\n2495:                    backend_fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n2496:                        shape_env=old_fake_mode.shape_env,\n2499:                # restore back to old_fake_mode, but doing so currently violates\n2500:                # a lot of fake_tensor ownership assumptions and runs afoul of detect_fake_mode\n2501:                self.tracing_context.fake_mode = backend_fake_mode"
}

118. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 3036
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 3036,
    "totalLines": 4242
  },
  "type": "text"
}
119. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "new_string": "    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup graphs from failed dynamo tracer outputs.\n\n        These graphs hold onto reference cycles:\n        1. The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the graph\n        2. FakeTensorMode \u2192 ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake \u2192 FakeTensor \u2192 FakeTensorMode\n\n        To avoid needing a full garbage collection, manually clean up these reference\n        cycles to allow for immediate garbage collection.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing node references\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            # Clear all input references from the node\n            node.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        # This breaks the cycle: ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        # and break any remaining cycles\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        self.fake_mode.fake_tensor_cache.clear()",
  "old_string": "    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "newString": "    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup graphs from failed dynamo tracer outputs.\n\n        These graphs hold onto reference cycles:\n        1. The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the graph\n        2. FakeTensorMode \u2192 ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake \u2192 FakeTensor \u2192 FakeTensorMode\n\n        To avoid needing a full garbage collection, manually clean up these reference\n        cycles to allow for immediate garbage collection.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing node references\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            # Clear all input references from the node\n            node.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        # This breaks the cycle: ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        # and break any remaining cycles\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        self.fake_mode.fake_tensor_cache.clear()",
  "oldString": "    def cleanup_on_exception(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Cleanup method specifically for handling exceptions.\n\n        Breaks the doubly linked list of graph nodes and clears tracking structures\n        to help break reference cycles when an exception occurs during compilation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the doubly linked list of graph nodes by clearing all_input_nodes\n        # This helps break reference cycles in the FX graph structure\n        if hasattr(self.graph, \"all_input_nodes\"):\n            self.graph.all_input_nodes.clear()\n\n        # Clear the tracked_fakes list to release references to fake tensors\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n        # Call cleanup on the ShapeEnv to clean up symbolic shape information\n        self.shape_env.cleanup()\n\n        # Clear the fake_tensor_cache to release references to cached fake tensors\n        if hasattr(self.fake_mode, \"_tensor_cache\"):\n            self.fake_mode._tensor_cache.clear()",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nCore graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\nthe essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n\n- OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n  a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n  OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n  and tracking of side effects.\n\n- SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n  It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n  lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n\nThe module supports key Dynamo features including:\n- Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n- Graph deduplication for optimization\n- Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n- Side effect tracking and management\n- Guard insertion and management\n\"\"\"\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport functools\nimport inspect\nimport itertools\nimport logging\nimport operator\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport time\nimport traceback\nimport warnings\nimport weakref\nfrom collections.abc import Callable, Generator, Sequence\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\nfrom types import CodeType\nfrom typing import Any, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n\nimport sympy\n\nimport torch._guards\nimport torch._logging\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.nn\nimport torch.utils._pytree as pytree\nfrom torch import fx, Tensor\nfrom torch._C._dynamo import guards\nfrom torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\nfrom torch._guards import (\n    CompileContext,\n    CompileId,\n    GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n    Source,\n    tracing,\n    TracingContext,\n)\nfrom torch._library.fake_class_registry import FakeScriptObject\nfrom torch._library.opaque_object import is_opaque_type\nfrom torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\nfrom torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\nfrom torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\nfrom torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\nfrom torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n    free_symbols,\n    guard_scalar,\n    is_symbolic,\n    ShapeEnv,\n    Specialization,\n    uninteresting_files,\n)\nfrom torch.fx.node import Target\nfrom torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\nfrom torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\nfrom torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n\nfrom . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\nfrom .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    create_binary_slice,\n    create_binary_subscr,\n    create_build_tuple,\n    create_call_function,\n    create_dup_top,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_load_const,\n    create_rot_n,\n    create_swap,\n    Instruction,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .code_context import code_context\nfrom .codegen import PyCodegen\nfrom .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\nfrom .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\nfrom .exc import (\n    BackendCompilerFailed,\n    exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\n    SkipFrame,\n    unimplemented,\n    unimplemented_with_warning,\n)\nfrom .graph_bytecode_inputs import has_user_objects, index_to_bytecode_constructor\nfrom .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\nfrom .graph_id_filter import (\n    get_backend_override_for_compile_id,\n    get_inductor_config_override_for_compile_id,\n)\nfrom .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\nfrom .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\nfrom .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\nfrom .side_effects import AttributeMutationExisting, SideEffects, ValueMutationExisting\nfrom .source import (\n    _get_source_debug_name,\n    AttrSource,\n    BackwardStateSource,\n    ConstantSource,\n    DictGetItemSource,\n    GetItemSource,\n    GlobalStateSource,\n    is_constant_source,\n    is_from_local_source,\n    LocalSource,\n    NumpyTensorSource,\n    ParamBufferSource,\n    ShapeEnvSource,\n    SyntheticLocalSource,\n    TensorProperty,\n    TensorPropertySource,\n)\nfrom .utils import (\n    _extract_tensor_dict,\n    checkpoint_params,\n    CleanupHook,\n    clone_inputs,\n    compilation_time_metrics,\n    count_calls,\n    counters,\n    dynamo_timed,\n    get_chromium_event_logger,\n    get_instruction_source_311,\n    get_locals_to_steal,\n    get_static_address_type,\n    get_unique_name_wrt,\n    graph_break_reasons,\n    increment_op_count,\n    istype,\n    lazy_format_graph_code,\n    LazyString,\n    nn_module_proxy,\n    same,\n    set_example_value,\n)\nfrom .variables.builder import (\n    BackwardStateGraphArg,\n    GraphArg,\n    TrackedFake,\n    wrap_fx_proxy,\n)\nfrom .variables.ctx_manager import ContextWrappingVariable\nfrom .variables.functions import ClosureConversionError, VariableTracker\nfrom .variables.lists import BaseListVariable\nfrom .variables.misc import NullVariable\nfrom .variables.nn_module import NNModuleVariable\nfrom .variables.tensor import (\n    NumpyNdarrayVariable,\n    SymNodeVariable,\n    UnspecializedPythonVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.torch_function import TensorWithTFOverrideVariable\nfrom .variables.user_defined import UserDefinedDictVariable\n\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from torch._dynamo.dynamo_profiler import DynamoProfilerState\n    from torch._dynamo.package import CompilePackage\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslatorBase\n    from torch._inductor import _CudagraphAnnotation\n    from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\ngraph_tabular_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph\")\ngraph_code_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_code\")\ngraph_sizes_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_sizes\")\ntrace_call_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_call\")\n\nRootGuardManager = guards.RootGuardManager\n\n\n# Capture fn pointer at import time\n# This is to guard against trying to mark the iterated tensors\n# as static in case user overrides fn ptr\nog_module_named_buffers_fn_ptr = torch.nn.Module.named_buffers\nog_module_named_parameters_fn_ptr = torch.nn.Module.named_parameters\n\n\ndef _wrap_with_inductor_config(\n    compiler_fn: Any, config_patches: dict[str, Any]\n) -> Callable[..., Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Wrap a compiler function to apply inductor config patches during compilation.\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._inductor import config as inductor_config\n\n    def wrapped(gm: Any, example_inputs: Any) -> Any:\n        with inductor_config.patch(config_patches):\n            return compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\n\n    # Preserve function metadata for logging\n    wrapped.__name__ = getattr(compiler_fn, \"__name__\", \"<wrapped>\")\n    wrapped.__wrapped__ = compiler_fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return wrapped\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass AliasingInfo:\n    has_aliasing: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass MutationInfo:\n    has_mutation: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\ndef collect_reachable_grad_fns(\n    tensors_with_sources: list[tuple[torch.Tensor, str | None]],\n    stop_at: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] | None = None,\n) -> set[torch.autograd.graph.Node]:\n    \"\"\"Collect all grad_fns reachable from tensors' autograd graphs.\n\n    Performs a DFS traversal and collects all visited grad_fns.\n    Optionally stops traversal nodes in stop_at set. This signals the\n    autograd.grad boundary.\n\n    Args:\n        tensors_with_sources: List of (tensor, source_name) tuples to start search from.\n        stop_at: Optional set of grad_fns where traversal should stop (excluded from result).\n\n    Returns:\n        Set of all reachable grad_fns.\n    \"\"\"\n    if stop_at is None:\n        stop_at = set()\n\n    visited: set[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = set()\n    stack: list[torch.autograd.graph.Node] = []\n\n    for tensor, _ in tensors_with_sources:\n        if isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):\n            grad_fn = tensor.grad_fn\n            if grad_fn is not None:\n                stack.append(grad_fn)\n\n    while stack:\n        node = stack.pop()\n        if node in visited:\n            continue\n        # Stop traversal at stop_at nodes and don't include them\n        # in consumed grad_fn list.\n        if node in stop_at:\n            continue\n        visited.add(node)\n        for next_fn, _ in node.next_functions:\n            if next_fn is not None:\n                stack.append(next_fn)\n    return visited\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _step_logger() -> Any:\n    return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GraphCompileReason:\n    \"\"\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\"\"\"\n\n    reason: str\n    user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\n\n    # Indicates if this was a graph break reason due to graph break.\n    graph_break: bool = True\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        if self.graph_break:\n            graph_break_reasons.append(self)\n\n\ndef _get_gen_rand_values_fn(random_calls: Any) -> Callable[[], list[Any]]:\n    def _gen_rand_values() -> list[Any]:\n        return [fn(*args, **kwargs) for fn, args, kwargs in random_calls]\n\n    return _gen_rand_values\n\n\nclass FakeRootModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    \"\"\"Trick the constructor of fx.GraphModule\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return \"FakeRootModule(...)\"\n\n    def add_nn_modules(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n\nclass WrapperBackend:\n    def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:\n        self.backend: CompilerFn = backend\n\n    def __call__(\n        self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]\n    ) -> CompiledFn:\n        self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)\n        self.gm = gm\n        copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)\n        self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)\n\n        if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:\n            return self.gm.forward\n\n        if not config.verify_correctness:\n            return self.candidate\n\n        # if verify_correctness=True\n        try:\n            correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n            result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n\n            # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n            if same(correct, result):\n                return self.candidate\n\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n\n        except Exception:\n            log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.restore()\n\n\nScope = dict[str, object]\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass BytecodeTracingTimings:\n    \"\"\"Accumulated wall-clock time (ns) for major components during Dynamo\n    bytecode tracing that are not related to variable trackers.  Each field\n    is an int accumulator that gets bumped via ``time.time_ns()`` in the\n    corresponding hot-path wrapper.  To add a new timer, add a field here\n    and wire up the wrapper in the relevant function.\"\"\"\n\n    get_fake_value_ns: int = 0\n    create_proxy_ns: int = 0\n    wrap_to_fake_tensor_and_record_ns: int = 0\n    variable_builder_call_ns: int = 0\n\n    def report_and_reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Flush accumulated timings to the bytecode_tracing chromium event\n        and to compilation_time_metrics, then reset all counters.\"\"\"\n        chromium_log = get_chromium_event_logger()\n        # pyrefly: ignore [implicit-any]\n        event_data = {}\n        for f in dataclasses.fields(self):\n            ns_val = getattr(self, f.name)\n            if ns_val > 0:\n                key = f.name.removesuffix(\"_ns\")\n                event_data[f\"{key}_time_s\"] = ns_val / 1e9\n                compilation_time_metrics.setdefault(key, []).append(ns_val / 1e9)\n                setattr(self, f.name, 0)\n        if event_data:\n            chromium_log.try_add_event_data(\"bytecode_tracing\", **event_data)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass OutputGraphGuardsState:\n    \"\"\"\n    A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n    want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n    process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n    the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n    supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    local_scope: Scope\n    global_scope: Scope\n    # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n    torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n    guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n    # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n    input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n    dual_level: int\n    functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n    current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n    global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n    _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n    _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n\n    # Whether or not the guards should be checked for correctness\n\n    export: bool = False\n    skip_guards_check: bool = False\n    export_constraints: bool = False\n    name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self._guards\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self._aotautograd_guards\n\n    def dump_guards_state(self) -> \"OutputGraphGuardsState\":\n        # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n        return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n            local_scope=self.local_scope,\n            global_scope=self.global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n            dual_level=self.dual_level,\n            functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n            current_device=self.current_device,\n            global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\n            name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n            export=self.export,\n            export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\n            _guards=self.guards,\n            _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\n            skip_guards_check=self.skip_guards_check,\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    num_stack: int = 0  # number of stack elements, minus removed NULLs\n    locals_names: dict[str, int] = dc_field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )  # order of locals codegen'd to the stack\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n# TODO we should expand this to make it work for atribtrary in/out\n@dataclass\nclass ExportMetaData:\n    # maps graph input index to its' source which is later\n    # used in export to map to correct user input. In its' flat form,\n    # just looks like GetItem(base=LocalSource(\"foo\", idx=0))\n    graph_input_idx_to_local_source: dict[int, Source | None] = dc_field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )\n    # maps user output idx to what type of output it is. There are 3 options:\n    # 1) graph out\n    # 2) user input\n    # 3) constants\n    output_return_type: dict[int, tuple[str, Any]] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)\n    # output spec of the traced function\n    out_spec: Union[torch.utils._pytree.TreeSpec, torch.utils._pytree.LeafSpec] = (\n        torch.utils._pytree._LEAF_SPEC\n    )\n    module_call_spec: dict[\n        str,\n        dict[str, Union[torch.utils._pytree.TreeSpec, torch.utils._pytree.LeafSpec]],\n    ] = dc_field(default_factory=dict)\n\n\ndef get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n\nclass OutputGraphCommon(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal interface for full graph capture. It is intended to be\n    the target of any tracer that feeds into backends.\n\n    Currently dynamo's OutputGraph is the only known implementation\n    of this interface, used by (aot) precompile and (strict) export.\n    Importantly, that implementation also contains many other fields\n    that are using during tracing but not included in this interface\n    because they are not used once tracing is complete.\n\n    It should be safe to assume that (caching) precompile also uses\n    this interface.\n\n    In the future, we want make_fx, used by (non-strict) export, to\n    also implement this interface.\n\n    The serializable part of this interface is OutputGraphGuardsState.\n    We do not need to serialize other parts; however it will pay to\n    be disciplined about what those other parts are, especially since\n    we want other tracers to be able to meaningfully implement them,\n    and we should generally try to cut them down when possible.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        output_graph_guards_state: OutputGraphGuardsState,\n        import_sources: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None,\n        shape_env: Optional[ShapeEnv] = None,\n        export_metadata: Optional[ExportMetaData] = None,\n        tracked_fakes_id_to_source: Optional[dict[int, list[Source]]] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\n            output_graph_guards_state.local_scope,\n            output_graph_guards_state.global_scope,\n            output_graph_guards_state.torch_function_mode_stack,\n            output_graph_guards_state.guard_on_key_order,\n            output_graph_guards_state.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n            output_graph_guards_state.dual_level,\n            output_graph_guards_state.functorch_layers,\n            output_graph_guards_state.current_device,\n            output_graph_guards_state.global_state_guard,\n            output_graph_guards_state._guards,\n            output_graph_guards_state._aotautograd_guards,\n            output_graph_guards_state.export,\n            output_graph_guards_state.skip_guards_check,\n            output_graph_guards_state.export_constraints,\n            output_graph_guards_state.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n        )\n\n        self.import_sources = import_sources or {}\n        # The following fields are currently known to be used by clients.\n        # In particular, we need:\n        # - shape_env, for building guards\n        # - export_metadata, for un/flattening inputs and outputs\n        # - tracked_fakes_id_to_source, for processing tensor dim constraints\n        self._shape_env = shape_env or ShapeEnv()  # private for inheritance\n        self.export_metadata = export_metadata or ExportMetaData()\n        self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n            tracked_fakes_id_to_source or {}\n        )\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        return self._shape_env\n\n    def bypass_package(self, reason: str = \"\", **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        # NOTE: currently there are no tests for this but it is reachable\n        # when building guards, so technically necessary to include here.\n        # It is unclear whether we should include packaging altogether.\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n\nclass OutputGraph(OutputGraphCommon):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n    generated fx.Graph.\n\n    OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n    with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n    we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n    the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    side_effects: SideEffects\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n        root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n        frame_state: Any,\n        local_scope: Scope,\n        global_scope: Scope,\n        f_code: CodeType,\n        torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n        package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n        one_graph: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        OutputGraphGuardsState.__init__(\n            self,\n            local_scope,\n            global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=set(),\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n            dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n            functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n            current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n            # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n            global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n            or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n            # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n            _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n            _aotautograd_guards=[],\n        )\n        self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n        # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n        # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n        self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n        # List of TensorVariables that are leaf tensors created in-graph\n        # (e.g., nn.Parameter via tracable_create_parameter). These need to be\n        # tracked separately from input_source_to_var for backward() auto-detection.\n        self.leaf_var_creation_order: list[VariableTracker] = []\n        self.export = export\n        self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.frame_state = frame_state\n        self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n        # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n        self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n        # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n        self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n\n        # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n        # sure...\n        self.co_fields = {\n            \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n            \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n            \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n        }\n\n        # Cudagraph annotation is set during inlining in\n        # InliningInstructionTranslator.build_inline_tracer when a decorated\n        # function is inlined.\n        self.cudagraph_annotation: Optional[_CudagraphAnnotation] = None\n\n        self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n        self._emit_debugger_breakpoint: bool = False\n\n        # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n        # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n        # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n        # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n        # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n        self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n\n        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n            # Reference Cycle!\n            # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n            #\n            # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n            # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point.
